verses.sanskrit: देहो ऽहम् इत्य् एव जडस्य बुद्धिः देहे च जीवे विदुषस्त्वहंधीः विवेकविज्ञानवतो महात्मनो ब्रह्माहम् इत्य् एव मतिः सदात्मनि
verses.transliteration: deho 'ham ity eva jaḍasya buddhiḥ dehe ca jīve viduṣastvahaṃdhīḥ & vivekavijñānavato mahātmano brahmāham ity eva matiḥ sadātmani
verses.translation: The stupid man thinks he is the body, the book-learned man identifies himself with the mixture of body and soul, while the sage possessed of realisation due to discrimination looks upon the eternal Atman as his Self, and thinks, "I am Brahman".

verses.sanskrit: अत्रात्मबुद्धिं त्यज मूढबुद्धे त्वङ्मांसमेदो ऽस्थिपुरीषराशौ सर्वात्मनि ब्रह्मणि निर्विकल्पे कुरुष्व शान्ति परमां भजस्व
verses.transliteration: atrātmabuddhiṃ tyaja mūḍhabuddhe tvaṅmāṃsamedo 'sthipurīṣarāśau & sarvātmani brahmaṇi nirvikalpe kuruṣva śānti paramāṃ bhajasva
verses.translation: O foolish person, cease to identify thyself with this bundle of skin, flesh, fat, bones and filth, and identify thyself instead with the Absolute Brahman, the Self of all, and thus attain to supreme Peace.

verses.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियादावसति भ्रमोदितां विद्वानहं तां न जहाति यावत् तावन् न तस्यास्ति विमुक्तिवार्ताप्य् अस्त्वेष वेदान्तनयान्तदर्शी
verses.transliteration: dehendriyādāvasati bhramoditāṃ vidvānahaṃ tāṃ na jahāti yāvat & tāvan na tasyāsti vimuktivārtāpy astveṣa vedāntanayāntadarśī
verses.translation: As long as the book-learned man does not give up his mistaken identification with the body, organs, etc., which are unreal, there is no talk of emancipation for him, even if he be ever so erudite in the Vedanta philosophy.

verses.sanskrit: छायाशरीरे प्रतिबिम्बगात्रे यत्स्वप्नदेहे हृदि कल्पिताङ्गे यथात्मबुद्धिस्तव नास्ति काचिज् जीवच्छरीरे च तथैव मास्तु
verses.transliteration: chāyāśarīre pratibimbagātre yatsvapnadehe hṛdi kalpitāṅge & yathātmabuddhistava nāsti kācij jīvaccharīre ca tathaiva māstu
verses.translation: Just as thou dost not identify thyself with the shadow-body, the image-body, the dream-body, or the body thou hast in the imaginations of thy heart, cease thou to do likewise with the living body also.

verses.sanskrit: देहात्मधीर् एव नृणामसद्धियां जन्मादिदुःखप्रभवस्य बीजम् यतस्ततस्त्वं जहि तां प्रयत्नात् त्यक्ते तु चित्ते न पुनर्भवाशा
verses.transliteration: dehātmadhīr eva nṛṇāmasaddhiyāṃ janmādiduḥkhaprabhavasya bījam & yatastatastvaṃ jahi tāṃ prayatnāt tyakte tu citte na punarbhavāśā
verses.translation: Identifications with the body alone is the root that produces the misery of birth etc., of people who are attached to the unreal; therefore destroy thou this with the utmost care. When this identification caused by the mind is given up, there is no more chance for rebirth.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियैः पञ्चभिरञ्चितो ऽयं प्राणो भवेत्प्राणमयस्तु कोशः येनात्मवानन्नमयो ऽनुपूर्णः प्रवर्तते ऽसौ सकलक्रियासु
verses.transliteration: karmendriyaiḥ pañcabhirañcito 'yaṃ prāṇo bhavetprāṇamayastu kośaḥ & yenātmavānannamayo 'nupūrṇaḥ pravartate 'sau sakalakriyāsu
verses.translation: The Prana, with which we are all familiar, coupled with the five organs of action, forms the vital sheath, permeated by which the material sheath engages itself in all activities as if it were living.

verses.sanskrit: नैवात्मापि प्राणमयो वायुविकारो गन्तागन्ता वायुवदन्तर्बहिरेषः यस्मात् किंचित् क्वापि न वेत्तीष्टमनिष्टं स्वं वान्यं वा किंचन नित्यं परतन्त्रः
verses.transliteration: naivātmāpi prāṇamayo vāyuvikāro gantāgantā vāyuvadantarbahireṣaḥ & yasmāt kiṃcit kvāpi na vettīṣṭamaniṣṭaṃ svaṃ vānyaṃ vā kiṃcana nityaṃ paratantraḥ
verses.translation: Neither is the vital sheath the Self because it is a modification of Vayu, and like the air it enters into and comes out of the body, and because it never knows in the least either its own weal and woe or those of others, being eternally dependent on the Self.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानेन्द्रियाणि च मनश्च मनोमयः स्यात् कोशो ममाहमिति वस्तुविकल्पहेतुः संज्ञादिभेदकलनाकलितो बलीयांस् तत्पूर्वकोशमभिपूर्य विजृम्भते यः
verses.transliteration: jñānendriyāṇi ca manaśca manomayaḥ syāt kośo mamāhamiti vastuvikalpahetuḥ & saṃjñādibhedakalanākalito balīyāṃs tatpūrvakośamabhipūrya vijṛmbhate yaḥ
verses.translation: The organs of knowledge together with the mind form the mental sheath the cause of the diversity of things such as "I" and "mine". It is powerful and endued with the faculty of creating differences of name etc., It manifests itself as permeating the preceding, i.e. the vital sheath.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चेन्द्रियैः पञ्चभिर् एव होतृभिः प्रचीयमानो विषयाज्यधारया जाज्वल्यमानो बहुवासनेन्धनैः मनोमयाग्निर्दहति प्रपञ्चम्
verses.transliteration: pañcendriyaiḥ pañcabhir eva hotṛbhiḥ pracīyamāno viṣayājyadhārayā & jājvalyamāno bahuvāsanendhanaiḥ manomayāgnirdahati prapañcam
verses.translation: The mental sheath is the (sacrificial) fire which, fed with the fuel of numerous desires by the five sense-organs which serve as priests, and set ablaze by the sense-objects which act as the stream of oblations, brings about this phenomenal universe.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यस्त्य् अविद्या मनसो ऽतिरिक्ता मनो ह्यविद्या भवबन्धहेतुः तस्मिन्विनष्टे सकलं विनष्टं विजृम्भिते ऽस्मिन्सकलं विजृम्भते
verses.transliteration: na hyasty avidyā manaso 'tiriktā mano hyavidyā bhavabandhahetuḥ & tasminvinaṣṭe sakalaṃ vinaṣṭaṃ vijṛmbhite 'sminsakalaṃ vijṛmbhate
verses.translation: There is no Ignorance (Avidya) outside the mind. The mind alone is Avidya, the cause of the bondage of transmigration. When that is destroyed, all else is destroyed, and when it is manifested, everything else is manifested.

verses.sanskrit: स्वप्ने ऽर्थशून्ये सृजति स्वशक्त्या भोक्त्रादिविश्वं मन एव सर्वम् तथैव जाग्रत्य् अपि नो विशेषः तत्सर्वमेतन्मनसो विजृम्भणम्
verses.transliteration: svapne 'rthaśūnye sṛjati svaśaktyā bhoktrādiviśvaṃ mana eva sarvam & tathaiva jāgraty api no viśeṣaḥ tatsarvametanmanaso vijṛmbhaṇam
verses.translation: In dreams, when there is no actual contact with the external world, the mind alone creates the whole universe consisting of the experiencer etc. Similarly in the waking state also; there is no difference. Therefore all this (phenomenal universe) is the projection of the mind.

verses.sanskrit: सुषुप्तिकाले मनसि प्रलीने नैवास्ति किंचित् सकलप्रसिद्धेः अतो मनःकल्पित् एव पुंसः संसार एतस्य न वस्तुतो ऽस्ति
verses.transliteration: suṣuptikāle manasi pralīne naivāsti kiṃcit sakalaprasiddheḥ & ato manaḥkalpit eva puṃsaḥ saṃsāra etasya na vastuto 'sti
verses.translation: In dreamless sleep, when the mind is reduced to its causal state, there exists nothing (for the person asleep), as is evident from universal experience. Hence mans relative existence is simply the creation of his mind, and has no objective reality.

verses.sanskrit: वायुनानीयते मेधः पुनस्तेनैव नीयते मनसा कल्प्यते बन्धो मोक्षस्तेनैव कल्प्यते
verses.transliteration: vāyunānīyate medhaḥ punastenaiva nīyate & manasā kalpyate bandho mokṣastenaiva kalpyate
verses.translation: Clouds are brought in by the wind and again driven away by the same agency. Similarly, mans bondage is caused by the mind, and Liberation too is caused by that alone.

verses.sanskrit: देहादिसर्वविषये परिकल्प्य रागं बध्नाति तेन पुरुषं पशुवद्गुणेन वैरस्यमत्र विषवत् सुवुधाय पश्चाद् एनं विमोचयति तन्मन एव बन्धात्
verses.transliteration: dehādisarvaviṣaye parikalpya rāgaṃ badhnāti tena puruṣaṃ paśuvadguṇena & vairasyamatra viṣavat suvudhāya paścād enaṃ vimocayati tanmana eva bandhāt
verses.translation: It (first) creates an attachment in man for the body and all other sense-objects, and binds him through that attachment like a beast by means of ropes. Afterwards, the selfsame mind creates in the individual an utter distaste for these sense-objects as if they were poison, and frees him from the bondage.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान्मनः कारणमस्य जन्तोः बन्धस्य मोक्षस्य च वा विधाने बन्धस्य हेतुर्मलिनं रजोगुणैः मोक्षस्य शुद्धं विरजस्तमस्कम्
verses.transliteration: tasmānmanaḥ kāraṇamasya jantoḥ bandhasya mokṣasya ca vā vidhāne & bandhasya heturmalinaṃ rajoguṇaiḥ mokṣasya śuddhaṃ virajastamaskam
verses.translation: Therefore the mind is the only cause that brings about mans bondage or Liberation: when tainted by the effects of Rajas it leads to bondage, and when pure and divested of the Rajas and Tamas elements it conduces to Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: विवेकवैराग्यगुणातिरेकाच् छुद्धत्वमासाद्य मनो विमुक्त्यै भवत्यतो बुद्धिमतो मुमुक्षोस् ताभ्यां दृढाभ्यां भवितव्यमग्रे
verses.transliteration: vivekavairāgyaguṇātirekāc chuddhatvamāsādya mano vimuktyai & bhavatyato buddhimato mumukṣos tābhyāṃ dṛḍhābhyāṃ bhavitavyamagre
verses.translation: Attaining purity through a preponderance of discrimination and renunciation, the mind makes for Liberation. Hence the wise seeker after Liberation must first strengthen these two.

verses.sanskrit: मनो नाम महाव्याघ्रो विषयारण्यभूमिषु चरत्यत्र न गच्छन्तु साधवो ये मुमुक्षवः
verses.transliteration: mano nāma mahāvyāghro viṣayāraṇyabhūmiṣu & caratyatra na gacchantu sādhavo ye mumukṣavaḥ
verses.translation: In the forest-tract of sense-pleasures there prowls a huge tiger called the mind. Let good people who have a longing for Liberation never go there.

verses.sanskrit: मनः प्रसूते विषयानशेषान् स्थूलात्मना सूक्ष्मतया च भोक्तुः शरीरवर्णाश्रमजातिभेदान् गुणक्रियाहेतुफलानि नित्यम्
verses.transliteration: manaḥ prasūte viṣayānaśeṣān sthūlātmanā sūkṣmatayā ca bhoktuḥ & śarīravarṇāśramajātibhedān guṇakriyāhetuphalāni nityam
verses.translation: The mind continually produces for the experiencer all sense-objects without exception, whether perceived as gross or fine, the differences of body, caste, order of life, and tribe, as well as the varieties of qualification, action, means and results.

verses.sanskrit: असङ्गचिद्रूपममुं विमोह्य देहेन्द्रियप्राणगुणैर् निबद्ध्य अहंममेति भ्रमयत्यजस्रं मनः स्वकृत्येषु फलोपभुक्तिषु
verses.transliteration: asaṅgacidrūpamamuṃ vimohya dehendriyaprāṇaguṇair nibaddhya & ahaṃmameti bhramayatyajasraṃ manaḥ svakṛtyeṣu phalopabhuktiṣu
verses.translation: Deluding the Jiva, which is unattached Pure Intelligence, and binding it by the ties of body, organs and Pranas, the mind causes it to wander, with ideas of "I" and "mine", amidst the varied enjoyment of results achieved by itself.

verses.sanskrit: अध्यासदोषात्पुरुषस्य संसृतिः अध्यासबन्धस्त्वमुनैव कल्पितः रजस्तमोदोषवतो ऽविवेकिनो जन्मादिदुःखस्य निदानमेतत्
verses.transliteration: adhyāsadoṣātpuruṣasya saṃsṛtiḥ adhyāsabandhastvamunaiva kalpitaḥ & rajastamodoṣavato 'vivekino janmādiduḥkhasya nidānametat
verses.translation: Mans transmigration is due to the evil of superimposition, and the bondage of superimposition is created by the mind alone. It is this that causes the misery of birth etc., for the man of non-discrimination who is tainted by Rajas and Tamas.

verses.sanskrit: अतः प्राहुर्मनो ऽविद्यां पण्डितास्तत्त्वदर्शिनः येनैव भ्राम्यते विश्वं वायुनेवाभ्रमण्डलम्
verses.transliteration: ataḥ prāhurmano 'vidyāṃ paṇḍitāstattvadarśinaḥ & yenaiva bhrāmyate viśvaṃ vāyunevābhramaṇḍalam
verses.translation: Hence sages who have fathomed its secret have designated the mind as Avidya or ignorance, by which alone the universe is moved to and fro, like masses of clouds by the wind.

verses.sanskrit: तन्मनःशोधनं कार्यं प्रयत्नेन मुमुक्षुणा विशुद्धे सति चैतस्मिन्मुक्तिः करफलायते
verses.transliteration: tanmanaḥśodhanaṃ kāryaṃ prayatnena mumukṣuṇā & viśuddhe sati caitasminmuktiḥ karaphalāyate
verses.translation: Therefore the seeker after Liberation must carefully purify the mind. When this is purified, Liberation is as easy of access as a fruit on the palm of ones hand.

verses.sanskrit: मोक्षैकसक्त्या विषयेषु रागं निर्मूल्य संन्यस्य च सर्वकर्म सच्छ्रद्धया यः श्रवणादिनिष्ठो रजःस्वभावं स धुनोति बुद्धेः
verses.transliteration: mokṣaikasaktyā viṣayeṣu rāgaṃ nirmūlya saṃnyasya ca sarvakarma & sacchraddhayā yaḥ śravaṇādiniṣṭho rajaḥsvabhāvaṃ sa dhunoti buddheḥ
verses.translation: He who by means of one-pointed devotion to Liberation roots out the attachment to sense-objects, renounces all actions, and with faith in the Real Brahman regularly practices hearing, etc., succeeds in purging the Rajasika nature of the intellect.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयो नापि भवेत्परात्मा ह्याद्यन्तवत्त्वात्परिणामिभावात् दुःखात्मकत्वाद्विषयत्वहेतोः द्रष्टा हि दृश्यात्मतया न दृष्टः
verses.transliteration: manomayo nāpi bhavetparātmā hyādyantavattvātpariṇāmibhāvāt & duḥkhātmakatvādviṣayatvahetoḥ draṣṭā hi dṛśyātmatayā na dṛṣṭaḥ
verses.translation: Neither can the mental sheath be the Supreme Self, because it has a beginning and an end, is subject to modifications, is characterised by pain and suffering and is an object; whereas the subject can never be identified with the objects of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धिर्बुद्धीन्द्रियैः सार्धं सवृत्तिः कर्तृलक्षणः विज्ञानमयकोशः स्यात्पुंसः संसारकारणम्
verses.transliteration: buddhirbuddhīndriyaiḥ sārdhaṃ savṛttiḥ kartṛlakṣaṇaḥ & vijñānamayakośaḥ syātpuṃsaḥ saṃsārakāraṇam
verses.translation: The Buddhi with its modifications and the organs of knowledge, forms the Vijnanamaya Kosha or knowledge sheath, of the agent, having the characteristics which is the cause of mans transmigration.

verses.sanskrit: अनुव्रजच्चित्प्रतिबिम्बशक्तिः विज्ञानसंज्ञः प्रकृतेर्विकारः ज्ञानक्रियावानहमित्यजस्रं देहेन्द्रियादिष्वभिमन्यते भृशम्
verses.transliteration: anuvrajaccitpratibimbaśaktiḥ vijñānasaṃjñaḥ prakṛtervikāraḥ & jñānakriyāvānahamityajasraṃ dehendriyādiṣvabhimanyate bhṛśam
verses.translation: This knowledge sheath, which seems to be followed by a reflection of the power of the Chit, is a modification of the Prakriti, is endowed with the function of knowledge, and always wholly identifies itself with the body, organs, etc.

verses.sanskrit: अनादिकालो ऽयमहंस्वभावो जीवः समस्तव्यवहारवोढा करोति कर्माण्य् अपि पूर्ववासनः पुण्यान्यपुण्यानि च तत्फलानि
verses.transliteration: anādikālo 'yamahaṃsvabhāvo jīvaḥ samastavyavahāravoḍhā & karoti karmāṇy api pūrvavāsanaḥ puṇyānyapuṇyāni ca tatphalāni
verses.translation: It is without beginning, characterised by egoism, is called the Jiva, and carries on all the activities on the relative plane. Through previous desires it performs good and evil actions and experiences their results. Being born in various bodies, it comes and goes, up and down. It is this knowledge sheath that has the waking, dream and other states, and experiences joy and grief.

verses.sanskrit: भुङ्क्ते विचित्रास्व् अपि योनिषु व्रजन् नायाति निर्यात्यध ऊर्ध्वमेषः अस्यैव विज्ञानमयस्य जाग्रत्- स्वप्नाद्यवस्थाः सुखदुःखभोगः
verses.transliteration: bhuṅkte vicitrāsv api yoniṣu vrajan nāyāti niryātyadha ūrdhvameṣaḥ & asyaiva vijñānamayasya jāgrat- svapnādyavasthāḥ sukhaduḥkhabhogaḥ
verses.translation: It is without beginning, characterised by egoism, is called the Jiva, and carries on all the activities on the relative plane. Through previous desires it performs good and evil actions and experiences their results. Being born in various bodies, it comes and goes, up and down. It is this knowledge sheath that has the waking, dream and other states, and experiences joy and grief.

verses.sanskrit: देहादिनिष्ठाश्रमधर्मकर्म- गुणाभिमानः सततं ममेति विज्ञानकोशो ऽयमतिप्रकाशः प्रकृष्टसान्निध्यवशात्परात्मनः अतो भवत्येष उपाधिरस्य यद् आत्मधीः संसरति भ्रमेण
verses.transliteration: dehādiniṣṭhāśramadharmakarma- guṇābhimānaḥ satataṃ mameti & vijñānakośo 'yamatiprakāśaḥ prakṛṣṭasānnidhyavaśātparātmanaḥ & ato bhavatyeṣa upādhirasya yad ātmadhīḥ saṃsarati bhrameṇa
verses.translation: It always mistakes the duties, functions and attributes of the orders of life which belong to the body, as its own. The knowledge sheath is exceedingly effulgent, owing to its close proximity to the Supreme Self, which identifying Itself with it suffers transmigration through delusion. It is therefore a superimposition on the Self.

verses.sanskrit: यो ऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु हृदि स्फुरत्ययं ज्योतिः कूटस्थः सन्नात्मा कर्ता भोक्ता भवत्युपाधिस्थः
verses.transliteration: yo 'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu hṛdi sphuratyayaṃ jyotiḥ & kūṭasthaḥ sannātmā kartā bhoktā bhavatyupādhisthaḥ
verses.translation: The self-effulgent Atman, which is Pure Knowledge, shines in the midst of the Pranas, within the heart. Though immutable, It becomes the agent and experiencer owing to Its superimposition, the knowledge sheath.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं परिच्छेदमुपेत्य बुद्धेः तादात्म्यदोषेण परं मृषात्मनः सर्वात्मकः सन्न् अपि वीक्षते स्वयं स्वतः पृथक्त्वेन मृदो घटान् इव
verses.transliteration: svayaṃ paricchedamupetya buddheḥ tādātmyadoṣeṇa paraṃ mṛṣātmanaḥ & sarvātmakaḥ sann api vīkṣate svayaṃ svataḥ pṛthaktvena mṛdo ghaṭān iva
verses.translation: Though the Self of everything that exists, this Atman, Itself assuming the limitations of the Buddhi and wrongly identifying Itself with this totally unreal entity, looks upon Itself as something different like earthen jars from the clay of which they are made.

verses.sanskrit: उपाधिसंबन्धवशात्परात्मा ह्युपाधिधर्माननुभाति तद्गुणः अयोविकारानविकारिवन्हिवत् सदैकरूपो ऽपि परः स्वभावात्
verses.transliteration: upādhisaṃbandhavaśātparātmā hyupādhidharmānanubhāti tadguṇaḥ & ayovikārānavikārivanhivat sadaikarūpo 'pi paraḥ svabhāvāt
verses.translation: Owing to Its connection with the super-impositions, the Supreme Self, even thou naturally perfect (transcending Nature) and eternally unchanging, assumes the qualities of the superimpositions and appears to act just as they do like the changeless fire assuming the modifications of the iron which it turns red-hot.

verses.sanskrit: शिष्य उवाच भ्रमेणाप्यन्यथा वास्तु जीवभावः परात्मनः तदुपाधेरनादित्वान्नानादेर्नाश इष्यते
verses.transliteration: śiṣya uvāca bhrameṇāpyanyathā vāstu jīvabhāvaḥ parātmanaḥ & tadupādheranāditvānnānādernāśa iṣyate
verses.translation: The disciple questioned: Be it through delusion or otherwise that the Supreme Self has come to consider Itself as the Jiva, this superimposition is without beginning, and that which has no beginning cannot be supposed to have an end either.

verses.sanskrit: अतो ऽस्य जीवभावो ऽपि नित्या भवति संसृतिः न निवर्तेत तन्मोक्षः कथं मे श्रीगुरो वद
verses.transliteration: ato 'sya jīvabhāvo 'pi nityā bhavati saṃsṛtiḥ & na nivarteta tanmokṣaḥ kathaṃ me śrīguro vada
verses.translation: Therefore the Jivahood of the soul also must have no end, and its transmigration must continue for ever. How then can there be Liberation for the soul ? Kindly enlighten me on this point, O revered Master.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीगुरुरुवाच सम्यक्पृष्टं त्वया विद्वन्सावधानेन तच्छृणु प्रामाणिकी न भवति भ्रान्त्या मोहितकल्पना
verses.transliteration: śrīgururuvāca samyakpṛṣṭaṃ tvayā vidvansāvadhānena tacchṛṇu & prāmāṇikī na bhavati bhrāntyā mohitakalpanā
verses.translation: The Teacher said: Thou hast rightly questioned, O learned man ! Listen therefore attentively: The imagination which has been conjured up by delusion can never be accepted as a fact.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रान्तिं विना त्वसङ्गस्य निष्क्रियस्य निराकृतेः न घटेतार्थसंबन्धो नभसो नीलतादिवत्
verses.transliteration: bhrāntiṃ vinā tvasaṅgasya niṣkriyasya nirākṛteḥ & na ghaṭetārthasaṃbandho nabhaso nīlatādivat
verses.translation: But for delusion there can be no connection of the Self which is unattached, beyond activity and formless with the objective world, as in the case of blueness etc., with reference to the sky.

verses.sanskrit: स्वस्य द्रष्टुर्निर्गुणस्याक्रियस्य प्रत्यग्बोधानन्दरूपस्य बुद्धेः भ्रान्त्या प्राप्तो जीवभावो न सत्यो मोहापाये नास्त्य् अवस्तुस्वभावात्
verses.transliteration: svasya draṣṭurnirguṇasyākriyasya pratyagbodhānandarūpasya buddheḥ & bhrāntyā prāpto jīvabhāvo na satyo mohāpāye nāsty avastusvabhāvāt
verses.translation: The Jivahood of the Atman, the Witness, which is beyond qualities and beyond activity, and which is realised within as Knowledge and Bliss Absolute has been superimposed by the delusion of the Buddhi, and is not real. And because it is by nature an unreality, it ceases to exist when the delusion is gone.

verses.sanskrit: यावद्भ्रान्तिस्तावदेवास्य सत्ता मिथ्याज्ञानोज्जृम्भितस्य प्रमादात् रज्ज्वां सर्पो भ्रान्तिकालीन एव भ्रान्तेर्नाशे नैव सर्पो ऽपि तद्वत्
verses.transliteration: yāvadbhrāntistāvadevāsya sattā mithyājñānojjṛmbhitasya pramādāt & rajjvāṃ sarpo bhrāntikālīna eva bhrānternāśe naiva sarpo 'pi tadvat
verses.translation: It exists only so long as the delusion lasts, being caused by indiscrimination due to an illusion. The rope is supposed to be the snake only so long as the mistake lasts, and there is no more snake when the illusion has vanished. Similar is the case here.

verses.sanskrit: अनादित्वमविद्यायाः कार्यस्यापि तथेष्यते उत्पन्नायां तु विद्यायामाविद्यकमनाद्य् अपि
verses.transliteration: anāditvamavidyāyāḥ kāryasyāpi tatheṣyate & utpannāyāṃ tu vidyāyāmāvidyakamanādy api
verses.translation: Avidya or Nescience and its effects are likewise considered as beginningless. But with the rise of Vidya or realisation, the entire effects of Avidya, even though beginningless, are destroyed together with their root like dreams on waking up from sleep. It is clear that the phenomenal universe, even though without beginning, is not eternal like previous non-existence.

verses.sanskrit: प्रबोधे स्वप्नवत्सर्वं सहमूलं विनश्यति अनाद्यपीदं नो नित्यं प्रागभाव इव स्फुटम्
verses.transliteration: prabodhe svapnavatsarvaṃ sahamūlaṃ vinaśyati & anādyapīdaṃ no nityaṃ prāgabhāva iva sphuṭam
verses.translation: Avidya or Nescience and its effects are likewise considered as beginningless. But with the rise of Vidya or realisation, the entire effects of Avidya, even though beginningless, are destroyed together with their root like dreams on waking up from sleep. It is clear that the phenomenal universe, even though without beginning, is not eternal like previous non-existence.

verses.sanskrit: अनादेर् अपि विध्वंसः प्रागभावस्य वीक्षितः यद्बुद्ध्युपाधिसंबन्धात्परिकल्पितम् आत्मनि
verses.transliteration: anāder api vidhvaṃsaḥ prāgabhāvasya vīkṣitaḥ & yadbuddhyupādhisaṃbandhātparikalpitam ātmani
verses.translation: Previous non-existence, even though beginningless, is observed to have an end. So the Jivahood which is imagined to be in the Atman through its relation with superimposed attributes such as the Buddhi, is not real; whereas the other (the Atman) is essentially different from it. The relation between the Atman and the Buddhi is due to a false knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: जीवत्वं न ततो ऽन्यस्तु स्वरूपेण विलक्षणः संबन्धस्त्व् आत्मनो बुद्ध्या मिथ्याज्ञानपुरःसरः
verses.transliteration: jīvatvaṃ na tato 'nyastu svarūpeṇa vilakṣaṇaḥ & saṃbandhastv ātmano buddhyā mithyājñānapuraḥsaraḥ
verses.translation: Previous non-existence, even though beginningless, is observed to have an end. So the Jivahood which is imagined to be in the Atman through its relation with superimposed attributes such as the Buddhi, is not real; whereas the other (the Atman) is essentially different from it. The relation between the Atman and the Buddhi is due to a false knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: विनिवृत्तिर्भवेत्तस्य सम्यग्ज्ञानेन नान्यथा ब्रह्मात्मैकत्वविज्ञानं सम्यग्ज्ञानं श्रुतेर्मतम्
verses.transliteration: vinivṛttirbhavettasya samyagjñānena nānyathā & brahmātmaikatvavijñānaṃ samyagjñānaṃ śrutermatam
verses.translation: The cessation of superimposition takes place through perfect knowledge and by no other means. According to the scriptures, perfect knowledge is realization of the identity of the individual self and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तदात्मानात्मनोः सम्यग्विवेकेनैव सिध्यति ततो विवेकः कर्तव्यः प्रत्यगात्मसदात्मनोः
verses.transliteration: tadātmānātmanoḥ samyagvivekenaiva sidhyati & tato vivekaḥ kartavyaḥ pratyagātmasadātmanoḥ
verses.translation: This realisation is attained by a perfect discrimination between the Self and the non-Self. Therefore one must strive for the discrimination between the individual soul and the eternal Self.

verses.sanskrit: जलं पङ्कवदत्यन्तं पङ्कापाये जलं स्फुटम् यथा भाति तथात्मापि दोषाभावे स्फुटप्रभः
verses.transliteration: jalaṃ paṅkavadatyantaṃ paṅkāpāye jalaṃ sphuṭam & yathā bhāti tathātmāpi doṣābhāve sphuṭaprabhaḥ
verses.translation: Just as the water which is very muddy again appears as transparent water when the mud is removed, so the Atman also manifests Its undimmed lustre when the taint has been removed.

verses.sanskrit: असन्निवृत्तौ तु सदात्मना स्फुटं प्रतीतिरेतस्य भवेत्प्रतीचः ततो निरासः करणीय एव सदात्मनः साध्वहमादिवस्तुनः
verses.transliteration: asannivṛttau tu sadātmanā sphuṭaṃ pratītiretasya bhavetpratīcaḥ & tato nirāsaḥ karaṇīya eva sadātmanaḥ sādhvahamādivastunaḥ
verses.translation: When the unreal ceases to exist, this very individual soul is definitely realised as the eternal Self. Therefore one must make it a point completely to remove things like egoism from the eternal Self.

verses.sanskrit: अतो नायं परात्मा स्याद्विज्ञानमयशब्दभाक् विकारित्वाज्जडत्वाच्च परिच्छिन्नत्वहेतुतः दृश्यत्वाद्व्यभिचारित्वान्नानित्यो नित्य इष्यते
verses.transliteration: ato nāyaṃ parātmā syādvijñānamayaśabdabhāk & vikāritvājjaḍatvācca paricchinnatvahetutaḥ & dṛśyatvādvyabhicāritvānnānityo nitya iṣyate
verses.translation: This knowledge sheath (Vijnanamaya Kosha) that we have been speaking of, cannot be the Supreme Self for the following reasons - because it is subject to change, is insentient, is a limited thing, an object of the senses, and is not constantly present: An unreal thing cannot indeed be taken for the real Atman.

verses.sanskrit: आनन्दप्रतिबिम्बचुम्बिततनुर्वृत्तिस्तमोजृम्भिता स्यादानन्दमयः प्रियादिगुणकः स्वेष्टार्थलाभोदयः पुण्यस्यानुभवे विभाति कृतिनामानन्दरूपः स्वयं सर्वो नन्दति यत्र साधु तनुभृन्मात्रः प्रयत्नं विना
verses.transliteration: ānandapratibimbacumbitatanurvṛttistamojṛmbhitā syādānandamayaḥ priyādiguṇakaḥ sveṣṭārthalābhodayaḥ & puṇyasyānubhave vibhāti kṛtināmānandarūpaḥ svayaṃ sarvo nandati yatra sādhu tanubhṛnmātraḥ prayatnaṃ vinā
verses.translation: The blissful sheath (Anandamaya Kosha) is that modification of Nescience which manifests itself catching a reflection of the Atman which is Bliss Absolute; whose attributes are pleasure and the rest; and which appears in view when some object agreeable to oneself presents itself. It makes itself spontaneously felt by the fortunate during the fruition of their virtuous deeds; from which every corporeal being derives great joy without the least effort.

verses.sanskrit: आनन्दमयकोशस्य सुषुप्तौ स्फूर्तिरुत्कटा स्वप्नजागरयोरीषदिष्टसंदर्शनाविना
verses.transliteration: ānandamayakośasya suṣuptau sphūrtirutkaṭā & svapnajāgarayorīṣadiṣṭasaṃdarśanāvinā
verses.translation: The blissful sheath has its fullest play during profound sleep, while in the dreaming and wakeful states it has only a partial manifestation, occasioned by the sight of agreeable objects and so forth.

verses.sanskrit: नैवायमानन्दमयः परात्मा सोपाधिकत्वात्प्रकृतेर्विकारात् कार्यत्वहेतोः सुकृतक्रियाया विकारसङ्घातसमाहितत्वात्
verses.transliteration: naivāyamānandamayaḥ parātmā sopādhikatvātprakṛtervikārāt & kāryatvahetoḥ sukṛtakriyāyā vikārasaṅghātasamāhitatvāt
verses.translation: Nor is the blissful sheath the Supreme Self, because it is endowed with the changeful attributes, is a modification of the Prakriti, is the effect of past good deeds, and imbedded in the other sheaths which are modifications.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चानाम् अपि कोशानां निषेधे युक्तितः श्रुतेः तन्निषेधावधि साक्षी बोधरूपो ऽवशिष्यते
verses.transliteration: pañcānām api kośānāṃ niṣedhe yuktitaḥ śruteḥ & tanniṣedhāvadhi sākṣī bodharūpo 'vaśiṣyate
verses.translation: When all the five sheaths have been eliminated by the reasoning on Shruti passages, what remains as the culminating point of the process, is the Witness, the Knowledge Absolute the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: यो ऽयमात्मा स्वयंज्योतिः पञ्चकोशविलक्षणः अवस्थात्रयसाक्षी सन्निर्विकारो निरञ्जनः \ सदानन्दः स विज्ञेयः स्वात्मत्वेन विपश्चिता
verses.transliteration: yo 'yamātmā svayaṃjyotiḥ pañcakośavilakṣaṇaḥ & avasthātrayasākṣī sannirvikāro nirañjanaḥ \ sadānandaḥ sa vijñeyaḥ svātmatvena vipaścitā
verses.translation: This self-effulgent Atman which is distinct from the five sheaths, the Witness of the three states, the Real, the Changeless, the Untainted, the everlasting Bliss is to be realised by the wise man as his own Self.

verses.sanskrit: शिष्य उवाच मिथ्यात्वेन निषिद्धेषु कोशेष्वेतेषु पञ्चसु सर्वाभावं विना किंचिन् न पश्याम्यत्र हे गुरो \ विज्ञेयं किमु वस्त्वस्ति स्वात्मनात्मविपश्चिता
verses.transliteration: śiṣya uvāca mithyātvena niṣiddheṣu kośeṣveteṣu pañcasu & sarvābhāvaṃ vinā kiṃcin na paśyāmyatra he guro \ vijñeyaṃ kimu vastvasti svātmanātmavipaścitā
verses.translation: The disciple questioned: After these five sheaths have been eliminated as unreal, I find nothing, O Master, in this universe but a Void, the absence of everything. What entity is there left forsooth with which the wise knower of the Self should realise his identity.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीगुरुरुवाच सत्यमुक्तं त्वया विदन्निपुणो ऽसि विचारणे अहमादिविकारास्ते तदभावो ऽयमप्यनु
verses.transliteration: śrīgururuvāca satyamuktaṃ tvayā vidannipuṇo 'si vicāraṇe & ahamādivikārāste tadabhāvo 'yamapyanu
verses.translation: The Guru answered: Thou has rightly said, O learned man ! Thou art clever indeed in discrimination. That by which all those modifications such as egoism as well as their subsequent absence (during deep sleep) are perceived, but which Itself is not perceived, know thou that Atman the Knower through the sharpest intellect.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे येनानुभूयन्ते यः स्वयं नानुभूयते तमात्मानं वेदितारं विद्दि बुद्ध्या सुसूक्ष्मया
verses.transliteration: sarve yenānubhūyante yaḥ svayaṃ nānubhūyate & tamātmānaṃ veditāraṃ viddi buddhyā susūkṣmayā
verses.translation: The Guru answered: Thou has rightly said, O learned man ! Thou art clever indeed in discrimination. That by which all those modifications such as egoism as well as their subsequent absence (during deep sleep) are perceived, but which Itself is not perceived, know thou that Atman the Knower through the sharpest intellect.

verses.sanskrit: तत्साक्षिकं भवेत्तत्तद्यद्यद्येनानुभूयते कस्याप्यननुभूतार्थे साक्षित्वं नोपयुज्यते
verses.transliteration: tatsākṣikaṃ bhavettattadyadyadyenānubhūyate & kasyāpyananubhūtārthe sākṣitvaṃ nopayujyate
verses.translation: That which is perceived by something else has for its witness the latter. When there is no agent to perceive a thing, we cannot speak of it as having been perceived at all.

verses.sanskrit: असौ स्वसाक्षिको भावो यतः स्वेनानुभूयते अतः परं स्वयं साक्षात्प्रत्यगात्मा न चेतरः
verses.transliteration: asau svasākṣiko bhāvo yataḥ svenānubhūyate & ataḥ paraṃ svayaṃ sākṣātpratyagātmā na cetaraḥ
verses.translation: This Atman is a self-cognised entity because It is cognised by Itself. Hence the individual soul is itself and directly the Supreme Brahman, and nothing else.

verses.sanskrit: जाग्रत्स्वप्नसुषुप्तिषु स्फुटतरं यो ऽसौ समुज्जृम्भते प्रत्यग्रूपतया सदाहमहमित्यन्तः स्फुरन्नैकधा नानाकारविकारभागिन इमान् पश्यन्नहंधीमुखान् नित्यानन्दचिद् आत्मना स्फुरति तं विद्धि स्वमेतं हृदि
verses.transliteration: jāgratsvapnasuṣuptiṣu sphuṭataraṃ yo 'sau samujjṛmbhate pratyagrūpatayā sadāhamahamityantaḥ sphurannaikadhā & nānākāravikārabhāgina imān paśyannahaṃdhīmukhān nityānandacid ātmanā sphurati taṃ viddhi svametaṃ hṛdi
verses.translation: That which clearly manifests Itself in the states of wakefulness, dream and profound sleep; which is inwardly perceived in the mind in various forms as an unbroken series of egoistic impressions; which witnesses the egoism, the Buddhi, etc., which are of diverse forms and modifications; and which makes Itself felt as the Existence-Knowledge-Bliss Absolute; know thou this Atman, thy own Self, within thy heart.

verses.sanskrit: घटोदके बिम्बितमर्कबिम्बम् आलोक्य मूढो रविम् एव मन्यते तथा चिदाभासमुपाधिसंस्थं भ्रान्त्याहम् इत्य् एव जडो ऽभिमन्यते
verses.transliteration: ghaṭodake bimbitamarkabimbam ālokya mūḍho ravim eva manyate & tathā cidābhāsamupādhisaṃsthaṃ bhrāntyāham ity eva jaḍo 'bhimanyate
verses.translation: Seeing the reflection of the sun mirrored in the water of a jar, the fool thinks it is the sun itself. Similarly the stupid man, through delusion, identifies himself with the reflection of the Chit caught in the Buddhi, which is Its superimposition.

verses.sanskrit: घटं जलं तद्गतमर्कबिम्बं विहाय सर्वं विनिरीक्ष्यते ऽर्कः तटस्थ एतत्त्रितयावभासकः स्वयंप्रकाशो विदुषा यथा तथा
verses.transliteration: ghaṭaṃ jalaṃ tadgatamarkabimbaṃ vihāya sarvaṃ vinirīkṣyate 'rkaḥ & taṭastha etattritayāvabhāsakaḥ svayaṃprakāśo viduṣā yathā tathā
verses.translation: Just as the wise man leaves aside the jar, the water and the reflection of the sun in it, and sees the self-luminous sun which illumines these three and is independent of them;

verses.sanskrit: देहं धियं चित्प्रतिबिम्बम् एवं विसृज्य बुद्धौ निहितं गुहायाम् द्रष्टारमात्मानमखण्डबोधं सर्वप्रकाशं सदसद्विलक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: dehaṃ dhiyaṃ citpratibimbam evaṃ visṛjya buddhau nihitaṃ guhāyām & draṣṭāramātmānamakhaṇḍabodhaṃ sarvaprakāśaṃ sadasadvilakṣaṇam
verses.translation: Similarly, discarding the body, the Buddhi and the reflection of the Chit in it, and realising the Witness, the Self, the Knowledge Absolute, the cause of the manifestation of everything, which is hidden in the recesses of the Buddhi, is distinct from the gross and subtle, eternal, omnipresent, all-pervading and extremely subtle, and which has neither interior nor exterior and is identical with one self fully realising this true nature of oneself, one becomes free from sin, taint, death and grief, and becomes the embodiment of Bliss. Illumined himself, he is afraid of none. For a seeker after Liberation there is no other way to the breaking of the bonds of transmigration than the realisation of the truth of ones own Self.

verses.sanskrit: नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं अन्तर्बहिःशून्यमनन्यम् आत्मनः विज्ञाय सम्यङ्निजरूपमेतत् पुमान् विपाप्मा विरजो विमृत्युः
verses.transliteration: nityaṃ vibhuṃ sarvagataṃ susūkṣmaṃ antarbahiḥśūnyamananyam ātmanaḥ & vijñāya samyaṅnijarūpametat pumān vipāpmā virajo vimṛtyuḥ
verses.translation: Similarly, discarding the body, the Buddhi and the reflection of the Chit in it, and realising the Witness, the Self, the Knowledge Absolute, the cause of the manifestation of everything, which is hidden in the recesses of the Buddhi, is distinct from the gross and subtle, eternal, omnipresent, all-pervading and extremely subtle, and which has neither interior nor exterior and is identical with one self fully realising this true nature of oneself, one becomes free from sin, taint, death and grief, and becomes the embodiment of Bliss. Illumined himself, he is afraid of none. For a seeker after Liberation there is no other way to the breaking of the bonds of transmigration than the realisation of the truth of ones own Self.

verses.sanskrit: विशोक आनन्दघनो विपश्चित् स्वयं कुतश्चिन् न बिभेति कश्चित् नान्यो ऽस्ति पन्था भवबन्धमुक्तेः विना स्वतत्त्वावगमं मुमुक्षोः
verses.transliteration: viśoka ānandaghano vipaścit svayaṃ kutaścin na bibheti kaścit & nānyo 'sti panthā bhavabandhamukteḥ vinā svatattvāvagamaṃ mumukṣoḥ
verses.translation: Similarly, discarding the body, the Buddhi and the reflection of the Chit in it, and realising the Witness, the Self, the Knowledge Absolute, the cause of the manifestation of everything, which is hidden in the recesses of the Buddhi, is distinct from the gross and subtle, eternal, omnipresent, all-pervading and extremely subtle, and which has neither interior nor exterior and is identical with one self fully realising this true nature of oneself, one becomes free from sin, taint, death and grief, and becomes the embodiment of Bliss. Illumined himself, he is afraid of none. For a seeker after Liberation there is no other way to the breaking of the bonds of transmigration than the realisation of the truth of ones own Self.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्माभिन्नत्वविज्ञानं भवमोक्षस्य कारणम् येनाद्वितीयमानन्दं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते बुधैः
verses.transliteration: brahmābhinnatvavijñānaṃ bhavamokṣasya kāraṇam & yenādvitīyamānandaṃ brahma sampadyate budhaiḥ
verses.translation: The realisation of ones identity with Brahman is the cause of Liberation from the bonds of Samsara, by means of which the wise man attains Brahman, the One without a second, the Bliss Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतस्तु संसृत्यै विद्वान्नावर्तते पुनः विज्ञातव्यमतः सम्यग्ब्रह्माभिन्नत्वम् आत्मनः
verses.transliteration: brahmabhūtastu saṃsṛtyai vidvānnāvartate punaḥ & vijñātavyamataḥ samyagbrahmābhinnatvam ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Once having realised Brahman, one no longer returns to the realm of transmigration. Therefore one must fully realise ones identity with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म विशुद्धं परं स्वतःसिद्धम् नित्यानन्दैकरसं प्रत्यगभिन्नं निरन्तरं जयति
verses.transliteration: satyaṃ jñānamanantaṃ brahma viśuddhaṃ paraṃ svataḥsiddham & nityānandaikarasaṃ pratyagabhinnaṃ nirantaraṃ jayati
verses.translation: Brahman is Existence, Knowledge, Infinity, pure, supreme, self-existent, eternal and indivisible Bliss, not different (in reality) from the individual soul, and devoid of interior or exterior. It is (ever) triumphant.

verses.sanskrit: सदिदं परमाद्वैतं स्वस्मादन्यस्य वस्तुनो ऽभावात् न ह्यन्यदस्ति किंचित् सम्यक् परमार्थतत्त्वबोधदशायाम्
verses.transliteration: sadidaṃ paramādvaitaṃ svasmādanyasya vastuno 'bhāvāt & na hyanyadasti kiṃcit samyak paramārthatattvabodhadaśāyām
verses.translation: It is this Supreme Oneness which alone is real, since there is nothing else but the Self. Verily, there remains no other independent entity in the state of realisation of the highest Truth.

verses.sanskrit: यदिदं सकलं विश्वं नानारूपं प्रतीतमज्ञानात् तत्सर्वं ब्रह्मैव प्रत्यस्ताशेषभावनादोषम्
verses.transliteration: yadidaṃ sakalaṃ viśvaṃ nānārūpaṃ pratītamajñānāt & tatsarvaṃ brahmaiva pratyastāśeṣabhāvanādoṣam
verses.translation: All this universe which through ignorance appears as of diverse forms, is nothing else but Brahman which is absolutely free from all the limitations of human thought.

verses.sanskrit: मृत्कार्यभूतो ऽपि मृदो न भिन्नः कुम्भो ऽस्ति सर्वत्र तु मृत्स्वरूपात् न कुम्भरूपं पृथगस्ति कुम्भः कुतो मृषा कल्पितनाममात्रः
verses.transliteration: mṛtkāryabhūto 'pi mṛdo na bhinnaḥ kumbho 'sti sarvatra tu mṛtsvarūpāt & na kumbharūpaṃ pṛthagasti kumbhaḥ kuto mṛṣā kalpitanāmamātraḥ
verses.translation: A jar, though a modification of clay, is not different from it; everywhere the jar is essentially the same as the clay. Why then call it a jar ? It is fictitious, a fancied name merely.

verses.sanskrit: केनापि मृद्भिन्नतया स्वरूपं घटस्य संदर्शयितुं न शक्यते अतो घटः कल्पित एव मोहान् मृद् एव सत्यं परमार्थभूतम्
verses.transliteration: kenāpi mṛdbhinnatayā svarūpaṃ ghaṭasya saṃdarśayituṃ na śakyate & ato ghaṭaḥ kalpita eva mohān mṛd eva satyaṃ paramārthabhūtam
verses.translation: None can demonstrate that the essence of a jar is something other than the clay (of which it is made). Hence the jar is merely imagined (as separate) through delusion, and the component clay alone is the abiding reality in respect of it.

verses.sanskrit: सद्ब्रह्मकार्यं सकलं सदेवं तन्मात्रमेतन् न ततो ऽन्यदस्ति अस्तीति यो वक्ति न तस्य मोहो विनिर्गतो निद्रितवत्प्रजल्पः
verses.transliteration: sadbrahmakāryaṃ sakalaṃ sadevaṃ tanmātrametan na tato 'nyadasti & astīti yo vakti na tasya moho vinirgato nidritavatprajalpaḥ
verses.translation: Similarly, the whole universe, being the effect of the real Brahman, is in reality nothing but Brahman. Its essence is That, and it does not exist apart from It. He who says it does is still under delusion he babbles like one asleep.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मैवेदं विश्वम् इत्य् एव वाणी श्रौती ब्रूते ऽथर्वनिष्ठा वरिष्ठा तस्मादेतद्ब्रह्ममात्रं हि विश्वं नाधिष्ठानाद्भिन्नतारोपितस्य
verses.transliteration: brahmaivedaṃ viśvam ity eva vāṇī śrautī brūte 'tharvaniṣṭhā variṣṭhā & tasmādetadbrahmamātraṃ hi viśvaṃ nādhiṣṭhānādbhinnatāropitasya
verses.translation: This universe is verily Brahman such is the august pronouncement of the Atharva Veda. Therefore this universe is nothing but Brahman, for that which is superimposed (on something) has no separate existence from its substratum.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यं यदि स्याज्जगदेतद् आत्मनो ऽनन्तत्त्वहानिर्निगमाप्रमाणता असत्यवादित्वमपीशितुः स्यान् नैतत्त्रयं साधु हितं महात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: satyaṃ yadi syājjagadetad ātmano 'nantattvahānirnigamāpramāṇatā & asatyavāditvamapīśituḥ syān naitattrayaṃ sādhu hitaṃ mahātmanām
verses.translation: If the universe, as it is, be real, there would be no cessation of the dualistic element, the scriptures would be falsified, and the Lord Himself would be guilty of an untruth. None of these three is considered either desirable or wholesome by the noble-minded.

verses.sanskrit: ईश्वरो वस्तुतत्त्वज्ञो न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः न च मत्स्थानि भूतानीत्य् एवम् एव व्यचीकॢपत्
verses.transliteration: īśvaro vastutattvajño na cāhaṃ teṣvavasthitaḥ & na ca matsthāni bhūtānīty evam eva vyacīkḷpat
verses.translation: The Lord, who knows the secret of all things has supported this view in the words: "But I am not in them" "nor are the beings in Me".

verses.sanskrit: यदि सत्यं भवेद्विश्वं सुषुप्तामुपलभ्यताम् यन्नोपलभ्यते किंचिद् अतो ऽसत्स्वप्नवन्मृषा
verses.transliteration: yadi satyaṃ bhavedviśvaṃ suṣuptāmupalabhyatām & yannopalabhyate kiṃcid ato 'satsvapnavanmṛṣā
verses.translation: If the universe be true, let it then be perceived in the state of deep sleep also. As it is not at all perceived, it must be unreal and false, like dreams.

verses.sanskrit: अतः पृथङ्नास्ति जगत्परात्मनः पृथक्प्रतीतिस्तु मृषा गुणादिवत् आरोपितस्यास्ति किमर्थवत्ताद् धिष्ठानमाभाति तथा भ्रमेण
verses.transliteration: ataḥ pṛthaṅnāsti jagatparātmanaḥ pṛthakpratītistu mṛṣā guṇādivat & āropitasyāsti kimarthavattād dhiṣṭhānamābhāti tathā bhrameṇa
verses.translation: Therefore the universe does not exist apart from the Supreme Self; and the perception of its separateness is false like the qualities (of blueness etc., in the sky). Has a superimposed attribute any meaning apart from its substratum ? It is the substratum which appears like that through delusion.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रान्तस्य यद्य् अद्भ्रमतः प्रतीतं भ्रामैव तत्तद्रजतं हि शुक्तिः इदंतया ब्रह्म सदैव रूप्यते त्वारोपितं ब्रह्मणि नाममात्रम्
verses.transliteration: bhrāntasya yady adbhramataḥ pratītaṃ bhrāmaiva tattadrajataṃ hi śuktiḥ & idaṃtayā brahma sadaiva rūpyate tvāropitaṃ brahmaṇi nāmamātram
verses.translation: Whatever a deluded man perceives through mistake, is Brahman and Brahman alone: The silver is nothing but the mother-of-pearl. It is Brahman which is always considered as this universe, whereas that which is superimposed on the Brahman, viz. the universe, is merely a name.

verses.sanskrit: अतः परं ब्रह्म सदद्वितीयं विशुद्धविज्ञानघनं निरञ्जनम् प्राशान्तमाद्यन्तविहीनमक्रियं निरन्तरानन्दरसस्वरूपम्
verses.transliteration: ataḥ paraṃ brahma sadadvitīyaṃ viśuddhavijñānaghanaṃ nirañjanam & prāśāntamādyantavihīnamakriyaṃ nirantarānandarasasvarūpam
verses.translation: Hence whatever is manifested, viz. this universe, is the Supreme Brahman Itself, the Real, the One without a second, pure, the Essence of Knowledge, taintless, serene, devoid of beginning and end, beyond activity, the Essence of Bliss Absolute transcending all the diversities created by Maya or Nescience, eternal, ever beyond the reach of pain, indivisible, immeasurable, formless, undifferentiated, nameless, immutable, self-luminous.

verses.sanskrit: निरस्तमायाकृतसर्वभेदं नित्यं सुखं निष्कलमप्रमेयम् अरूपमव्यक्तमनाख्यमव्ययं ज्योतिः स्वयं किंचिद् इदं चकास्ति
verses.transliteration: nirastamāyākṛtasarvabhedaṃ nityaṃ sukhaṃ niṣkalamaprameyam & arūpamavyaktamanākhyamavyayaṃ jyotiḥ svayaṃ kiṃcid idaṃ cakāsti
verses.translation: Hence whatever is manifested, viz. this universe, is the Supreme Brahman Itself, the Real, the One without a second, pure, the Essence of Knowledge, taintless, serene, devoid of beginning and end, beyond activity, the Essence of Bliss Absolute transcending all the diversities created by Maya or Nescience, eternal, ever beyond the reach of pain, indivisible, immeasurable, formless, undifferentiated, nameless, immutable, self-luminous.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञातृज्ञेयज्ञानशून्यमनन्तं निर्विकल्पकम् केवलाखण्डचिन्मात्रं परं तत्त्वं विदुर्बुधाः
verses.transliteration: jñātṛjñeyajñānaśūnyamanantaṃ nirvikalpakam & kevalākhaṇḍacinmātraṃ paraṃ tattvaṃ vidurbudhāḥ
verses.translation: Sages realise the Supreme Truth, Brahman, in which there is no differentiation of knower, knowledge and known, which is infinite, transcendent, and the Essence of Knowledge Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: अहेयमनुपादेयं मनोवाचामगोचरम् अप्रमेयमनाद्यन्तं ब्रह्म पूर्णमहं महः
verses.transliteration: aheyamanupādeyaṃ manovācāmagocaram & aprameyamanādyantaṃ brahma pūrṇamahaṃ mahaḥ
verses.translation: Which can be neither thrown away nor taken up, which is beyond the reach of mind and speech, immeasurable, without beginning and end, the Whole, ones very Self, and of surpassing glory.

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वंपदाभ्यामभिधीयमानयोः ब्रह्मात्मनोः शोधितयोर्यदीत्थम् श्रुत्या तयोस्तत्त्वमसीति सम्यग् एकत्वम् एव प्रतिपाद्यते मुहुः
verses.transliteration: tattvaṃpadābhyāmabhidhīyamānayoḥ brahmātmanoḥ śodhitayoryadīttham & śrutyā tayostattvamasīti samyag ekatvam eva pratipādyate muhuḥ
verses.translation: If thus the Shruti, in the dictum "Thou art That" (Tat-Tvam-Asi), repeatedly establishes the absolute identity of Brahman (or Ishwara) and Jiva, denoted by the terms That (Tat) and thou (Tvam) respectively, divesting these terms of their relative associations, then it is the identity of their implied, not literal, meanings which is sought to be inculcated; for they are of contradictory attributes to each other like the sun and a glow-worm, the king and a servant, the ocean and a well, or Mount Meru and an atom.

verses.sanskrit: ऐक्यं तयोर् लक्षितयोर् न वाच्ययोः निगद्यते ऽन्योन्यविरुद्धधर्मिणोः खद्योतभान्वोर् इव राजभृत्ययोः कूपाम्बुराश्योः परमाणुमेर्वोः
verses.transliteration: aikyaṃ tayor lakṣitayor na vācyayoḥ nigadyate 'nyonyaviruddhadharmiṇoḥ & khadyotabhānvor iva rājabhṛtyayoḥ kūpāmburāśyoḥ paramāṇumervoḥ
verses.translation: If thus the Shruti, in the dictum "Thou art That" (Tat-Tvam-Asi), repeatedly establishes the absolute identity of Brahman (or Ishwara) and Jiva, denoted by the terms That (Tat) and thou (Tvam) respectively, divesting these terms of their relative associations, then it is the identity of their implied, not literal, meanings which is sought to be inculcated; for they are of contradictory attributes to each other like the sun and a glow-worm, the king and a servant, the ocean and a well, or Mount Meru and an atom.

verses.sanskrit: तयोर् विरोधो ऽयमुपाधिकल्पितो न वास्तवः कश्चिदुपाधिरेषः ईशस्य माया महदादिकारणं जीवस्य कार्यं शृणु पञ्चकोशम्
verses.transliteration: tayor virodho 'yamupādhikalpito na vāstavaḥ kaścidupādhireṣaḥ & īśasya māyā mahadādikāraṇaṃ jīvasya kāryaṃ śṛṇu pañcakośam
verses.translation: This contradiction between them is created by superimposition, and is not something real. This superimposition, in the case of Ishwara (the Lord), is Maya or Nescience, which is the cause of Mahat and the rest, and in the case of the Jiva (the individual soul), listen the five sheaths, which are the effects of Maya, stand for it.

verses.sanskrit: एतावुपाधी परजीवयोस्तयोः सम्यङ्निरासे न परो न जीवः राज्यं नरेन्द्रस्य भटस्य खेटक् तयोर् अपोहे न भटो न राजा
verses.transliteration: etāvupādhī parajīvayostayoḥ samyaṅnirāse na paro na jīvaḥ & rājyaṃ narendrasya bhaṭasya kheṭak tayor apohe na bhaṭo na rājā
verses.translation: These two are the superimpositions of Ishwara and the Jiva respectively, and when these are perfectly eliminated, there is neither Ishwara nor Jiva. A kingdom is the symbol of a king, and a shield of the soldier, and when these are taken away, there is neither king nor soldier.

verses.sanskrit: अथात आदेश इति श्रुतिः स्वयं निषेधति ब्रह्मणि कल्पितं द्वयम् श्रुतिप्रमाणानुगृहीतबोधात् तयोर् निरासः करणीय एव
verses.transliteration: athāta ādeśa iti śrutiḥ svayaṃ niṣedhati brahmaṇi kalpitaṃ dvayam & śrutipramāṇānugṛhītabodhāt tayor nirāsaḥ karaṇīya eva
verses.translation: The Vedas themselves in the words "now then is the injunction" etc., repudiate the duality imagined in Brahman. One must needs eliminate those two superimpositions by means of realisation supported by the authority of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: नेदं नेदं कल्पितत्वान् न सत्यं रज्जुदृष्टव्यालवत्स्वप्नवच्च इत्थं दृश्यं साधुयुक्त्या व्यपोह्य ज्ञेयः पश्चादेकभावस्तयोर् यः
verses.transliteration: nedaṃ nedaṃ kalpitatvān na satyaṃ rajjudṛṣṭavyālavatsvapnavacca & itthaṃ dṛśyaṃ sādhuyuktyā vyapohya jñeyaḥ paścādekabhāvastayor yaḥ
verses.translation: Neither this gross nor this subtle universe (is the Atman). Being imagined, they are not real like the snake seen in the rope, and like dreams. Perfectly eliminating the objective world in this way by means of reasoning, one should next realise the oneness that underlies Ishwara and the Jiva.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तु तौ लक्षणया सुलक्ष्यौ तयोर् अखण्डैकरसत्वसिद्धये नालं जहत्या न तथाजहत्या किन्तूभयार्थात्मिकयैव भाव्यम्
verses.transliteration: tatastu tau lakṣaṇayā sulakṣyau tayor akhaṇḍaikarasatvasiddhaye & nālaṃ jahatyā na tathājahatyā kintūbhayārthātmikayaiva bhāvyam
verses.translation: Hence those two terms (Ishwara and Jiva) must be carefully considered through their implied meanings, so that their absolute identity may be established. Neither the method of total rejection nor that of complete retention will do. One must reason out through the process which combines the two.

verses.sanskrit: स देवदत्तो ऽयमितीह चैकता विरुद्धधर्मांशमपास्य कथ्यते यथा तथा तत्त्वमसीतिवाक्ये विरुद्धधर्मानुभयत्र हित्वा
verses.transliteration: sa devadatto 'yamitīha caikatā viruddhadharmāṃśamapāsya kathyate & yathā tathā tattvamasītivākye viruddhadharmānubhayatra hitvā
verses.translation: Just as in the sentence, "This is that Devadatta", the identity is spoken of, eliminating the contradictory portions, so in the sentence "Thou art That", the wise man must give up the contradictory elements on both sides and recognise the identity of Ishwara and Jiva, noticing carefully the essence of both, which is Chit, Knowledge Absolute. Thus hundreds of scriptural texts inculcate the oneness and identity of Brahman and Jiva.

verses.sanskrit: संलक्ष्य चिन्मात्रतया सदात्मनोः अखण्डभावः परिचीयते बुधैः एवं महावाक्यशतेन कथ्यते ब्रह्मात्मनोर् ऐक्यमखण्डभावः
verses.transliteration: saṃlakṣya cinmātratayā sadātmanoḥ akhaṇḍabhāvaḥ paricīyate budhaiḥ & evaṃ mahāvākyaśatena kathyate brahmātmanor aikyamakhaṇḍabhāvaḥ
verses.translation: Just as in the sentence, "This is that Devadatta", the identity is spoken of, eliminating the contradictory portions, so in the sentence "Thou art That", the wise man must give up the contradictory elements on both sides and recognise the identity of Ishwara and Jiva, noticing carefully the essence of both, which is Chit, Knowledge Absolute. Thus hundreds of scriptural texts inculcate the oneness and identity of Brahman and Jiva.

verses.sanskrit: अस्थूलमित्येतदसन्निरस्य सिद्धं स्वतो व्योमवदप्रतर्क्यम् अतो मृषामात्रमिदं प्रतीतं जहीहि यत्स्वात्मतया गृहीतम् \ ब्रह्माहम् इत्य् एव विशुद्धबुद्ध्या विद्धि स्वमात्मानमखण्डबोधम्
verses.transliteration: asthūlamityetadasannirasya siddhaṃ svato vyomavadapratarkyam & ato mṛṣāmātramidaṃ pratītaṃ jahīhi yatsvātmatayā gṛhītam \ brahmāham ity eva viśuddhabuddhyā viddhi svamātmānamakhaṇḍabodham
verses.translation: Eliminating the not-Self, in the light of such passages as "It is not gross" etc., (one realises the Atman), which is self-established, unattached like the sky, and beyond the range of thought. Therefore dismiss this mere phantom of a body which thou perceivest and hast accepted as thy own self. By means of the purified understanding that thou art Brahman, realise thy own self, the Knowledge Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: मृत्कार्यं सकलं घटादि सततं मृन्मात्रमेवाहितं तद्वत्सज्जनितं सदात्मकमिदं सन्मात्रमेवाखिलम् यस्मान्नास्ति सतः परं किम् अपि तत्सत्यं स आत्मा स्वयं तस्मात्तत्त्वमसि प्रशान्तममलं ब्रह्माद्वयं यत्परम्
verses.transliteration: mṛtkāryaṃ sakalaṃ ghaṭādi satataṃ mṛnmātramevāhitaṃ tadvatsajjanitaṃ sadātmakamidaṃ sanmātramevākhilam & yasmānnāsti sataḥ paraṃ kim api tatsatyaṃ sa ātmā svayaṃ tasmāttattvamasi praśāntamamalaṃ brahmādvayaṃ yatparam
verses.translation: All modifications of clay, such as the jar, which are always accepted by the mind as real, are (in reality) nothing but clay. Similarly, this entire universe which is produced from the real Brahman, is Brahman Itself and nothing but That. Because there is nothing else whatever but Brahman, and That is the only self-existent Reality, our very Self, therefore art thou that serene, pure, Supreme Brahman, the One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: निद्राकल्पितदेशकालविषयज्ञात्रादि सर्वं यथा मिथ्या तद्वदिहापि जाग्रति जगत्स्वाज्ञानकार्यत्वतः यस्माद् एवमिदं शरीरकरणप्राणाहमाद्यप्यसत् तस्मात्तत्त्वमसि प्रशान्तममलं ब्रह्माद्वयं यत्परम्
verses.transliteration: nidrākalpitadeśakālaviṣayajñātrādi sarvaṃ yathā mithyā tadvadihāpi jāgrati jagatsvājñānakāryatvataḥ & yasmād evamidaṃ śarīrakaraṇaprāṇāhamādyapyasat tasmāttattvamasi praśāntamamalaṃ brahmādvayaṃ yatparam
verses.translation: As the place, time, objects, knower, etc., called up in dream are all unreal, so is also the world experienced here in the waking state, for it is all an effect of ones own ignorance. Because this body, the organs, the Pranas, egoism, etc., are also thus unreal, therefore art thou that serene, pure, supreme Brahman, the One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र भ्रान्त्या कल्पित तद्विवेके तत्तन्मात्रं नैव तस्माद्विभिन्नम् स्वप्ने नष्टं स्वप्नविश्वं विचित्रं स्वस्माद्भिन्नं किन्नु दृष्टं प्रबोधे
verses.transliteration: yatra bhrāntyā kalpita tadviveke tattanmātraṃ naiva tasmādvibhinnam & svapne naṣṭaṃ svapnaviśvaṃ vicitraṃ svasmādbhinnaṃ kinnu dṛṣṭaṃ prabodhe
verses.translation: (What is) erroneously supposed to exist in something, is, when the truth about it has been known, nothing but that substratum, and not at all different from it: The diversified dream universe (appears and) passes away in the dream itself. Does it appear on waking as something distinct from ones own Self ?

verses.sanskrit: जातिनीतिकुलगोत्रदूरगं नामरूपगुणदोषवर्जितम् देशकालविषयातिवर्ति यद् ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: jātinītikulagotradūragaṃ nāmarūpaguṇadoṣavarjitam & deśakālaviṣayātivarti yad brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is beyond caste and creed, family and lineage; devoid of name and form, merit and demerit; transcending space, time and sense-object that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: यत्परं सकलवागगोचरं गोचरं विमलबोधचक्षुषः शुद्धचिद्घनमनादि वस्तु यद् ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: yatparaṃ sakalavāgagocaraṃ gocaraṃ vimalabodhacakṣuṣaḥ & śuddhacidghanamanādi vastu yad brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That Supreme Brahman which is beyond the range of all speech, but accessible to the eye of pure illumination; which is pure, the Embodiment of Knowledge, the beginningless entity that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: षड्भिरूर्मिभिरयोगि योगिहृद्- भावितं न करणैर् विभावितम् बुद्ध्यवेद्यमनवद्यमस्ति यद् ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: ṣaḍbhirūrmibhirayogi yogihṛd- bhāvitaṃ na karaṇair vibhāvitam & buddhyavedyamanavadyamasti yad brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is untouched by the sixfold wave; meditated upon by the Yogis heart, but not grasped by the sense-organs; which the Buddhi cannot know; and which is unimpeachable that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रान्तिकल्पितजगत्कलाश्रयं स्वाश्रयं च सदसद्विलक्षणम् निष्कलं निरुपमानवद्धि यद् ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: bhrāntikalpitajagatkalāśrayaṃ svāśrayaṃ ca sadasadvilakṣaṇam & niṣkalaṃ nirupamānavaddhi yad brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is the substratum of the universe with its various subdivisions, which are all creations of delusion; which Itself has no other support; which is distinct from the gross and subtle; which has no parts, and has verily no exemplar that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: जन्मवृद्धिपरिणत्यपक्षय- व्याधिनाशनविहीनमव्ययम् विश्वसृष्ट्यवविघातकारणं ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: janmavṛddhipariṇatyapakṣaya- vyādhināśanavihīnamavyayam & viśvasṛṣṭyavavighātakāraṇaṃ brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is free from birth, growth, development, waste, disease and death; which is indestructible; which is the cause of the projection, maintenance and dissolution of the universe that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: अस्तभेदमनपास्तलक्षणं निस्तरङ्गजलराशिनिश्चलम् नित्यमुक्तमविभक्तमूर्ति यद् ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: astabhedamanapāstalakṣaṇaṃ nistaraṅgajalarāśiniścalam & nityamuktamavibhaktamūrti yad brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is free from differentiation; whose essence is never non-existent; which is unmoved like the ocean without waves; the ever-free; of indivisible Form that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: एकम् एव सदनेककारणं कारणान्तरनिरास्यकारणम् कार्यकारणविलक्षणं स्वयं ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: ekam eva sadanekakāraṇaṃ kāraṇāntaranirāsyakāraṇam & kāryakāraṇavilakṣaṇaṃ svayaṃ brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which, though One only, is the cause of the many; which refutes all other causes, but is Itself without cause; distinct from Maya and its effect, the universe; and independent that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: निर्विकल्पकमनल्पमक्षरं यत्क्षराक्षरविलक्षणं परम् नित्यमव्ययसुखं निरञ्जनं ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: nirvikalpakamanalpamakṣaraṃ yatkṣarākṣaravilakṣaṇaṃ param & nityamavyayasukhaṃ nirañjanaṃ brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That which is free from duality; which is infinite and indestructible; distinct from the universe and Maya, supreme, eternal; which is undying Bliss; taintless that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: यद्विभाति सदनेकधा भ्रमान् नामरूपगुणविक्रियात्मना हेमवत्स्वयमविक्रियं सदा ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: yadvibhāti sadanekadhā bhramān nāmarūpaguṇavikriyātmanā & hemavatsvayamavikriyaṃ sadā brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That Reality which (though One) appears variously owing to delusion, taking on names and forms, attributes and changes, Itself always unchanged, like gold in its modifications that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: यच्चकास्त्यनपरं परात्परं प्रत्यगेकरसम् आत्मलक्षणम् सत्यचित्सुखमनन्तमव्ययं ब्रह्म तत्त्वमसि भावयात्मनि
verses.transliteration: yaccakāstyanaparaṃ parātparaṃ pratyagekarasam ātmalakṣaṇam & satyacitsukhamanantamavyayaṃ brahma tattvamasi bhāvayātmani
verses.translation: That beyond which there is nothing; which shines even above Maya, which again is superior to its effect, the universe; the inmost Self of all, free from differentiation; the Real Self, the Existence-Knowledge-Bliss Absolute; infinite and immutable that Brahman art thou, meditate on this in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: उक्तमर्थमिमम् आत्मनि स्वयं भावयेत्प्रथितयुक्तिभिर्धिया संशयादिरहितं कराम्बुवत् तेन तत्त्वनिगमो भविष्यति
verses.transliteration: uktamarthamimam ātmani svayaṃ bhāvayetprathitayuktibhirdhiyā & saṃśayādirahitaṃ karāmbuvat tena tattvanigamo bhaviṣyati
verses.translation: On the Truth, inculcated above, one must oneself meditate in ones mind, through the intellect, by means of the recognised arguments. By that means one will realise the truth free from doubt etc., like water in the palm of ones hand.

verses.sanskrit: संबोधमात्रं परिशुद्धतत्त्वं विज्ञाय सङ्घे नृपवच्च सैन्ये तदाश्रयः स्वात्मनि सर्वदा स्थितो विलापय ब्रह्मणि विश्वजातम्
verses.transliteration: saṃbodhamātraṃ pariśuddhatattvaṃ vijñāya saṅghe nṛpavacca sainye & tadāśrayaḥ svātmani sarvadā sthito vilāpaya brahmaṇi viśvajātam
verses.translation: Realising in this body the Knowledge Absolute free from Nescience and its effects like the king in an army and being ever established in thy own Self by resting on that Knowledge, merge the universe in Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धौ गुहायां सदसद्विलक्षणं ब्रह्मास्ति सत्यं परमद्वितीयम् तद् आत्मना यो ऽत्र वसेद्गुहायां पुनर्न तस्याङ्गगुहाप्रवेशः
verses.transliteration: buddhau guhāyāṃ sadasadvilakṣaṇaṃ brahmāsti satyaṃ paramadvitīyam & tad ātmanā yo 'tra vasedguhāyāṃ punarna tasyāṅgaguhāpraveśaḥ
verses.translation: In the cave of the Buddhi there is the Brahman, distinct from the gross and subtle, the Existence Absolute, Supreme, the One without a second. For one who lives in this cave as Brahman, O beloved, there is no more entrance into the mothers womb.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञाते वस्तुन्य् अपि बलवती वासनानादिरेषा कर्ता भोक्ताप्यहमिति दृढा यास्य संसारहेतुः प्रत्यग्दृष्ट्यात्मनि निवसता सापनेया प्रयत्नान् मुक्तिं प्राहुस्तदिह मुनयो वासनातानवं यत्
verses.transliteration: jñāte vastuny api balavatī vāsanānādireṣā kartā bhoktāpyahamiti dṛḍhā yāsya saṃsārahetuḥ & pratyagdṛṣṭyātmani nivasatā sāpaneyā prayatnān muktiṃ prāhustadiha munayo vāsanātānavaṃ yat
verses.translation: Even after the Truth has been realised, there remains that strong, beginningless, obstinate impression that one is the agent and experiencer, which is the cause of ones transmigration. It has to be carefully removed by living in a state of constant identification with the Supreme Self. Sages call that Liberation which is the attenuation of Vasanas (impressions) here and now.

verses.sanskrit: अहं ममेति यो भावो देहाक्षादावनात्मनि अध्यासो ऽयं निरस्तव्यो विदुषा स्वात्मनिष्ठया
verses.transliteration: ahaṃ mameti yo bhāvo dehākṣādāvanātmani & adhyāso 'yaṃ nirastavyo viduṣā svātmaniṣṭhayā
verses.translation: The idea of "me and mine" in the body, organs, etc., which are the non-Self this superimposition the wise man must put a stop to, by identifying himself with the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञात्वा स्वं प्रत्यगात्मानं बुद्धितद्वृत्तिसाक्षिणम् सो ऽहम् इत्य् एव सद्वृत्त्यानात्मन्यात्ममतिं जहि
verses.transliteration: jñātvā svaṃ pratyagātmānaṃ buddhitadvṛttisākṣiṇam & so 'ham ity eva sadvṛttyānātmanyātmamatiṃ jahi
verses.translation: Realising thy own Inmost Self, the Witness of the Buddhi and its modifications, and constantly revolving the positive thought, "I am That", conquer this identification with the non-Self.

verses.sanskrit: लोकानुवर्तनं त्यक्त्वा त्यक्त्वा देहानुवर्तनम् शास्त्रानुवर्तनं त्यक्त्वा स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: lokānuvartanaṃ tyaktvā tyaktvā dehānuvartanam & śāstrānuvartanaṃ tyaktvā svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Relinquishing the observance of social formalities, giving up all ideas of trimming up the body, and avoiding too mush engrossment with the Scriptures, do away with the superimposition that has come upon thyself.

verses.sanskrit: लोकवासनया जन्तोः शास्त्रवासनयापि च देहवासनया ज्ञानं यथावन्नैव जायते
verses.transliteration: lokavāsanayā jantoḥ śāstravāsanayāpi ca & dehavāsanayā jñānaṃ yathāvannaiva jāyate
verses.translation: Owing to the desire to run after society, the passion for too much study of the Scriptures and the desire to keep the body in good trim, people cannot attain to proper Realisation.

verses.sanskrit: संसारकारागृहमोक्षमिच्छोर् अयोमयं पादनिबन्धशृङ्खलम् वदन्ति तज्ज्ञाः पटु वासनात्रयं यो ऽस्माद्विमुक्तः समुपैति मुक्तिम्
verses.transliteration: saṃsārakārāgṛhamokṣamicchor ayomayaṃ pādanibandhaśṛṅkhalam & vadanti tajjñāḥ paṭu vāsanātrayaṃ yo 'smādvimuktaḥ samupaiti muktim
verses.translation: For one who seeks deliverance from the prison of this world (Samsara), those three desires have been designated by the wise as strong iron fetters to shackle ones feet. He who is free from them truly attains to Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: जलादिसंसर्गवशात्प्रभूत- दुर्गन्धधूतागरुदिव्यवासना संघर्षणेनैव विभाति सम्यग्- विधूयमाने सति बाह्यगन्धे
verses.transliteration: jalādisaṃsargavaśātprabhūta- durgandhadhūtāgarudivyavāsanā & saṃgharṣaṇenaiva vibhāti samyag- vidhūyamāne sati bāhyagandhe
verses.translation: The lovely odour of the Agaru (agalochum) which is hidden by a powerful stench due to its contact with water etc., manifests itself as soon as the foreign smell has been fully removed by rubbing.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तःश्रितानन्तदूरन्तवासना- धूलीविलिप्ता परम् आत्मवासना प्रज्ञातिसंघर्षणतो विशुद्धा प्रतीयते चन्दनगन्धवत् स्फुटम्
verses.transliteration: antaḥśritānantadūrantavāsanā- dhūlīviliptā param ātmavāsanā & prajñātisaṃgharṣaṇato viśuddhā pratīyate candanagandhavat sphuṭam
verses.translation: Like the fragrance of the sandal-wood, the perfume of the Supreme Self, which is covered with the dust of endless, violent impressions imbedded in the mind, when purified by the constant friction of Knowledge, is (again) clearly perceived.

verses.sanskrit: अनात्मवासनाजालैस्तिरोभूतात्मवासना नित्यात्मनिष्ठया तेषां नाशे भाति स्वयं स्फुटम्
verses.transliteration: anātmavāsanājālaistirobhūtātmavāsanā & nityātmaniṣṭhayā teṣāṃ nāśe bhāti svayaṃ sphuṭam
verses.translation: The desire for Self-realisation is obscured by innumerable desires for things other than the Self. When they have been destroyed by the constant attachment to the Self, the Atman clearly manifests Itself of Its own accord.

verses.sanskrit: यथा यथा प्रत्यगवस्थितं मनः तथा तथा मुञ्चति बाह्यवासनाम् निःशेषमोक्षे सति वासनानां आत्मानुभूतिः प्रतिबन्धशून्या
verses.transliteration: yathā yathā pratyagavasthitaṃ manaḥ tathā tathā muñcati bāhyavāsanām & niḥśeṣamokṣe sati vāsanānāṃ ātmānubhūtiḥ pratibandhaśūnyā
verses.translation: As the mind becomes gradually established in the Inmost Self, it proportionately gives up the desires for external objects. And when all such desires have been eliminated, there takes place the unobstructed realisation of the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: स्वात्मन्य् एव सदा स्थित्वा मनो नश्यति योगिनः वासनानां क्षयश्चातः स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: svātmany eva sadā sthitvā mano naśyati yoginaḥ & vāsanānāṃ kṣayaścātaḥ svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: The Yogis mind dies, being constantly fixed on his own Self. Thence follows the cessation of desires. Therefore do away with thy superimposition.

verses.sanskrit: तमो द्वाभ्यां रजः सत्त्वात्सत्त्वं शुद्धेन नश्यति तस्मात्सत्त्वमवष्टभ्य स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: tamo dvābhyāṃ rajaḥ sattvātsattvaṃ śuddhena naśyati & tasmātsattvamavaṣṭabhya svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Tamas is destroyed by both Sattva and Rajas, Rajas by Sattva, and Sattva dies when purified. Therefore do way with thy superimposition through the help of Sattva.

verses.sanskrit: प्रारब्धं पुष्यति वपुरिति निश्चित्य निश्चलः धैर्यमालम्ब्य यत्नेन स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: prārabdhaṃ puṣyati vapuriti niścitya niścalaḥ & dhairyamālambya yatnena svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Knowing for certain that the Prarabdha work will maintain this body, remain quiet and do away with thy superimposition carefully and with patience.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं जीवः परं ब्रह्मेत्यतद्व्यावृत्तिपूर्वकम् वासनावेगतः प्राप्तस्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ jīvaḥ paraṃ brahmetyatadvyāvṛttipūrvakam & vāsanāvegataḥ prāptasvādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: "I am not the individual soul, but the Supreme Brahman" eliminating thus all that is not-Self, do away with thy superimposition, which has come through the momentum of (past) impressions.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्या युक्त्या स्वानुभूत्या ज्ञात्वा सार्वात्म्यम् आत्मनः क्वचिदाभासतः प्राप्तस्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: śrutyā yuktyā svānubhūtyā jñātvā sārvātmyam ātmanaḥ & kvacidābhāsataḥ prāptasvādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Realising thyself as the Self of all by means of Scripture, reasoning and by thy own realisation, do away thy superimposition, even when a trace of it seems to appear.

verses.sanskrit: अनादानविसर्गाभ्यामीषन्नास्ति क्रिया मुनेः तदेकनिष्ठया नित्यं स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: anādānavisargābhyāmīṣannāsti kriyā muneḥ & tadekaniṣṭhayā nityaṃ svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: The sage has no connection with action, since he has no idea of accepting or giving up. Therefore, through constant engrossment on the Brahman, do away with thy superimposition.

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वमस्यादिवाक्योत्थब्रह्मात्मैकत्वबोधतः ब्रह्मण्य् आत्मत्वदार्ढ्याय स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: tattvamasyādivākyotthabrahmātmaikatvabodhataḥ & brahmaṇy ātmatvadārḍhyāya svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Through the realisation of the identity of Brahman and the soul, resulting from such great dicta as "Thou art That", do away with thy superimposition, with a view to strengthening thy identification with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अहंभावस्य देहे ऽस्मिन्निःशेषविलयावधि सावधानेन युक्तात्मा स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: ahaṃbhāvasya dehe 'sminniḥśeṣavilayāvadhi & sāvadhānena yuktātmā svādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: Until the identification with this body is completely rooted out, do away with thy superimposition with watchfulness and a concentrated mind.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतीतिर्जीवजगतोः स्वप्नवद्भाति यावता तावन्निरन्तरं विद्वन्स्वाध्यासापनयं कुरु
verses.transliteration: pratītirjīvajagatoḥ svapnavadbhāti yāvatā & tāvannirantaraṃ vidvansvādhyāsāpanayaṃ kuru
verses.translation: So long as even a dream-like perception of the universe and souls persists, do away with thy superimposition, O learned man, without the least break.

verses.sanskrit: निद्राया लोकवार्तायाः शब्दादेर् अपि विस्मृतेः क्वचिन्नावसरं दत्त्वा चिन्तयात्मानम् आत्मनि
verses.transliteration: nidrāyā lokavārtāyāḥ śabdāder api vismṛteḥ & kvacinnāvasaraṃ dattvā cintayātmānam ātmani
verses.translation: Without giving the slightest chance to oblivion on account of sleep, concern in secular matters or the sense-objects, reflect on the Self in thy mind.

verses.sanskrit: मातापित्रोर् मलोद्भूतं मलमांसमयं वपुः त्यक्त्वा चाण्डालवद्दूरं ब्रह्मीभूय कृती भव
verses.transliteration: mātāpitror malodbhūtaṃ malamāṃsamayaṃ vapuḥ & tyaktvā cāṇḍālavaddūraṃ brahmībhūya kṛtī bhava
verses.translation: Shunning from a safe distance the body which has come from impurities of the parents and itself consists of flesh and impurities as one does an outcast be thou Brahman and realise the consummation of thy life.

verses.sanskrit: घटाकाशं महाकाश इवात्मानं परात्मनि विलाप्याखण्डभावेन तूष्णी भव सदा मुने
verses.transliteration: ghaṭākāśaṃ mahākāśa ivātmānaṃ parātmani & vilāpyākhaṇḍabhāvena tūṣṇī bhava sadā mune
verses.translation: Merging the finite soul in the Supreme Self, like the space enclosed by a jar in the infinite space, by means of meditation on their identity, always keep quiet, O sage.

verses.sanskrit: स्वप्रकाशमधिष्ठानं स्वयंभूय सदात्मना ब्रह्माण्डम् अपि पिण्डाण्डं त्यज्यतां मलभाण्डवत्
verses.transliteration: svaprakāśamadhiṣṭhānaṃ svayaṃbhūya sadātmanā & brahmāṇḍam api piṇḍāṇḍaṃ tyajyatāṃ malabhāṇḍavat
verses.translation: Becoming thyself the self-effulgent Brahman, the substratum of all phenomena as that Reality give up both the macrocosm and the microcosm, like two filthy receptacles.

verses.sanskrit: चिदात्मनि सदानन्दे देहारूढामहंधियम् निवेश्य लिङ्गमुत्सृज्य केवलो भव सर्वदा
verses.transliteration: cidātmani sadānande dehārūḍhāmahaṃdhiyam & niveśya liṅgamutsṛjya kevalo bhava sarvadā
verses.translation: Transferring the identification now rooted in the body to the Atman, the Existence-Knowledge-Bliss Absolute, and discarding the subtle body, be thou ever alone, independent.

verses.sanskrit: यत्रैष जगदाभासो दर्पणान्तः पुरं यथा तद्ब्रह्माहमिति ज्ञात्वा कृतकृत्यो भविष्यसि
verses.transliteration: yatraiṣa jagadābhāso darpaṇāntaḥ puraṃ yathā & tadbrahmāhamiti jñātvā kṛtakṛtyo bhaviṣyasi
verses.translation: That in which there is this reflection of the universe, as of a city in a mirror that Brahman art thou; knowing this thou wilt attain the consummation of thy life.

verses.sanskrit: यत्सत्यभूतं निजरूपमाद्यं चिदद्वयानन्दमरूपमक्रियम् तदेत्य मिथ्यावपुरुत्सृजेत शैलूषवद्वेषमुपात्तमात्मनः
verses.transliteration: yatsatyabhūtaṃ nijarūpamādyaṃ cidadvayānandamarūpamakriyam & tadetya mithyāvapurutsṛjeta śailūṣavadveṣamupāttamātmanaḥ
verses.translation: That which is real and ones own primeval Essence, that Knowledge and Bliss Absolute, the One without a second, which is beyond form and activity attaining That one should cease to identify oneself with ones false bodies, like an actor giving up his assumed mask.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मना दृश्यमिदं मृषैव नैवाहमर्थः क्षणिकत्वदर्शनात् जानाम्यहं सर्वमिति प्रतीतिः कुतो ऽहम् आदेः क्षणिकस्य सिध्येत्
verses.transliteration: sarvātmanā dṛśyamidaṃ mṛṣaiva naivāhamarthaḥ kṣaṇikatvadarśanāt & jānāmyahaṃ sarvamiti pratītiḥ kuto 'ham ādeḥ kṣaṇikasya sidhyet
verses.translation: This objective universe is absolutely unreal; neither is egoism a reality, for it is observed to be momentary. How can the perception, "I know all", be true of egoism etc., which are momentary ?

verses.sanskrit: अहंपदार्थस्त्वहमादिसाक्षी नित्यं सुषुप्ताव् अपि भावदर्शनात् ब्रूते ह्यजो नित्य इति श्रुतिः स्वयं तत्प्रत्यगात्मा सदसद्विलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: ahaṃpadārthastvahamādisākṣī nityaṃ suṣuptāv api bhāvadarśanāt & brūte hyajo nitya iti śrutiḥ svayaṃ tatpratyagātmā sadasadvilakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: But the real I" is that which witnesses the ego and the rest. It exists always, even in the state of profound sleep. The Shruti itself says, "It is birthless, eternal", etc. Therefore the Paramatman is different from the gross and subtle bodies.

verses.sanskrit: विकारिणां सर्वविकारवेत्ता नित्याविकारो भवितुं समर्हति मनोरथस्वप्नसुषुप्तिषु स्फुटं पुनः पुनर्दृष्टमसत्त्वमेतयोः
verses.transliteration: vikāriṇāṃ sarvavikāravettā nityāvikāro bhavituṃ samarhati & manorathasvapnasuṣuptiṣu sphuṭaṃ punaḥ punardṛṣṭamasattvametayoḥ
verses.translation: The knower of all changes in things subject to change should necessarily be eternal and changeless. The unreality of the gross and subtle bodies is again and again clearly observed in imagination, dream and profound sleep.

verses.sanskrit: अतो ऽभिमानं त्यज मांसपिण्डे पिण्डाभिमानिन्य् अपि बुद्धिकल्पिते कालत्रयाबाध्यमखण्डबोधं ज्ञात्वा स्वमात्मानमुपैहि शान्तिम्
verses.transliteration: ato 'bhimānaṃ tyaja māṃsapiṇḍe piṇḍābhimāniny api buddhikalpite & kālatrayābādhyamakhaṇḍabodhaṃ jñātvā svamātmānamupaihi śāntim
verses.translation: Therefore give up the identification with this lump of flesh, the gross body, as well as with the ego or the subtle body, which are both imagined by the Buddhi. Realising thy own Self, which is Knowledge Absolute and not to be denied in the past, present or future, attain to Peace.

verses.sanskrit: त्यजाभिमानं कुलगोत्रनाम- रूपाश्रमेष्वार्द्रशवाश्रितेषु लिङ्गस्य धर्मान् अपि कर्तृतादिंस् त्यक्ता भवाखण्डसुखस्वरूपः
verses.transliteration: tyajābhimānaṃ kulagotranāma- rūpāśrameṣvārdraśavāśriteṣu & liṅgasya dharmān api kartṛtādiṃs tyaktā bhavākhaṇḍasukhasvarūpaḥ
verses.translation: Cease to identify thyself with the family, lineage, name, form and the order of life, which pertain to the body that is like a rotten corpse (to a man of realisation). Similarly, giving up ideas of agency and so forth, which are attributes of the subtle body, be the Essence of Bliss Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: सन्त्यन्ये प्रतिबन्धाः पुंसः संसारहेतवो दृष्टाः तेषाम् एवं मूलं प्रथमविकारो भवत्यहंकारः
verses.transliteration: santyanye pratibandhāḥ puṃsaḥ saṃsārahetavo dṛṣṭāḥ & teṣām evaṃ mūlaṃ prathamavikāro bhavatyahaṃkāraḥ
verses.translation: Other obstacles are also observed to exist for men, which lead to transmigration. The root of them, for the above reasons, is the first modification of Nescience called egoism.

verses.sanskrit: यावत्स्यात्स्वस्य संबन्धो ऽहंकारेण दुरात्मना तावन् न लेशमात्रापि मुक्तिवार्ता विलक्षणा
verses.transliteration: yāvatsyātsvasya saṃbandho 'haṃkāreṇa durātmanā & tāvan na leśamātrāpi muktivārtā vilakṣaṇā
verses.translation: So long as one has any relation to this wicked ego, there should not be the least talk about Liberation, which is unique.

verses.sanskrit: अहंकारग्रहान्मुक्तः स्वरूपमुपपद्यते चन्द्रवद्विमलः पूर्णः सदानन्दः स्वयंप्रभः
verses.transliteration: ahaṃkāragrahānmuktaḥ svarūpamupapadyate & candravadvimalaḥ pūrṇaḥ sadānandaḥ svayaṃprabhaḥ
verses.translation: Freed from the clutches of egoism, as the moon from those of Rahu, man attains to his real nature, and becomes pure, infinite, ever blissful and self-luminous.

verses.sanskrit: यो वा पुरे सो ऽहम् इति प्रतीतो बुद्ध्या प्रकॢप्तस्तमसातिमूढया तस्यैव निःशेषतया विनाशे ब्रह्मात्मभावः प्रतिबन्धशून्यः
verses.transliteration: yo vā pure so 'ham iti pratīto buddhyā prakḷptastamasātimūḍhayā & tasyaiva niḥśeṣatayā vināśe brahmātmabhāvaḥ pratibandhaśūnyaḥ
verses.translation: When the ego created by an intellect deluded by ignorance—the bodily notion “I am such and such”—is completely destroyed, unobstructed identity with Brahman is realized.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मानन्दनिधिर्महाबलवताहंकारघोराहिना संवेष्ट्य् आत्मनि रक्ष्यते गुणमयैश्चण्डेस्त्रिभिर्मस्तकैः विज्ञानाख्यमहासिना श्रुतिमता विच्छिद्य शीर्षत्रयं निर्मूल्याहिमिमं निधिं सुखकरं धीरो ऽनुभोक्तुंक्षमः
verses.transliteration: brahmānandanidhirmahābalavatāhaṃkāraghorāhinā saṃveṣṭy ātmani rakṣyate guṇamayaiścaṇḍestribhirmastakaiḥ & vijñānākhyamahāsinā śrutimatā vicchidya śīrṣatrayaṃ nirmūlyāhimimaṃ nidhiṃ sukhakaraṃ dhīro 'nubhoktuṃkṣamaḥ
verses.translation: The treasure of the Bliss of Brahman is coiled round by the mighty and dreadful serpent of egoism, and guarded for its own use by means of its three fierce hoods consisting of the three Gunas. Only the wise man, destroying it by severing its three hoods with the great sword of realisation in accordance with the teachings of the Shrutis, can enjoy this treasure which confers bliss.

verses.sanskrit: यावद्वा यत् किंचिद् विषदोषस्फूर्तिरस्ति चेद्देहे कथम् आरोग्याय भवेत्तद्वदहन्तापि योगिनो मुक्त्यै
verses.transliteration: yāvadvā yat kiṃcid viṣadoṣasphūrtirasti ceddehe & katham ārogyāya bhavettadvadahantāpi yogino muktyai
verses.translation: As long as there is a trace of poisoning left in the body, how can one hope for recovery ? Similar is the effect of egoism on the Yogis Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: अहमो ऽत्यन्तनिवृत्त्या तत्कृतनानाविकल्पसंहृत्या प्रत्यक्तत्त्वविवेकादिदमहमस्मीति विन्दते तत्त्वम्
verses.transliteration: ahamo 'tyantanivṛttyā tatkṛtanānāvikalpasaṃhṛtyā & pratyaktattvavivekādidamahamasmīti vindate tattvam
verses.translation: Through the complete cessation of egoism, through the stoppage of the diverse mental waves due to it, and through the discrimination of the inner Reality, one realises that Reality as "I am This".

verses.sanskrit: अहंकारे कर्तर्यहमिति मतिं मुञ्च सहसा विकारात्मन्य् आत्मप्रतिफलजुषि स्वस्थितिमुषि यदध्यासात्प्राप्ता जनिमृतिजरादुःखबहुला प्रतीचश्चिन्मूर्तेस्तव सुखतनोः संसृतिरियम्
verses.transliteration: ahaṃkāre kartaryahamiti matiṃ muñca sahasā vikārātmany ātmapratiphalajuṣi svasthitimuṣi & yadadhyāsātprāptā janimṛtijarāduḥkhabahulā pratīcaścinmūrtestava sukhatanoḥ saṃsṛtiriyam
verses.translation: Give up immediately thy identification with egoism, the agent, which is by its nature a modification, is endued with a reflection of the Self, and diverts one from being established in the Self identifying thyself with which thou hast come by this relative existence, full of the miseries of birth, decay and death, though thou art the Witness, the Essence of Knowledge and Bliss Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: सदैकरूपस्य चिदात्मनो विभोर् आनन्दमूर्तेरनवद्यकीर्तेः नैवान्यथा क्वाप्यविकारिणस्ते विनाहमध्यासममुष्य संसृतिः
verses.transliteration: sadaikarūpasya cidātmano vibhor ānandamūrteranavadyakīrteḥ & naivānyathā kvāpyavikāriṇaste vināhamadhyāsamamuṣya saṃsṛtiḥ
