verses.translation: But for thy identification with that egoism there can never be any transmigration for thee who art immutable and eternally the same, the Knowledge Absolute, omnipresent, the Bliss Absolute, and of untarnished glory.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादहंकारमिमं स्वशत्रुं भोक्तुर्गले कण्टकवत्प्रतीतम् विच्छिद्य विज्ञानमहासिना स्फुटं भुङ्क्ष्वात्मसाम्राज्यसुखं यथेष्टम्
verses.transliteration: tasmādahaṃkāramimaṃ svaśatruṃ bhokturgale kaṇṭakavatpratītam & vicchidya vijñānamahāsinā sphuṭaṃ bhuṅkṣvātmasāmrājyasukhaṃ yatheṣṭam
verses.translation: Therefore destroying this egoism, thy enemy - which appears like a thorn sticking in the throat of a man taking meal with the great sword of realisation, enjoy directly and freely the bliss of thy own empire, the majesty of the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽहम् आदेर्विनिवर्त्य वृत्तिं संत्यक्तरागः परमार्थलाभात् तूष्णीं समास्स्वात्मसुखानुभूत्या पूर्णात्मना ब्रह्मणि निर्विकल्पः
verses.transliteration: tato 'ham ādervinivartya vṛttiṃ saṃtyaktarāgaḥ paramārthalābhāt & tūṣṇīṃ samāssvātmasukhānubhūtyā pūrṇātmanā brahmaṇi nirvikalpaḥ
verses.translation: Checking the activities of egoism etc., and giving up all attachment through the realisation of the Supreme Reality, be free from all duality through the enjoyment of the Bliss of Self, and remain quiet in Brahman, for thou hast attained thy infinite nature.

verses.sanskrit: समूलकृत्तो ऽपि महानहं पुनः व्युल्लेखितः स्याद्यदि चेतसा क्षणम् संजीव्य विक्षेपशतं करोति नभस्वता प्रावृषि वारिदो यथा
verses.transliteration: samūlakṛtto 'pi mahānahaṃ punaḥ vyullekhitaḥ syādyadi cetasā kṣaṇam & saṃjīvya vikṣepaśataṃ karoti nabhasvatā prāvṛṣi vārido yathā
verses.translation: Even though completely rooted out, this terrible egoism, if revolved in the mind even for a moment, returns to life and creates hundreds of mischiefs, like a cloud ushered in by the wind during the rainy season.

verses.sanskrit: निगृह्य शत्रोरहमो ऽवकाशः क्वचिन् न देयो विषयानुचिन्तया स एव संजीवनहेतुरस्य प्रक्षीणजम्बीरतरोर् इवाम्बु
verses.transliteration: nigṛhya śatrorahamo 'vakāśaḥ kvacin na deyo viṣayānucintayā & sa eva saṃjīvanaheturasya prakṣīṇajambīrataror ivāmbu
verses.translation: Overpowering this enemy, egoism, not a moments respite should be given to it by thinking on the sense-objects. That is verily the cause of its coming back to life, like water to a citron tree that has almost dried up.

verses.sanskrit: देहात्मना संस्थित एव कामी विलक्षणः कामयिता कथं स्यात् अतो ऽर्थसन्धानपरत्वम् एव भेदप्रसक्त्या भवबन्धहेतुः
verses.transliteration: dehātmanā saṃsthita eva kāmī vilakṣaṇaḥ kāmayitā kathaṃ syāt & ato 'rthasandhānaparatvam eva bhedaprasaktyā bhavabandhahetuḥ
verses.translation: He alone who has identified himself with the body is greedy after sense-pleasures. How can one, devoid of the body-idea, be greedy (like him) ? Hence the tendency to think on the sense-objects is verily the cause of the bondage of transmigration, giving rise to an idea of distinction or duality.

verses.sanskrit: कार्यप्रवर्धनाद्बीजप्रवृद्धिः परिदृश्यते कार्यनाशाद्बीजनाशस्तस्मात्कार्यं निरोधयेत्
verses.transliteration: kāryapravardhanādbījapravṛddhiḥ paridṛśyate & kāryanāśādbījanāśastasmātkāryaṃ nirodhayet
verses.translation: When the effects are developed, the seed also is observed to be such, and when the effects are destroyed, the seed also is seen to be destroyed. Therefore one must subdue the effects.

verses.sanskrit: वासनावृद्धितः कार्यं कार्यवृद्ध्या च वासना वर्धते सर्वथा पुंसः संसारो न निवर्तते
verses.transliteration: vāsanāvṛddhitaḥ kāryaṃ kāryavṛddhyā ca vāsanā & vardhate sarvathā puṃsaḥ saṃsāro na nivartate
verses.translation: Through the increase of desires selfish work increases, and when there is an increase of selfish work, there is an increase of desire also. And mans transmigration is never at an end.

verses.sanskrit: संसारबन्धविच्छित्त्यै तद् द्वयं प्रदहेद्यतिः वासनावृद्धिरेताभ्यां चिन्तया क्रियया बहिः
verses.transliteration: saṃsārabandhavicchittyai tad dvayaṃ pradahedyatiḥ & vāsanāvṛddhiretābhyāṃ cintayā kriyayā bahiḥ
verses.translation: For the sake of breaking the chain of transmigration, the Sannyasin should burn to ashes those two; for thinking of the sense-objects and doing selfish acts lead to an increase of desires.

verses.sanskrit: ताभ्यां प्रवर्धमाना सा सूते संसृतिम् आत्मनः त्रयाणां च क्षयोपायः सर्वावस्थासु सर्वदा
verses.transliteration: tābhyāṃ pravardhamānā sā sūte saṃsṛtim ātmanaḥ & trayāṇāṃ ca kṣayopāyaḥ sarvāvasthāsu sarvadā
verses.translation: Augmented by these two, desires produce ones transmigration. The way to destroy these three, however, lies in looking upon everything, under all circumstances, always, everywhere and in all respects, as Brahman and Brahman alone. Through the strengthening of the longing to be one with Brahman, those three are annihilated.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वत्र सर्वतः सर्वब्रह्ममात्रावलोकनैः सद्भाववासनादार्ढ्यात्तत्त्रयं लयमश्नुते
verses.transliteration: sarvatra sarvataḥ sarvabrahmamātrāvalokanaiḥ & sadbhāvavāsanādārḍhyāttattrayaṃ layamaśnute
verses.translation: Augmented by these two, desires produce ones transmigration. The way to destroy these three, however, lies in looking upon everything, under all circumstances, always, everywhere and in all respects, as Brahman and Brahman alone. Through the strengthening of the longing to be one with Brahman, those three are annihilated.

verses.sanskrit: क्रियानाशे भवेच्चिन्तानाशो ऽस्माद्वासनाक्षयः वासनाप्रक्षयो मोक्षः सा जीवन्मुक्तिरिष्यते
verses.transliteration: kriyānāśe bhaveccintānāśo 'smādvāsanākṣayaḥ & vāsanāprakṣayo mokṣaḥ sā jīvanmuktiriṣyate
verses.translation: With the cessation of selfish action the brooding on the sense-objects is stopped, which is followed by the destruction of desires. The destruction of desires is Liberation, and this is considered as Liberation-in-life

verses.sanskrit: सद्वासनास्फूर्तिविजृम्भणे सति ह्यसौ विलीनाप्यहमादिवासना अतिप्रकृष्टाप्यरुणप्रभायां विलीयते साधु यथा तमिस्रा
verses.transliteration: sadvāsanāsphūrtivijṛmbhaṇe sati hyasau vilīnāpyahamādivāsanā & atiprakṛṣṭāpyaruṇaprabhāyāṃ vilīyate sādhu yathā tamisrā
verses.translation: When the desire for realising Brahman has a marked manifestation, the egoistic desires readily vanish, as the most intense darkness effectively vanishes before the glow of the rising sun.

verses.sanskrit: तमस्तमःकार्यमनर्थजालं न दृश्यते सत्युदिते दिनेशे तथाद्वयानन्दरसानुभूतौ न वास्ति बन्धो न च दुःखगन्धः
verses.transliteration: tamastamaḥkāryamanarthajālaṃ na dṛśyate satyudite dineśe & tathādvayānandarasānubhūtau na vāsti bandho na ca duḥkhagandhaḥ
verses.translation: Darkness and the numerous evils that attend on it are not noticed when the sun rises. Similarly, on the realisation of the Bliss Absolute, there is neither bondage nor the least trace of misery.

verses.sanskrit: दृश्यं प्रतीतं प्रविलापयन्सन् सन्मात्रमानन्दघनं विभावयन् समाहितः सन्बहिरन्तरं वा कालं नयेथाः सति कर्मबन्धे
verses.transliteration: dṛśyaṃ pratītaṃ pravilāpayansan sanmātramānandaghanaṃ vibhāvayan & samāhitaḥ sanbahirantaraṃ vā kālaṃ nayethāḥ sati karmabandhe
verses.translation: Causing the external and internal universe, which are now perceived, to vanish, and meditating on the Reality, the Bliss Embodied, one should pass ones time watchfully, if there be any residue of Prarabdha work left.

verses.sanskrit: प्रमादो ब्रह्मनिष्ठायां न कर्तव्यः कदाचन प्रमादो मृत्युरित्याह भगवान्ब्रह्मणः सुतः
verses.transliteration: pramādo brahmaniṣṭhāyāṃ na kartavyaḥ kadācana & pramādo mṛtyurityāha bhagavānbrahmaṇaḥ sutaḥ
verses.translation: One should never be careless in ones steadfastness to Brahman. Bhagavan Sanatkumara, who is Brahmas son, has called inadvertence to be death itself.

verses.sanskrit: न प्रमादादनर्थो ऽन्यो ज्ञानिनः स्वस्वरूपतः ततो मोहस्ततो ऽहंधीस्ततो बन्धस्ततो व्यथा
verses.transliteration: na pramādādanartho 'nyo jñāninaḥ svasvarūpataḥ & tato mohastato 'haṃdhīstato bandhastato vyathā
verses.translation: There is no greater danger for the Jnanin than carelessness about his own real nature. From this comes delusion, thence egoism, this is followed by bondage, and then comes misery.

verses.sanskrit: विषयाभिमुखं दृष्ट्वा विद्वांसम् अपि विस्मृतिः विक्षेपयति धीदोषैर् योषा जारम् इव प्रियम्
verses.transliteration: viṣayābhimukhaṃ dṛṣṭvā vidvāṃsam api vismṛtiḥ & vikṣepayati dhīdoṣair yoṣā jāram iva priyam
verses.translation: Finding even a wise man hankering after the sense-objects, oblivion torments him through the evil propensities of the Buddhi, as a woman does her doting paramour.

verses.sanskrit: यथापकृष्टं शैवालं क्षणमात्रं न तिष्ठति आवृणोति तथा माया प्राज्ञं वापि पराङ्मुखम्
verses.transliteration: yathāpakṛṣṭaṃ śaivālaṃ kṣaṇamātraṃ na tiṣṭhati & āvṛṇoti tathā māyā prājñaṃ vāpi parāṅmukham
verses.translation: As sedge, even if removed, does not stay away for a moment, but covers the water again, so Maya or Nescience also covers even a wise man, if he is averse to meditation on the Self.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्यच्युतं चेद्यदि चित्तमीषद् बहिर्मुखं सन्निपतेत्ततस्ततः प्रमादतः प्रच्युतकेलिकन्दुकः सोपानपङ्क्तौ पतितो यथा तथा
verses.transliteration: lakṣyacyutaṃ cedyadi cittamīṣad bahirmukhaṃ sannipatettatastataḥ & pramādataḥ pracyutakelikandukaḥ sopānapaṅktau patito yathā tathā
verses.translation: If the mind ever so slightly strays from the Ideal and becomes outgoing, then it goes down and down, just as a play-ball inadvertently dropped on the staircase bounds down from one step to another.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेष्वाविशच्चेतः संकल्पयति तद्गुणान् सम्यक्संकल्पनात्कामः कामात्पुंसः प्रवर्तनम्
verses.transliteration: viṣayeṣvāviśaccetaḥ saṃkalpayati tadguṇān & samyaksaṃkalpanātkāmaḥ kāmātpuṃsaḥ pravartanam
verses.translation: The mind that is attached to the sense-objects reflects on their qualities; from mature reflection arises desire, and after desiring a man sets about having that thing.

verses.sanskrit: अतः प्रमादान् न परो ऽस्ति मृत्युः विवेकिनो ब्रह्मविदः समाधौ समाहितः सिद्धिमुपैति सम्यक् समाहितात्मा भव सावधानः
verses.transliteration: ataḥ pramādān na paro 'sti mṛtyuḥ vivekino brahmavidaḥ samādhau & samāhitaḥ siddhimupaiti samyak samāhitātmā bhava sāvadhānaḥ
verses.translation: Hence to the discriminating knower of Brahman there is no worse death than inadvertence with regard to concentration. But the man who is concentrated attains complete success. (Therefore) carefully concentrate thy mind (on Brahman).

verses.sanskrit: ततः स्वरूपविभ्रंशो विभ्रष्टस्तु पतत्यधः पतितस्य विना नाशं पुनर्नारोह ईक्ष्यते
verses.transliteration: tataḥ svarūpavibhraṃśo vibhraṣṭastu patatyadhaḥ & patitasya vinā nāśaṃ punarnāroha īkṣyate
verses.translation: Through inadvertence a man deviates from his real nature, and the man who has thus deviated falls. The fallen man comes to ruin, and is scarcely seen to rise again.

verses.sanskrit: संकल्पं वर्जयेत्तस्मात्सर्वानर्थस्य कारणम् जीवतो यस्य कैवल्यं विदेहे स च केवलः \ यत् किंचित् पश्यतो भेदं भयं ब्रूते यजुःश्रुतिः
verses.transliteration: saṃkalpaṃ varjayettasmātsarvānarthasya kāraṇam & jīvato yasya kaivalyaṃ videhe sa ca kevalaḥ \ yat kiṃcit paśyato bhedaṃ bhayaṃ brūte yajuḥśrutiḥ
verses.translation: Therefore one should give up reflecting on the sense-objects, which is the root of all mischief. He who is completely aloof even while living, is alone aloof after the dissolution of the body. The Yajur-Veda declares that there is fear for one who sees the least bit of distinction.

verses.sanskrit: यदा कदा वापि विपश्चिदेष ब्रह्मण्य् अनन्ते ऽप्य् अणुमात्रभेदम् पश्यत्यथामुष्य भयं तदैव यद्वीक्षितं भिन्नतया प्रमादात्
verses.transliteration: yadā kadā vāpi vipaścideṣa brahmaṇy anante 'py aṇumātrabhedam & paśyatyathāmuṣya bhayaṃ tadaiva yadvīkṣitaṃ bhinnatayā pramādāt
verses.translation: Whenever the wise man sees the least difference in the infinite Brahman, at once that which he sees as different through mistake, becomes a source of terror to him.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतिस्मृतिन्यायशतैर् निषिद्धे दृश्ये ऽत्र यः स्वात्ममतिं करोति उपैति दुःखोपरि दुःखजातं निषिद्धकर्ता स मलिम्लुचो यथा
verses.transliteration: śrutismṛtinyāyaśatair niṣiddhe dṛśye 'tra yaḥ svātmamatiṃ karoti & upaiti duḥkhopari duḥkhajātaṃ niṣiddhakartā sa malimluco yathā
verses.translation: He who identifies himself with the objective universe which has been denied by hundreds of Shrutis, Smritis and reasonings, experiences misery after misery, like a thief, for he does something forbidden.

verses.sanskrit: सत्याभिसंधानरतो विमुक्तो महत्त्वमात्मीयमुपैति नित्यम् मिथ्याभिसन्धानरतस्तु नश्येद् दृष्टं तदेतद्यदचौरचौरयोः
verses.transliteration: satyābhisaṃdhānarato vimukto mahattvamātmīyamupaiti nityam & mithyābhisandhānaratastu naśyed dṛṣṭaṃ tadetadyadacauracaurayoḥ
verses.translation: He who has devoted himself to meditation on the Reality (Brahman) and is free from Nescience, attains to the eternal glory of the Atman. But he who dwells on the unreal (the universe) is destroyed. That this is so is evidenced in the case of one who is not a thief and one who is a thief.

verses.sanskrit: यतिरसदनुसन्धिं बन्धहेतुं विहाय स्वयमयमहमस्मीत्य् आत्मदृष्ट्यैव तिष्ठेत् सुखयति ननु निष्ठा ब्रह्मणि स्वानुभूत्या हरति परमविद्याकार्यदुःखं प्रतीतम्
verses.transliteration: yatirasadanusandhiṃ bandhahetuṃ vihāya svayamayamahamasmīty ātmadṛṣṭyaiva tiṣṭhet & sukhayati nanu niṣṭhā brahmaṇi svānubhūtyā harati paramavidyākāryaduḥkhaṃ pratītam
verses.translation: The Sannyasin should give up dwelling on the unreal, which causes bondage, and should always fix his thoughts on the Atman as "I myself am This". For the steadfastness in Brahman through the realisation of ones identity with It gives rise to bliss and thoroughly removes the misery born of nescience, which one experiences (in the ignorant state).

verses.sanskrit: बाह्यानुसन्धिः परिवर्धयेत्फलं दुर्वासनाम् एव ततस्ततो ऽधिकाम् ज्ञात्वा विवेकैः परिहृत्य बाह्यं स्वात्मानुसन्धिं विदधीत नित्यम्
verses.transliteration: bāhyānusandhiḥ parivardhayetphalaṃ durvāsanām eva tatastato 'dhikām & jñātvā vivekaiḥ parihṛtya bāhyaṃ svātmānusandhiṃ vidadhīta nityam
verses.translation: The dwelling on external objects will only intensify its fruits, viz. furthering evil propensities, which grow worse and worse. Knowing this through discrimination, one should avoid external objects and constantly apply oneself to meditation on the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: बाह्ये निरुद्धे मनसः प्रसन्नता मनःप्रसादे परमात्मदर्शनम् तस्मिन्सुदृष्टे भवबन्धनाशो बहिर्निरोधः पदवी विमुक्तेः
verses.transliteration: bāhye niruddhe manasaḥ prasannatā manaḥprasāde paramātmadarśanam & tasminsudṛṣṭe bhavabandhanāśo bahirnirodhaḥ padavī vimukteḥ
verses.translation: When the external world is shut out, the mind is cheerful, and cheerfulness of the mind brings on the vision of the Paramatman. When It is perfectly realised, the chain of birth and death is broken. Hence the shutting out of the external world is the stepping-stone to Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: कः पण्डितः सन्सदसद्विवेकी श्रुतिप्रमाणः परमार्थदर्शी जानन्हि कुर्यादसतो ऽवलम्बं स्वपातहेतोः शिशुवन्मुमुक्षुः
verses.transliteration: kaḥ paṇḍitaḥ sansadasadvivekī śrutipramāṇaḥ paramārthadarśī & jānanhi kuryādasato 'valambaṃ svapātahetoḥ śiśuvanmumukṣuḥ
verses.translation: Where is the man who being learned, able to discriminate the real from the unreal, believing the Vedas as authority, fixing his gaze on the Atman, the Supreme Reality, and being a seeker after Liberation, will, like a child, consciously have recourse to the unreal (the universe) which will cause his fall ?

verses.sanskrit: देहादिसंसक्तिमतो न मुक्तिः मुक्तस्य देहाद्यभिमत्यभावः सुप्तस्य नो जागरणं न जाग्रतः स्वप्नस्तयोर् भिन्नगुणाश्रयत्वात्
verses.transliteration: dehādisaṃsaktimato na muktiḥ muktasya dehādyabhimatyabhāvaḥ & suptasya no jāgaraṇaṃ na jāgrataḥ svapnastayor bhinnaguṇāśrayatvāt
verses.translation: There is no Liberation for one who has attachment to the body etc., and the liberated man has no identification with the body etc. The sleeping man is not awake, nor is the waking man asleep, for these two states are contradictory in nature.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तर्बहिः स्वं स्थिरजङ्गमेषु ज्ञात्वात्मनाधारतया विलोक्य त्यक्ताखिलोपाधिरखण्डरूपः पूर्णात्मना यः स्थित एष मुक्तः
verses.transliteration: antarbahiḥ svaṃ sthirajaṅgameṣu jñātvātmanādhāratayā vilokya & tyaktākhilopādhirakhaṇḍarūpaḥ pūrṇātmanā yaḥ sthita eṣa muktaḥ
verses.translation: He is free who, knowing through his mind the Self in moving and unmoving objects and observing It as their substratum, gives up all superimpositions and remains as the Absolute and the infinite Self.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मना बन्धविमुक्तिहेतुः सर्वात्मभावान् न परो ऽस्ति कश्चित् दृश्याग्रहे सत्युपपद्यते ऽसौ सर्वात्मभावो ऽस्य सदात्मनिष्ठया
verses.transliteration: sarvātmanā bandhavimuktihetuḥ sarvātmabhāvān na paro 'sti kaścit & dṛśyāgrahe satyupapadyate 'sau sarvātmabhāvo 'sya sadātmaniṣṭhayā
verses.translation: To realise the whole universe as the Self is the means of getting rid of bondage. There is nothing higher than identifying the universe with the Self. One realises this state by excluding the objective world through steadfastness in the eternal Atman.

verses.sanskrit: दृश्यस्याग्रहणं कथं नु घटते देहात्मना तिष्ठतो बाह्यार्थानुभवप्रसक्तमनसस्तत्तत्क्रियां कुर्वतः संन्यस्ताखिल-धर्म-कर्म-विषयैर् नित्यात्मनिष्ठापरैः तत्त्वज्ञैः करणीयम् आत्मनि सदानन्देच्छुभिर्यत्नतः
verses.transliteration: dṛśyasyāgrahaṇaṃ kathaṃ nu ghaṭate dehātmanā tiṣṭhato bāhyārthānubhavaprasaktamanasastattatkriyāṃ kurvataḥ & saṃnyastākhila-dharma-karma-viṣayair nityātmaniṣṭhāparaiḥ tattvajñaiḥ karaṇīyam ātmani sadānandecchubhiryatnataḥ
verses.translation: How is the exclusion of the objective world possible for one who lives identified with the body, whose mind is attached to the perception of external objects, and who performs various acts for that end ? This exclusion should be carefully practised by sages who have renounced all kinds of duties and actions and objects, who are passionately devoted to the eternal Atman, and who wish to possess an undying bliss.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मसिद्धये भिक्षोः कृतश्रवणकर्मणः समाधिं विदधात्येषा शान्तो दान्त इति श्रुतिः
verses.transliteration: sarvātmasiddhaye bhikṣoḥ kṛtaśravaṇakarmaṇaḥ & samādhiṃ vidadhātyeṣā śānto dānta iti śrutiḥ
verses.translation: To the Sannyasin who has gone through the act of hearing, the Shruti passage, "Calm, self-controlled." Etc., prescribes Samadhi for realising the identity of the universe with the Self.

verses.sanskrit: आरूढशक्तेरहमो विनाशः कर्तुन् न शक्य सहसापि पण्डितैः ये निर्विकल्पाख्यसमाधिनिश्चलाः तानन्तरानन्तभवा हि वासनाः
verses.transliteration: ārūḍhaśakterahamo vināśaḥ kartun na śakya sahasāpi paṇḍitaiḥ & ye nirvikalpākhyasamādhiniścalāḥ tānantarānantabhavā hi vāsanāḥ
verses.translation: Even wise men cannot suddenly destroy egoism after it has once become strong, barring those who are perfectly calm through the Nirvikalpa Samadhi. Desires are verily the effect of innumerable births.

verses.sanskrit: अहंबुद्ध्यैव मोहिन्या योजयित्वावृतेर्बलात् विक्षेपशक्तिः पुरुषं विक्षेपयति तद्गुणैः
verses.transliteration: ahaṃbuddhyaiva mohinyā yojayitvāvṛterbalāt & vikṣepaśaktiḥ puruṣaṃ vikṣepayati tadguṇaiḥ
verses.translation: The projecting power, through the aid of the veiling power, connects a man with the siren of an egoistic idea, and distracts him through the attributes of that.

verses.sanskrit: विक्षेपशक्तिविजयो विषमो विधातुं निःशेषमावरणशक्तिनिवृत्त्यभावे दृग्दृश्ययोः स्फुटपयोजलवद्विभागे नश्येत्तदावरणम् आत्मनि च स्वभावात् \ निःसंशयेन भवति प्रतिबन्धशून्यो विक्षेपणं नहिं तदा यदि चेन्मृषार्थे
verses.transliteration: vikṣepaśaktivijayo viṣamo vidhātuṃ niḥśeṣamāvaraṇaśaktinivṛttyabhāve & dṛgdṛśyayoḥ sphuṭapayojalavadvibhāge naśyettadāvaraṇam ātmani ca svabhāvāt \ niḥsaṃśayena bhavati pratibandhaśūnyo vikṣepaṇaṃ nahiṃ tadā yadi cenmṛṣārthe
verses.translation: It is extremely difficult to conquer the projecting power unless the veiling power is perfectly rooted out. And that covering over the Atman naturally vanishes when the subject is perfectly distinguished from the objects, like milk from water. But the victory is undoubtedly (complete and) free from obstacles when there is no oscillation of the mind due to the unreal sense-objects.

verses.sanskrit: सम्यग्विवेकः स्फुटबोधजन्यो विभज्य दृग्दृश्यपदार्थतत्त्वम् छिनत्ति मायाकृतमोहबन्धं यस्माद्विमुक्तस्तु पुनर्न संसृतिः
verses.transliteration: samyagvivekaḥ sphuṭabodhajanyo vibhajya dṛgdṛśyapadārthatattvam & chinatti māyākṛtamohabandhaṃ yasmādvimuktastu punarna saṃsṛtiḥ
verses.translation: Perfect discrimination brought on by direct realisation distinguishes the true nature of the subject from that of the object, and breaks the bond of delusion created by Maya; and there is no more transmigration for one who has been freed from this.

verses.sanskrit: परावरैकत्वविवेकवन्हिः दहत्यविद्यागहनं ह्यशेषम् किं स्यात्पुनः संसरणस्य बीजं अद्वैतभावं समुपेयुषो ऽस्य
verses.transliteration: parāvaraikatvavivekavanhiḥ dahatyavidyāgahanaṃ hyaśeṣam & kiṃ syātpunaḥ saṃsaraṇasya bījaṃ advaitabhāvaṃ samupeyuṣo 'sya
verses.translation: The knowledge of the identity of the Jiva and Brahman entirely consumes the impenetrable forest of Avidya or Nescience. For one who has realised the state of Oneness, is there any seed left for future transmigration ?

verses.sanskrit: आवरणस्य निवृत्तिर्भवति हि सम्यक्पदार्थदर्शनतः मिथ्याज्ञानविनाशस्तद्विक्षेपजनितदुःखनिवृत्तिः
verses.transliteration: āvaraṇasya nivṛttirbhavati hi samyakpadārthadarśanataḥ & mithyājñānavināśastadvikṣepajanitaduḥkhanivṛttiḥ
verses.translation: The veil that hides Truth vanishes only when the Reality is fully realised. (Thence follow) the destruction of false knowledge and the cessation of misery brought about by its distracting influence.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्त्रितयं दृष्टं सम्यग्रज्जुस्वरूपविज्ञानात् तस्माद्वस्तुसतत्त्वं ज्ञातव्यं बन्धमुक्तये विदुषा
verses.transliteration: etattritayaṃ dṛṣṭaṃ samyagrajjusvarūpavijñānāt & tasmādvastusatattvaṃ jñātavyaṃ bandhamuktaye viduṣā
verses.translation: These three are observed in the case of a rope when its real nature is fully known. Therefore the wise man should know the real nature of things for the breaking of his bonds.

verses.sanskrit: अयो ऽग्नियोगाद् इव सत्समन्वयान् मात्रादिरूपेण विजृम्भते धीः तत्कार्यमेतद्द्वितयं यतो मृषा दृष्टं भ्रमस्वप्नमनोरथेषु
verses.transliteration: ayo 'gniyogād iva satsamanvayān mātrādirūpeṇa vijṛmbhate dhīḥ & tatkāryametaddvitayaṃ yato mṛṣā dṛṣṭaṃ bhramasvapnamanoratheṣu
verses.translation: Like iron manifesting as sparks through contact with fire, the Buddhi manifests itself as knower and known through the inherence of Brahman. As these two (knower and known), the effects of the Buddhi, are observed to be unreal in the case of delusion, dream and fancy, similarly, the modifications of the Prakriti, from egoism down to the body and all sense-objects are also unreal. Their unreality is verily due to their being subject to change every moment. But the Atman never changes.

verses.sanskrit: ततो विकाराः प्रकृतेरहंमुखा देहावसाना विषयाश्च सर्वे क्षणे ऽन्यथाभावितया ह्यमीषाम् असत्त्वमात्मा तु कदापि नान्यथा
verses.transliteration: tato vikārāḥ prakṛterahaṃmukhā dehāvasānā viṣayāśca sarve & kṣaṇe 'nyathābhāvitayā hyamīṣām asattvamātmā tu kadāpi nānyathā
verses.translation: Like iron manifesting as sparks through contact with fire, the Buddhi manifests itself as knower and known through the inherence of Brahman. As these two (knower and known), the effects of the Buddhi, are observed to be unreal in the case of delusion, dream and fancy, similarly, the modifications of the Prakriti, from egoism down to the body and all sense-objects are also unreal. Their unreality is verily due to their being subject to change every moment. But the Atman never changes.

verses.sanskrit: नित्याद्वयाखण्डचिदेकरूपो बुद्ध्यादिसाक्षी सदसद्विलक्षणः अहंपदप्रत्ययलक्षितार्थः प्रत्यक् सदानन्दघनः परात्मा
verses.transliteration: nityādvayākhaṇḍacidekarūpo buddhyādisākṣī sadasadvilakṣaṇaḥ & ahaṃpadapratyayalakṣitārthaḥ pratyak sadānandaghanaḥ parātmā
verses.translation: The Supreme Self is ever of the nature of eternal, indivisible knowledge, one without a second, the Witness of the Buddhi and the rest, distinct from the gross and subtle, the implied meaning of the term and idea "I", the embodiment of inward, eternal bliss.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं विपश्चित्सदसद्विभज्य निश्चित्य तत्त्वं निजबोधदृष्ट्या ज्ञात्वा स्वमात्मानमखण्डबोधं तेभ्यो विमुक्तः स्वयम् एव शाम्यति
verses.transliteration: itthaṃ vipaścitsadasadvibhajya niścitya tattvaṃ nijabodhadṛṣṭyā & jñātvā svamātmānamakhaṇḍabodhaṃ tebhyo vimuktaḥ svayam eva śāmyati
verses.translation: The wise man, discriminating thus the real and the unreal, ascertaining the Truth through his illuminative insight, and realising his own Self which is Knowledge Absolute, gets rid of the obstructions and directly attains Peace.

verses.sanskrit: अज्ञानहृदयग्रन्थेर्निःशेषविलयस्तदा समाधिनाविकल्पेन यदाद्वैतात्मदर्शनम्
verses.transliteration: ajñānahṛdayagrantherniḥśeṣavilayastadā & samādhināvikalpena yadādvaitātmadarśanam
verses.translation: When the Atman, the One without a second, is realised by means of the Nirvikalpa Samadhi, then the hearts knot of ignorance is totally destroyed.

verses.sanskrit: त्वमहमिदमितीयं कल्पना बुद्धिदोषात् प्रभवति परमात्मन्यद्वये निर्विशेषे प्रविलसति समाधावस्य सर्वो विकल्पो विलयनमुपगच्छेद्वस्तुतत्त्वावधृत्या
verses.transliteration: tvamahamidamitīyaṃ kalpanā buddhidoṣāt prabhavati paramātmanyadvaye nirviśeṣe & pravilasati samādhāvasya sarvo vikalpo vilayanamupagacchedvastutattvāvadhṛtyā
verses.translation: Such imaginations as "thou", "I" or "this" take place through the defects of the Buddhi. But when the Paramatman, the Absolute, the One without a second, manifests Itself in Samadhi, all such imaginations are dissolved for the aspirant, through the realisation of the truth of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: शान्तो दान्तः परमुपरतः क्षान्तियुक्तः समाधिं कुर्वन्नित्यं कलयति यतिः स्वस्य सर्वात्मभावम् तेनाविद्यातिमिरजनितान्साधु दग्ध्वा विकल्पान् ब्रह्माकृत्या निवसति सुखं निष्क्रियो निर्विकल्पः
verses.transliteration: śānto dāntaḥ paramuparataḥ kṣāntiyuktaḥ samādhiṃ kurvannityaṃ kalayati yatiḥ svasya sarvātmabhāvam & tenāvidyātimirajanitānsādhu dagdhvā vikalpān brahmākṛtyā nivasati sukhaṃ niṣkriyo nirvikalpaḥ
verses.translation: The Sannyasin, calm, self-controlled, perfectly retiring from the sense-world, forbearing, and devoting himself to the practice of Samadhi, always reflects on his own self being the Self of the whole universe. Destroying completely by this means the imaginations which are due to the gloom of ignorance, he lives blissfully as Brahman, free from action and the oscillations of the mind.

verses.sanskrit: समाहिता ये प्रविलाप्य बाह्यं श्रोत्रादि चेतः स्वमहं चिदात्मनि त एव मुक्ता भवपाशबन्धैः नान्ये तु पारोक्ष्यकथाभिधायिनः
verses.transliteration: samāhitā ye pravilāpya bāhyaṃ śrotrādi cetaḥ svamahaṃ cidātmani & ta eva muktā bhavapāśabandhaiḥ nānye tu pārokṣyakathābhidhāyinaḥ
verses.translation: Those alone are free from the bondage of transmigration who, attaining Samadhi, have merged the objective world, the sense-organs, the mind, nay, the very ego, in the Atman, the Knowledge Absolute and none else, who but dabble in second-hand talks.

verses.sanskrit: उपाधिभेदात्स्वयम् एव भिद्यते चोपाध्यपोहे स्वयम् एव केवलः तस्मादुपाधेर्विलयाय विद्वान् वसेत्सदाकल्पसमाधिनिष्ठया
verses.transliteration: upādhibhedātsvayam eva bhidyate copādhyapohe svayam eva kevalaḥ & tasmādupādhervilayāya vidvān vasetsadākalpasamādhiniṣṭhayā
verses.translation: Through the diversity of the supervening conditions (Upadhis), a man is apt to think of himself as also full of diversity; but with the removal of these he is again his own Self, the immutable. Therefore the wise man should ever devote himself to the practice of Nirvikalpa Samadhi, for the dissolution of the Upadhis.

verses.sanskrit: सति सक्तो नरो याति सद्भावं ह्येकनिष्ठया कीटको भ्रमरं ध्यायन् भ्रमरत्वाय कल्पते
verses.transliteration: sati sakto naro yāti sadbhāvaṃ hyekaniṣṭhayā & kīṭako bhramaraṃ dhyāyan bhramaratvāya kalpate
verses.translation: The man who is attached to the Real becomes Real, through his one-pointed devotion. Just as the cockroach thinking intently on the Bhramara is transformed into a Bhramara.

verses.sanskrit: क्रियान्तरासक्तिमपास्य कीटको ध्यायन्नलित्वं ह्यलिभावमृच्छति तथैव योगी परमात्मतत्त्वं ध्यात्वा समायाति तदेकनिष्ठया
verses.transliteration: kriyāntarāsaktimapāsya kīṭako dhyāyannalitvaṃ hyalibhāvamṛcchati & tathaiva yogī paramātmatattvaṃ dhyātvā samāyāti tadekaniṣṭhayā
verses.translation: Just as the cockroach, giving up the attachment to all other actions, thinks intently on the Bhramara and becomes transformed into that worm, exactly in the same manner the Yogi, meditating on the truth of the Paramatman, attains to It through his one-pointed devotion to that.

verses.sanskrit: अतीव सूक्ष्मं परमात्मतत्त्वं न स्थूलदृष्ट्या प्रतिपत्तुमर्हति समाधिनात्यन्तसुसूक्ष्मवृत्या ज्ञातव्यमार्यैर् अतिशुद्धबुद्धिभिः
verses.transliteration: atīva sūkṣmaṃ paramātmatattvaṃ na sthūladṛṣṭyā pratipattumarhati & samādhinātyantasusūkṣmavṛtyā jñātavyamāryair atiśuddhabuddhibhiḥ
verses.translation: The truth of the Paramatman is extremely subtle, and cannot be reached by the gross outgoing tendency of the mind. It is only accessible to noble souls with perfectly pure minds, by means of Samadhi brought on by an extraordinary fineness of the mental state.

verses.sanskrit: यथा सुवर्णं पुटपाकशोधितं त्यक्त्वा मलं स्वात्मगुणं समृच्छति तथा मनः सत्त्वरजस्तमोमलं ध्यानेन सन्त्यज्य समेति तत्त्वम्
verses.transliteration: yathā suvarṇaṃ puṭapākaśodhitaṃ tyaktvā malaṃ svātmaguṇaṃ samṛcchati & tathā manaḥ sattvarajastamomalaṃ dhyānena santyajya sameti tattvam
verses.translation: As gold purified by thorough heating on the fire gives up its impurities and attains to its own lustre, so the mind, through meditation, gives up its impurities of Sattva, Rajas and Tamas, and attains to the reality of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: निरन्तराभ्यासवशात्तदित्थं पक्वं मनो ब्रह्मणि लीयते यदा तदा समाधिः सविकल्पवर्जितः स्वतो ऽद्वयानन्दरसानुभावकः
verses.transliteration: nirantarābhyāsavaśāttaditthaṃ pakvaṃ mano brahmaṇi līyate yadā & tadā samādhiḥ savikalpavarjitaḥ svato 'dvayānandarasānubhāvakaḥ
verses.translation: When the mind, thus purified by constant practice, is merged in Brahman, then Samadhi passes on from the Savikalpa to the Nirvikalpa stage, and leads directly to the realisation of the Bliss of Brahman, the One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: समाधिनानेन समस्तवासना- ग्रन्थेर्विनाशो ऽखिलकर्मनाशः अन्तर्बहिः सर्वत एव सर्वदा स्वरूपविस्फूर्तिरयत्नतः स्यात्
verses.transliteration: samādhinānena samastavāsanā- granthervināśo 'khilakarmanāśaḥ & antarbahiḥ sarvata eva sarvadā svarūpavisphūrtirayatnataḥ syāt
verses.translation: By this Samadhi are destroyed all desires which are like knots, all work is at an end, and inside and out there takes place everywhere and always the spontaneous manifestation of ones real nature.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतेः शतगुणं विद्यान्मननं मननाद् अपि निदिंध्यासं लक्षगुणमनन्तं निर्विकल्पकम्
verses.transliteration: śruteḥ śataguṇaṃ vidyānmananaṃ mananād api & nidiṃdhyāsaṃ lakṣaguṇamanantaṃ nirvikalpakam
verses.translation: Reflection should be considered a hundred times superior to hearing, and meditation a hundred thousand times superior even to reflection, but the Nirvikalpa Samadhi is infinite in its results.

verses.sanskrit: निर्विकल्पकसमाधिना स्फुटं ब्रह्मतत्त्वमवगम्यते ध्रुवम् नान्यथा चलतया मनोगतेः प्रत्ययान्तरविमिश्रितं भवेत्
verses.transliteration: nirvikalpakasamādhinā sphuṭaṃ brahmatattvamavagamyate dhruvam & nānyathā calatayā manogateḥ pratyayāntaravimiśritaṃ bhavet
verses.translation: By the Nirvikalpa Samadhi the truth of Brahman is clearly and definitely realised, but not otherwise, for then the mind, being unstable by nature, is apt to be mixed up with other perceptions.

verses.sanskrit: अतः समाधत्स्व यतेन्द्रियः सन् निरन्तरं शान्तमनाः प्रतीचि विध्वंसय ध्वान्तमनाद्यविद्यया कृतं सदेकत्वविलोकनेन
verses.transliteration: ataḥ samādhatsva yatendriyaḥ san nirantaraṃ śāntamanāḥ pratīci & vidhvaṃsaya dhvāntamanādyavidyayā kṛtaṃ sadekatvavilokanena
verses.translation: Hence with the mind calm and the senses controlled always drown the mind in the Supreme Self that is within, and through the realisation of thy identity with that Reality destroy the darkness created by Nescience, which is without beginning.

verses.sanskrit: योगस्य प्रथमद्वारं वाङ्निरोधो ऽपरिग्रहः निराशा च निरीहा च नित्यमेकान्तशीलता
verses.transliteration: yogasya prathamadvāraṃ vāṅnirodho 'parigrahaḥ & nirāśā ca nirīhā ca nityamekāntaśīlatā
verses.translation: The first steps to Yoga are control of speech, non-receiving of gifts, entertaining of no expectations, freedom from activity, and always living in a retired place.

verses.sanskrit: एकान्तस्थितिरिन्द्रियोपरमणे हेनुर्दमश्चेतसः संरोधे करणं शमेन विलयं यायादहंवासना तेनानन्दरसानुभूतिरचला ब्राह्मी सदा योगिनः तस्माच्चित्तनिरोध एव सततं कार्यः प्रयत्नो मुनेः
verses.transliteration: ekāntasthitirindriyoparamaṇe henurdamaścetasaḥ saṃrodhe karaṇaṃ śamena vilayaṃ yāyādahaṃvāsanā & tenānandarasānubhūtiracalā brāhmī sadā yoginaḥ tasmāccittanirodha eva satataṃ kāryaḥ prayatno muneḥ
verses.translation: Living in a retired place serves to control the sense-organs, control of the senses helps to control the mind, through control of the mind egoism is destroyed; and this again gives the Yogi an unbroken realisation of the Bliss of Brahman. Therefore the man of reflection should always strive only to control the mind.

verses.sanskrit: वाचं नियच्छात्मनि तं नियच्छ बुद्धौ धियं यच्छ च बुद्धिसाक्षिणि तं चापि पूर्णात्मनि निर्विकल्पे विलाप्य शान्तिं परमां भजस्व
verses.transliteration: vācaṃ niyacchātmani taṃ niyaccha buddhau dhiyaṃ yaccha ca buddhisākṣiṇi & taṃ cāpi pūrṇātmani nirvikalpe vilāpya śāntiṃ paramāṃ bhajasva
verses.translation: Restrain speech in the Manas, and restrain Manas in the Buddhi; this again restrain in the witness of Buddhi, and merging that also in the Infinite Absolute Self, attain to supreme Peace.

verses.sanskrit: देहप्राणेन्द्रियमनोबुद्ध्यादिभिरुपाधिभिः यैर् यैर् वृत्तेःसमायोगस्ततद्भावो ऽस्य योगिनः
verses.transliteration: dehaprāṇendriyamanobuddhyādibhirupādhibhiḥ & yair yair vṛtteḥsamāyogastatadbhāvo 'sya yoginaḥ
verses.translation: The body, Pranas, organs, manas, Buddhi and the rest with whichsoever of these supervening adjuncts the mind is associated, the Yogi is transformed, as it were, into that.

verses.sanskrit: तन्निवृत्त्या मुनेः सम्यक् सर्वोपरमणं सुखम् संदृश्यते सदानन्दरसानुभवविप्लवः
verses.transliteration: tannivṛttyā muneḥ samyak sarvoparamaṇaṃ sukham & saṃdṛśyate sadānandarasānubhavaviplavaḥ
verses.translation: When this is stopped, the man of reflection is found to be easily detached from everything, and to get the experience of an abundance of everlasting Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तस्त्यागो बहिस्त्यागो विरक्तस्यैव युज्यते त्यजत्यन्तर्बहिःसङ्गं विरक्तस्तु मुमुक्षया
verses.transliteration: antastyāgo bahistyāgo viraktasyaiva yujyate & tyajatyantarbahiḥsaṅgaṃ viraktastu mumukṣayā
verses.translation: It is the man of dispassion (Vairagya) who is fit for this internal as well as external renunciation; for the dispassionate man, out of the desire to be free, relinquishes both internal and external attachment.

verses.sanskrit: बहिस्तु विषयैः सङ्गं तथान्तरहमादिभिः विरक्त एव शक्नोति त्यक्तुं ब्रह्मणि निष्ठितः
verses.transliteration: bahistu viṣayaiḥ saṅgaṃ tathāntarahamādibhiḥ & virakta eva śaknoti tyaktuṃ brahmaṇi niṣṭhitaḥ
verses.translation: It is only the dispassionate man who, being thoroughly grounded in Brahman, can give up the external attachment to the sense-objects and the internal attachment for egoism etc.

verses.sanskrit: वैराग्यबोधौ पुरुषस्य पक्षिवत् पक्षौ विजानीहि विचक्षण त्वम् विमुक्तिसौधाग्रलताधिरोहणं ताभ्यां विना नान्यतरेण सिध्यति
verses.transliteration: vairāgyabodhau puruṣasya pakṣivat pakṣau vijānīhi vicakṣaṇa tvam & vimuktisaudhāgralatādhirohaṇaṃ tābhyāṃ vinā nānyatareṇa sidhyati
verses.translation: Know, O wise man, dispassion and discrimination to be like the two wings of a bird in the case of an aspirant. Unless both are there, none can, with the help of either one, reach the creeper of Liberation that grows, as it were, on the top of an edifice.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यन्तवैराग्यवतः समाधिः समाहितस्यैव दृढप्रबोधः प्रबुद्धतत्त्वस्य हि बन्धमुक्तिः मुक्तात्मनो नित्यसुखानुभूतिः
verses.transliteration: atyantavairāgyavataḥ samādhiḥ samāhitasyaiva dṛḍhaprabodhaḥ & prabuddhatattvasya hi bandhamuktiḥ muktātmano nityasukhānubhūtiḥ
verses.translation: The extremely dispassionate man alone has Samadhi, and the man of Samadhi alone gets steady realisation; the man who has realised the Truth is alone free from bondage, and the free soul only experiences eternal Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: वैराग्यान् न परं सुखस्य जनकं पश्यामि वश्यात्मनः तच्चेच्छुद्धतरात्मबोधसहितं स्वाराज्यसाम्राज्यधुक् एतद्द्वारमजस्रमुक्तियुवतेर्यस्मात्त्वमस्मात्परं सर्वत्रास्पृहया सदात्मनि सदा प्रज्ञां कुरु श्रेयसे
verses.transliteration: vairāgyān na paraṃ sukhasya janakaṃ paśyāmi vaśyātmanaḥ taccecchuddhatarātmabodhasahitaṃ svārājyasāmrājyadhuk & etaddvāramajasramuktiyuvateryasmāttvamasmātparaṃ sarvatrāspṛhayā sadātmani sadā prajñāṃ kuru śreyase
verses.translation: For the man of self-control I do not find any better instrument of happiness than dispassion, and if that is coupled with a highly pure realisation of the Self, it conduces to the suzerainty of absolute Independence; and since this is the gateway to the damsel of everlasting liberation, therefore for thy welfare, be dispassionate both internally and externally, and always fix thy mind on the eternal Self.

verses.sanskrit: आशां छिन्द्धि विषोपमेषु विषयेष्वेषैव मृत्योः कृतिस् त्यक्त्वा जातिकुलाश्रमेष्वभिमतिं मुञ्चातिदूरात्क्रियाः देहादावसति त्यजात्मधिषणां प्रज्ञां कुरुष्वात्मनि त्वं द्रष्टास्यमनो ऽसि निर्द्वयपरं ब्रह्मासि यद्वस्तुतः
verses.transliteration: āśāṃ chinddhi viṣopameṣu viṣayeṣveṣaiva mṛtyoḥ kṛtis tyaktvā jātikulāśrameṣvabhimatiṃ muñcātidūrātkriyāḥ & dehādāvasati tyajātmadhiṣaṇāṃ prajñāṃ kuruṣvātmani tvaṃ draṣṭāsyamano 'si nirdvayaparaṃ brahmāsi yadvastutaḥ
verses.translation: Sever thy craving for the sense-objects, which are like poison, for it is the very image of death, and giving up thy pride of caste, family and order of life, fling actions to a distance. Give up thy identification with such unreal things as the body, and fix thy mind on the Atman. For thou art really the Witness, Brahman, unshackled by the mind, the One without a second, and Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्ये ब्रह्मणि मानसं दृढतरं संस्थाप्य बाह्येन्द्रियं स्वस्थाने विनिवेश्य निश्चलतनुश्चोपेक्ष्य देहस्थितिम् ब्रह्मात्मैक्यमुपेत्य तन्मयतया चाखण्डवृत्त्यानिशं ब्रह्मानन्दरसं पिबात्मनि मुदा शून्यैः किमन्यैर् भृशम्
verses.transliteration: lakṣye brahmaṇi mānasaṃ dṛḍhataraṃ saṃsthāpya bāhyendriyaṃ svasthāne viniveśya niścalatanuścopekṣya dehasthitim & brahmātmaikyamupetya tanmayatayā cākhaṇḍavṛttyāniśaṃ brahmānandarasaṃ pibātmani mudā śūnyaiḥ kimanyair bhṛśam
verses.translation: Fixing the mind firmly on the Ideal, Brahman, and restraining the external organs in their respective centres; with the body held steady and taking no thought for its maintenance; attaining identity with Brahman and being one with It always drink joyfully of the Bliss of Brahman in thy own Self, without a break. What is the use of other things which are entirely hollow ?

verses.sanskrit: अनात्मचिन्तनं त्यक्त्वा कश्मलं दुःखकारणम् चिन्तयात्मानमानन्दरूपं यन्मुक्तिकारणम्
verses.transliteration: anātmacintanaṃ tyaktvā kaśmalaṃ duḥkhakāraṇam & cintayātmānamānandarūpaṃ yanmuktikāraṇam
verses.translation: Giving up the thought of the non-Self which is evil and productive of misery, think of the Self, the Bliss Absolute, which conduces to Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: एष स्वयंज्योतिरशेषसाक्षी विज्ञानकोशो विलसत्यजस्रम् लक्ष्यं विधायैनमसद्विलक्षणम् अखण्डवृत्त्यात्मतयानुभावय
verses.transliteration: eṣa svayaṃjyotiraśeṣasākṣī vijñānakośo vilasatyajasram & lakṣyaṃ vidhāyainamasadvilakṣaṇam akhaṇḍavṛttyātmatayānubhāvaya
verses.translation: Here shines eternally the Atman, the Self-effulgent Witness of everything, which has the Buddhi for Its seat. Making this Atman which is distinct from the unreal, the goal, meditate on It as thy own Self, excluding all other thought.

verses.sanskrit: एतमच्छीन्नया वृत्त्या प्रत्ययान्तरशून्यया उल्लेखयन्विजानीयात्स्वस्वरूपतया स्फुटम्
verses.transliteration: etamacchīnnayā vṛttyā pratyayāntaraśūnyayā & ullekhayanvijānīyātsvasvarūpatayā sphuṭam
verses.translation: Reflecting on this Atman continuously and without any foreign thought intervening, one must distinctly realise It to be ones real Self.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रात्मत्वं दृढीकुर्वन्नहमादिषु संत्यजन् उदासीनतया तेषु तिष्ठेत्स्फुटघटादिवत्
verses.transliteration: atrātmatvaṃ dṛḍhīkurvannahamādiṣu saṃtyajan & udāsīnatayā teṣu tiṣṭhetsphuṭaghaṭādivat
verses.translation: Strengthening ones identification with This, and giving up that with egoism and the rest, one must live without any concern for them, as if they were trifling things, like a cracked jar or the like.

verses.sanskrit: विशुद्धमन्तःकरणं स्वरूपे निवेश्य साक्षिण्य् अवबोधमात्रे शनैः शनैर् निश्चलतामुपानयन् पूर्णं स्वमेवानुविलोकयेत्ततः
verses.transliteration: viśuddhamantaḥkaraṇaṃ svarūpe niveśya sākṣiṇy avabodhamātre & śanaiḥ śanair niścalatāmupānayan pūrṇaṃ svamevānuvilokayettataḥ
verses.translation: Fixing the purified mind in the Self, the Witness, the Knowledge Absolute, and slowly making it still, one must then realise ones own infinite Self.

verses.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियप्राणमनो ऽहम् आदिभिः स्वाज्ञानकॢप्तैर् अखिलैर् उपाधिभिः विमुक्तमात्मानमखण्डरूपं पूर्णं महाकाशमिवावलोकयेत्
verses.transliteration: dehendriyaprāṇamano 'ham ādibhiḥ svājñānakḷptair akhilair upādhibhiḥ & vimuktamātmānamakhaṇḍarūpaṃ pūrṇaṃ mahākāśamivāvalokayet
verses.translation: One should behold the Atman, the Indivisible and Infinite, free from all limiting adjuncts such as the body, organs, Pranas, Manas and egoism, which are creations of ones own ignorance like the infinite sky.

verses.sanskrit: घटकलशकुसूलसूचिमुख्यैः गगनमुपाधिशतैर् विमुक्तमेकम् भवति न विविधं तथैव शुद्धं परमहमादिविमुक्तमेकम् एव
verses.transliteration: ghaṭakalaśakusūlasūcimukhyaiḥ gaganamupādhiśatair vimuktamekam & bhavati na vividhaṃ tathaiva śuddhaṃ paramahamādivimuktamekam eva
verses.translation: The sky, divested of the hundreds of limiting adjuncts such as a jar, a pitcher, a receptacle for grains or a needle, is one, and not diverse; exactly in a similar way the pure Brahman, when divested of egoism etc., is verily One.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मादिस्तम्बपर्यन्ता मृषामात्रा उपाधयः ततः पूर्णं स्वमात्मानं पश्येदेकात्मना स्थितम्
verses.transliteration: brahmādistambaparyantā mṛṣāmātrā upādhayaḥ & tataḥ pūrṇaṃ svamātmānaṃ paśyedekātmanā sthitam
verses.translation: The limiting adjuncts from Brahma down to a clump of grass are all wholly unreal. Therefore one should realise ones own Infinite Self as the only Principle.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र भ्रान्त्या कल्पितं तद्विवेके तत्तन्मात्रं नैव तस्माद्विभिन्नम् भ्रान्तेर्नाशे भाति दृष्टाहितत्त्वं रज्जुस्तद्वद्विश्वम् आत्मस्वरूपम्
verses.transliteration: yatra bhrāntyā kalpitaṃ tadviveke tattanmātraṃ naiva tasmādvibhinnam & bhrānternāśe bhāti dṛṣṭāhitattvaṃ rajjustadvadviśvam ātmasvarūpam
verses.translation: That in which something is imagined to exist through error, is, when rightly discriminated, that thing itself, and not distinct from it. When the error is gone, the reality about the snake falsely perceived becomes the rope. Similarly the universe is in reality the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं ब्रह्मा स्वयं विष्णुः स्वयमिन्द्रः स्वयं शिवः स्वयं विश्वमिदं सर्वं स्वस्मादन्यन् न किंचन
verses.transliteration: svayaṃ brahmā svayaṃ viṣṇuḥ svayamindraḥ svayaṃ śivaḥ & svayaṃ viśvamidaṃ sarvaṃ svasmādanyan na kiṃcana
verses.translation: The Self is Brahma, the Self is Vishnu, the Self is Indra, the Self is Shiva; the Self is all this universe. Nothing exists except the Self.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तः स्वयं चापि बहिः स्वयं च स्वयं पुरस्तात् स्वयम् एव पश्चात् स्वयं ह्यावाच्यां स्वयमप्युदीच्यां तथोपरिष्टात्स्वयमप्यधस्तात्
verses.transliteration: antaḥ svayaṃ cāpi bahiḥ svayaṃ ca svayaṃ purastāt svayam eva paścāt & svayaṃ hyāvācyāṃ svayamapyudīcyāṃ tathopariṣṭātsvayamapyadhastāt
verses.translation: The Self is within, and the Self is without; the Self is before and the Self is behind; the Self is in the south, and the Self is in the north; the Self likewise is above as also below.

verses.sanskrit: तरङ्गफेनभ्रमबुद्बुदादि सर्वं स्वरूपेण जलं यथा तथा चिद् एव देहाद्यहमन्तमेतत् सर्वं चिद् एवैकरसं विशुद्धम्
verses.transliteration: taraṅgaphenabhramabudbudādi sarvaṃ svarūpeṇa jalaṃ yathā tathā & cid eva dehādyahamantametat sarvaṃ cid evaikarasaṃ viśuddham
verses.translation: As the wave, the foam, the whirlpool, the bubble, etc., are all in essence but water, similarly the Chit (Knowledge Absolute) is all this, from the body up to egoism. Everything is verily the Chit, homogeneous and pure.

verses.sanskrit: सदेवेदं सर्वं जगदवगतं वाङ्मनसयोः सतो ऽन्यन्नास्त्य् एव प्रकृतिपरसीम्नि स्थितवतः पृथक् किं मृत्स्नायाः कलशघटकुम्भाद्यवगतं वदत्येष भ्रान्तस्त्वमहमिति मायामदिरया
verses.transliteration: sadevedaṃ sarvaṃ jagadavagataṃ vāṅmanasayoḥ sato 'nyannāsty eva prakṛtiparasīmni sthitavataḥ & pṛthak kiṃ mṛtsnāyāḥ kalaśaghaṭakumbhādyavagataṃ vadatyeṣa bhrāntastvamahamiti māyāmadirayā
verses.translation: All this universe known through speech and mind is nothing but Brahman; there is nothing besides Brahman, which exists beyond the utmost range of the Prakriti. Are the pitcher, jug, jar, etc., known to be distinct from the clay of which they are composed ? It is the deluded man who talks of "thou" and "I", as an effect of the wine of Maya.

verses.sanskrit: क्रियासमभिहारेण यत्र नान्यदिति श्रुतिः ब्रवीति द्वैतराहित्यं मिथ्याध्यासनिवृत्तये
verses.transliteration: kriyāsamabhihāreṇa yatra nānyaditi śrutiḥ & bravīti dvaitarāhityaṃ mithyādhyāsanivṛttaye
verses.translation: The Shruti, in the passage, "Where one sees nothing else", etc., declares by an accumulation of verbs the absence of duality, in order to remove the false superimpositions.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशवन्निर्मलनिर्विकल्पं निःसीमनिःस्पन्दननिर्विकारम् अन्तर्बहिःशून्यमनन्यमद्वयं स्वयं परं ब्रह्म किमस्ति बोध्यम्
verses.transliteration: ākāśavannirmalanirvikalpaṃ niḥsīmaniḥspandananirvikāram & antarbahiḥśūnyamananyamadvayaṃ svayaṃ paraṃ brahma kimasti bodhyam
verses.translation: The Supreme Brahman is, like the sky, pure, absolute, infinite, motionless and changeless, devoid of interior or exterior, the One Existence, without a second, and is ones own Self. Is there any other object of knowledge ?

verses.sanskrit: वक्तव्यं किमु विद्यते ऽत्र बहुधा ब्रह्मैव जीवः स्वयं ब्रह्मैतज्जगदाततं नु सकलं ब्रह्माद्वितीयं श्रुतिः ब्रह्मैवाहमिति प्रबुद्धमतयः संत्यक्तबाह्याः स्फुटं ब्रह्मीभूय वसन्ति सन्ततचिदानन्दात्मनैतद्ध्रुवम्
verses.transliteration: vaktavyaṃ kimu vidyate 'tra bahudhā brahmaiva jīvaḥ svayaṃ brahmaitajjagadātataṃ nu sakalaṃ brahmādvitīyaṃ śrutiḥ & brahmaivāhamiti prabuddhamatayaḥ saṃtyaktabāhyāḥ sphuṭaṃ brahmībhūya vasanti santatacidānandātmanaitaddhruvam
verses.translation: What is the use of dilating on this subject ? The Jiva is no other than Brahman; this whole extended universe is Brahman Itself; the Shruti inculcates the Brahman without a second; and it is an indubitable fact that people of enlightened minds who know their identity with Brahman and have given up their connection with the objective world, live palpably unifold with Brahman as Eternal Knowledge and Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: जहि मलमयकोशे ऽहंधियोत्थापिताशां प्रसभमनिलकल्पे लिङ्गदेहे ऽपि पश्चात् निगमगदितकीर्तिं नित्यमानन्दमूर्तिं स्वयमिति परिचीय ब्रह्मरूपेण तिष्ठ
verses.transliteration: jahi malamayakośe 'haṃdhiyotthāpitāśāṃ prasabhamanilakalpe liṅgadehe 'pi paścāt & nigamagaditakīrtiṃ nityamānandamūrtiṃ svayamiti paricīya brahmarūpeṇa tiṣṭha
verses.translation: (First) destroy the hopes raised by egoism in this filthy gross body, then do the same forcibly with the air-like subtle body; and realising Brahman, the embodiment of eternal Bliss whose glories the Scriptures proclaim as thy own Self, live as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: शवाकारं यावद्भजति मनुजस्तावदशुचिः परेभ्यः स्यात्क्लेशो जननमरणव्याधिनिलयः यदात्मानं शुद्धं कलयति शिवाकारमचलम् तदा तेभ्यो मुक्तो भवति हि तदाह श्रुतिर् अपि
verses.transliteration: śavākāraṃ yāvadbhajati manujastāvadaśuciḥ parebhyaḥ syātkleśo jananamaraṇavyādhinilayaḥ & yadātmānaṃ śuddhaṃ kalayati śivākāramacalam tadā tebhyo mukto bhavati hi tadāha śrutir api
verses.translation: So long as man has any regard for this corpse-like body, he is impure, and suffers from his enemies as also from birth, death and disease; but when he thinks of himself as pure, as the essence of good and immovable, he assuredly becomes free from them; the Shrutis also say this.

verses.sanskrit: स्वात्मन्यारोपिताशेषाभासर्वस्तुनिरासतः स्वयम् एव परं ब्रह्म पूर्णमद्वयमक्रियम्
verses.transliteration: svātmanyāropitāśeṣābhāsarvastunirāsataḥ & svayam eva paraṃ brahma pūrṇamadvayamakriyam
verses.translation: By the elimination of all apparent existences superimposed on the soul, the supreme Brahman, Infinite, the One without a second and beyond action, remains as Itself.

verses.sanskrit: समाहितायां सति चित्तवृत्तौ परात्मनि ब्रह्मणि निर्विकल्पे न दृश्यते कश्चिदयं विकल्पः प्रजल्पमात्रः परिशिष्यते यतः
verses.transliteration: samāhitāyāṃ sati cittavṛttau parātmani brahmaṇi nirvikalpe & na dṛśyate kaścidayaṃ vikalpaḥ prajalpamātraḥ pariśiṣyate yataḥ
verses.translation: When the mind-functions are merged in the Paramatman, the Brahman, the Absolute, none of this phenomenal world is seen, whence it is reduced to mere talk.

verses.sanskrit: असत्कल्पो विकल्पो ऽयं विश्वमित्येकवस्तुनि निर्विकारे निराकारे निर्विशेषे भिदा कुतः
verses.transliteration: asatkalpo vikalpo 'yaṃ viśvamityekavastuni & nirvikāre nirākāre nirviśeṣe bhidā kutaḥ
verses.translation: In the One Entity (Brahman) the conception of the universe is a mere phantom. Whence can there be any diversity in That which is changeless, formless and Absolute ?

verses.sanskrit: द्रष्टुदर्शनदृश्यादिभावशून्यैकवस्तुनि निर्विकारे निराकारे निर्विशेषे भिदा कुतः
verses.transliteration: draṣṭudarśanadṛśyādibhāvaśūnyaikavastuni & nirvikāre nirākāre nirviśeṣe bhidā kutaḥ
verses.translation: In the One Entity devoid of the concepts of seer, seeing and seen which is changeless, formless and Absolute whence can there be any diversity ?

verses.sanskrit: कल्पार्णव इवात्यन्तपरिपूर्णैकवस्तुनि निर्विकारे निराकारे निर्विशेषे भिदा कुतः
verses.transliteration: kalpārṇava ivātyantaparipūrṇaikavastuni & nirvikāre nirākāre nirviśeṣe bhidā kutaḥ
verses.translation: In the one changeless, formless, absolute reality, all-pervading and still like the ocean after cosmic dissolution, how can diversity exist?

verses.sanskrit: तेजसीव तमो यत्र प्रलीनं भ्रान्तिकारणम् अद्वितीये परे तत्त्वे निर्विशेषे भिदा कुतः
verses.transliteration: tejasīva tamo yatra pralīnaṃ bhrāntikāraṇam & advitīye pare tattve nirviśeṣe bhidā kutaḥ
verses.translation: Where the root of delusion is dissolved like darkness in light in the supreme Reality, the One without a second, the Absolute whence can there be any diversity ?

verses.sanskrit: एकात्मके परे तत्त्वे भेदवार्ता कथं वसेत् सुषुप्तौ सुखमात्रायां भेदः केनावलोकितः
verses.transliteration: ekātmake pare tattve bhedavārtā kathaṃ vaset & suṣuptau sukhamātrāyāṃ bhedaḥ kenāvalokitaḥ
verses.translation: How can the talk of diversity apply to the Supreme Reality which is one and homogeneous ? Who has ever observed diversity in the unmixed bliss of the state of profound sleep ?

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यस्ति विश्वं परतत्त्वबोधात् सदात्मनि ब्रह्मणि निर्विकल्पे कालत्रये नाप्यहिरीक्षितो गुणे न ह्यम्बुबिन्दुर्मृगतृष्णिकायाम्
verses.transliteration: na hyasti viśvaṃ paratattvabodhāt sadātmani brahmaṇi nirvikalpe & kālatraye nāpyahirīkṣito guṇe na hyambubindurmṛgatṛṣṇikāyām
verses.translation: Even before the realisation of the highest Truth, the universe does not exist in the Absolute Brahman, the Essence of Existence. In none of the three states of time is the snake ever observed in the rope, nor a drop of water in the mirage.

verses.sanskrit: मायामात्रमिदं द्वैतमद्वैतं परमार्थतः इति ब्रूते श्रुतिः साक्षात्सुषुप्तावनुभूयते
verses.transliteration: māyāmātramidaṃ dvaitamadvaitaṃ paramārthataḥ & iti brūte śrutiḥ sākṣātsuṣuptāvanubhūyate
verses.translation: The Shrutis themselves declare that this dualistic universe is but a delusion from the standpoint of Absolute Truth. This is also experienced in the state of dreamless sleep.

verses.sanskrit: अनन्यत्वमधिष्ठानादारोप्यस्य निरीक्षितम् पण्डितै रज्जुसर्पादौ विकल्पो भ्रान्तिजीवनः
verses.transliteration: ananyatvamadhiṣṭhānādāropyasya nirīkṣitam & paṇḍitai rajjusarpādau vikalpo bhrāntijīvanaḥ
verses.translation: That which is superimposed upon something else is observed by the wise to be identical with the substratum, as in the case of the rope appearing as the snake. The apparent difference depends solely on error.

verses.sanskrit: चित्तमूलो विकल्पो ऽयं चित्ताभावे न कश्चन अतश्चित्तं समाधेहि प्रत्यग्रूपे परात्मनि
verses.transliteration: cittamūlo vikalpo 'yaṃ cittābhāve na kaścana & ataścittaṃ samādhehi pratyagrūpe parātmani
verses.translation: This apparent universe has its root in the mind, and never persists after the mind is annihilated. Therefore dissolve the mind by concentrating it on the Supreme Self, which is thy inmost Essence.

verses.sanskrit: किम् अपि सततबोधं केवलानन्दरूपं निरुपममतिवेलं नित्यमुक्तं निरीहम् निरवधिगगनाभं निष्कलं निर्विकल्पं हृदि कलयति विद्वान् ब्रह्म पूर्णं समाधौ
verses.transliteration: kim api satatabodhaṃ kevalānandarūpaṃ nirupamamativelaṃ nityamuktaṃ nirīham & niravadhigaganābhaṃ niṣkalaṃ nirvikalpaṃ hṛdi kalayati vidvān brahma pūrṇaṃ samādhau
verses.translation: The wise man realises in his heart, through Samadhi, the Infinite Brahman, which is something of the nature of eternal Knowledge and absolute Bliss, which has no exemplar, which transcends all limitations, is ever free and without activity, and which is like the limitless sky, indivisible and absolute.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिविकृतिशून्यं भावनातीतभावं समरसमसमानं मानसं बन्धदूरम् निगमवचनसिद्धं नित्यमस्मत्प्रसिद्धं हृदि कलयति विद्वान् ब्रह्म पूर्णं समाधौ
verses.transliteration: prakṛtivikṛtiśūnyaṃ bhāvanātītabhāvaṃ samarasamasamānaṃ mānasaṃ bandhadūram & nigamavacanasiddhaṃ nityamasmatprasiddhaṃ hṛdi kalayati vidvān brahma pūrṇaṃ samādhau
verses.translation: The wise man realises in his heart, through Samadhi, the Infinite Brahman, which is devoid of the ideas of cause and effect, which is the Reality beyond all imaginations, homogeneous, matchless, beyond the range of proofs, established by the pronouncements of the Vedas, and ever familiar to us as the sense of the ego.

verses.sanskrit: अजरममरमस्ताभाववस्तुस्वरूपं स्तिमितसलिलराशिप्रख्यमाख्याविहीनम् शमितगुणविकारं शाश्वतं शान्तमेकं हृदि कलयति विद्वान् ब्रह्म पूर्णं समाधौ
verses.transliteration: ajaramamaramastābhāvavastusvarūpaṃ stimitasalilarāśiprakhyamākhyāvihīnam & śamitaguṇavikāraṃ śāśvataṃ śāntamekaṃ hṛdi kalayati vidvān brahma pūrṇaṃ samādhau
verses.translation: The wise man realises in his heart, through Samadhi, the Infinite Brahman, which is undecaying and immortal, the positive Entity which precludes all negations, which resembles the placid ocean and is without a name, in which there are neither merits nor demerits, and which is eternal, pacified and One.

verses.sanskrit: समाहितान्तःकरणः स्वरूपे विलोकयात्मानमखण्डवैभवम् विच्छिन्द्धि बन्धं भवगन्धगन्धितं यत्नेन पुंस्त्वं सफलीकुरुष्व
verses.transliteration: samāhitāntaḥkaraṇaḥ svarūpe vilokayātmānamakhaṇḍavaibhavam & vicchinddhi bandhaṃ bhavagandhagandhitaṃ yatnena puṃstvaṃ saphalīkuruṣva
verses.translation: With the mind restrained in Samadhi, behold in thy self the Atman, of infinite glory, cut off thy bondage strengthened by the impressions of previous births, and carefully attain the consummation of thy birth as a human being.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वोपाधिविनिर्मुक्तं सच्चिदानन्दमद्वयम्। भावयात्मानम् आत्मस्थं न भूयः कल्पसे ऽध्वने
verses.transliteration: sarvopādhivinirmuktaṃ saccidānandamadvayam| bhāvayātmānam ātmasthaṃ na bhūyaḥ kalpase 'dhvane
verses.translation: Meditate on the Atman, which resides in thee, which is devoid of all limiting adjuncts, the Existence-Knowledge-Bliss Absolute, the One without a second, and thou shalt no more come under the round of births and deaths.

verses.sanskrit: छायेव पुंसः परिदृश्यमान् माभासरूपेण फलानुभूत्या शरीरमाराच्छववन्निरस्तं पुनर्न संधत्त इदं महात्मा
verses.transliteration: chāyeva puṃsaḥ paridṛśyamān mābhāsarūpeṇa phalānubhūtyā & śarīramārācchavavannirastaṃ punarna saṃdhatta idaṃ mahātmā
verses.translation: After the body has once been cast off to a distance like a corpse, the sage never more attaches himself to it, though it is visible as an appearance, like the shadow of a man, owing to the experience of the effects of past deeds.

verses.sanskrit: सततविमलबोधानन्दरूपं समेत्य त्यज जडमलरूपोपाधिमेतं सुदूरे अथ पुनर् अपि नैष स्मर्यतां वान्तवस्तु स्मरणविषयभूतं पल्पते कुत्सनाय
verses.transliteration: satatavimalabodhānandarūpaṃ sametya tyaja jaḍamalarūpopādhimetaṃ sudūre & atha punar api naiṣa smaryatāṃ vāntavastu smaraṇaviṣayabhūtaṃ palpate kutsanāya
verses.translation: Realising the Atman, the eternal, pure Knowledge and Bliss, throw far away this limitation of a body, which is inert and filthy by nature. Then remember it no more, for something that has been vomited excites but disgust when called in memory.

verses.sanskrit: समूलमेतत्परिदाह्य वन्हौ सदात्मनि ब्रह्मणि निर्विकल्पे ततः स्वयं नित्यविशुद्धबोधा नन्दात्मना तिष्ठति विद्वरिष्ठः
verses.transliteration: samūlametatparidāhya vanhau sadātmani brahmaṇi nirvikalpe & tataḥ svayaṃ nityaviśuddhabodhā nandātmanā tiṣṭhati vidvariṣṭhaḥ
verses.translation: Burning all this, with its very root, in the fire of Brahman, the Eternal and Absolute Self, the truly wise man thereafter remains alone, as the Atman, the eternal, pure Knowledge and Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: प्रारब्धसूत्रग्रथितं शरीरं प्रयातु वा तिष्ठतु गोर् इव स्रक् न तत्पुनः पश्यति तत्त्ववेत्ता- (आ)नन्दात्मनि ब्रह्मणि लीनवृत्तिः
verses.transliteration: prārabdhasūtragrathitaṃ śarīraṃ prayātu vā tiṣṭhatu gor iva srak & na tatpunaḥ paśyati tattvavettā- (ā)nandātmani brahmaṇi līnavṛttiḥ
verses.translation: The knower of Truth does no more care whether this body, spun out by the threads of Prarabdha work, falls or remains like the garland on a cow for his mind-functions are at rest in the Brahman, the Essence of Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: अखण्डानन्दमात्मानं विज्ञाय स्वस्वरूपतः किमिच्छन् कस्य वा हेतोर् देहं पुष्णाति तत्त्ववित्
verses.transliteration: akhaṇḍānandamātmānaṃ vijñāya svasvarūpataḥ & kimicchan kasya vā hetor dehaṃ puṣṇāti tattvavit
verses.translation: Realising the Atman, the Infinite Bliss, as his very Self, with what object, or for whom, should the knower of Truth cherish the body.

verses.sanskrit: संसिद्धस्य फलं त्वेतज्जीवन्मुक्तस्य योगिनः बहिरन्तः सदानन्दरसास्वादनम् आत्मनि
verses.transliteration: saṃsiddhasya phalaṃ tvetajjīvanmuktasya yoginaḥ & bahirantaḥ sadānandarasāsvādanam ātmani
verses.translation: The Yogi who has attained perfection and is liberated-in-life gets this as result he enjoys eternal Bliss in his mind, internally as well as externally.

verses.sanskrit: वैराग्यस्य फलं बोधो बोधस्योपरतिः फलम् स्वानन्दानुभवाच्छान्तिरेषैवोपरतेः फलम्
verses.transliteration: vairāgyasya phalaṃ bodho bodhasyoparatiḥ phalam & svānandānubhavācchāntireṣaivoparateḥ phalam
verses.translation: The result of dispassion is knowledge, that of Knowledge is withdrawal from sense-pleasures, which leads to the experience of the Bliss of the Self, whence follows Peace.

verses.sanskrit: यद्य् उत्तरोत्तराभावः पूर्वपूर्वन्तु निष्फलम् निवृत्तिः परमा तृप्तिरानन्दो ऽनुपमः स्वतः
verses.transliteration: yady uttarottarābhāvaḥ pūrvapūrvantu niṣphalam & nivṛttiḥ paramā tṛptirānando 'nupamaḥ svataḥ
verses.translation: If there is an absence of the succeeding stages, the preceding ones are futile. (When the series is perfect) the cessation of the objective world, extreme satisfaction, and matchless bliss follow as a matter of course.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्टदुःखेष्वनुद्वेगो विद्यायाः प्रस्तुतं फलम् यत्कृतं भ्रान्तिवेलायां नाना कर्म जुगुप्सितम् \ पश्चान्नरो विवेकेन तत् कथं कर्तुम् अर्हति
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭaduḥkheṣvanudvego vidyāyāḥ prastutaṃ phalam & yatkṛtaṃ bhrāntivelāyāṃ nānā karma jugupsitam \ paścānnaro vivekena tat kathaṃ kartum arhati
verses.translation: Being unruffled by earthly troubles is the result in question of knowledge. How can a man who did various loathsome deeds during the state of delusion, commit the same afterwards, possessed of discrimination ?

verses.sanskrit: विद्याफलं स्यादसतो निवृत्तिः प्रवृत्तिरज्ञानफलं तदीक्षितम् तज्ज्ञाज्ञयोर् यन्मृगतृष्णिकादौ नोचेद्विदां दृष्टफलं किमस्मात्
verses.transliteration: vidyāphalaṃ syādasato nivṛttiḥ pravṛttirajñānaphalaṃ tadīkṣitam & tajjñājñayor yanmṛgatṛṣṇikādau nocedvidāṃ dṛṣṭaphalaṃ kimasmāt
verses.translation: The result of knowledge should be the turning away from unreal things, while attachment to these is the result of ignorance. This is observed in the case of one who knows a mirage and things of that sort, and one who does not. Otherwise, what other tangible result do the knowers of Brahman obtain ?

verses.sanskrit: अज्ञानहृदयग्रन्थेर्विनाशो यद्य् अशेषतः अनिच्छोर्विषयः किं नु प्रवृत्तेः कारणं स्वतः
verses.transliteration: ajñānahṛdayagranthervināśo yady aśeṣataḥ & anicchorviṣayaḥ kiṃ nu pravṛtteḥ kāraṇaṃ svataḥ
verses.translation: If the hearts knot of ignorance is totally destroyed, what natural cause can there be for inducing such a man to selfish action, for he is averse to sense-pleasures ?

verses.sanskrit: वासनानुदयो भोग्ये वैरागस्य तदावधिः अहंभावोदयाभावो बोधस्य परमावधिः \ लीनवृत्तैर् अनुत्पत्तिर्मर्यादोपरतेस्तु सा
verses.transliteration: vāsanānudayo bhogye vairāgasya tadāvadhiḥ & ahaṃbhāvodayābhāvo bodhasya paramāvadhiḥ \ līnavṛttair anutpattirmaryādoparatestu sā
verses.translation: When the sense-objects excite no more desire, then is the culmination of dispassion. The extreme perfection of knowledge is the absence of any impulsion of the egoistic idea. And the limit of self-withdrawal is reached when the mind-functions that have been merged, appear no more.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्माकारतया सदा स्थिततया निर्मुक्तबाह्यार्थधीर् अन्यावेदितभोग्यभोगकलनो निद्रालुवद्बालवत् स्वप्नालोकितलोकवज्जगदिदं पश्यन् क्वचिल् लब्धधीर् आस्ते कश्चिदनन्तपुण्यफलभुग्धन्यः स मान्यो भुवि
verses.transliteration: brahmākāratayā sadā sthitatayā nirmuktabāhyārthadhīr anyāveditabhogyabhogakalano nidrāluvadbālavat & svapnālokitalokavajjagadidaṃ paśyan kvacil labdhadhīr āste kaścidanantapuṇyaphalabhugdhanyaḥ sa mānyo bhuvi
verses.translation: Freed from all sense of reality of the external sense-objects on account of his always remaining merged in Brahman; only seeming to enjoy such sense-objects as are offered by others, like one sleepy, or like a child; beholding this world as one seen in dreams, and having cognition of it at chance moments rare indeed is such a man, the enjoyer of the fruits of endless merit, and he alone is blessed and esteemed on earth.

verses.sanskrit: स्थितप्रज्ञो यतिरयं यः सदानन्दमश्नुते ब्रह्मण्य् एव विलीनात्मा निर्विकारो विनिष्क्रियः
verses.transliteration: sthitaprajño yatirayaṃ yaḥ sadānandamaśnute & brahmaṇy eva vilīnātmā nirvikāro viniṣkriyaḥ
verses.translation: That Sannyasin has got a steady illumination who, having his soul wholly merged in Brahman, enjoys eternal bliss, is changeless and free from activity.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मात्मनोः शोधितयोर् एकभावावगाहिनी निर्विकल्पा च चिन्मात्रा वृत्तिः प्रज्ञेति कथ्यते \ सुस्थितासौ भवेद्यस्य स्थितप्रज्ञः स उच्यते
verses.transliteration: brahmātmanoḥ śodhitayor ekabhāvāvagāhinī & nirvikalpā ca cinmātrā vṛttiḥ prajñeti kathyate \ susthitāsau bhavedyasya sthitaprajñaḥ sa ucyate
verses.translation: That kind of mental function which cognises only the identity of the Self and Brahman, purified of all adjuncts, which is free from duality, and which concerns itself only with Pure Intelligence, is called illumination. He who has this perfectly steady is called a man of steady illumination.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य स्थिता भवेत्प्रज्ञा यस्यानन्दो निरन्तरः प्रपञ्चो विस्मृतप्रायः स जीवन्मुक्त इष्यते
verses.transliteration: yasya sthitā bhavetprajñā yasyānando nirantaraḥ & prapañco vismṛtaprāyaḥ sa jīvanmukta iṣyate
verses.translation: He whose illumination is steady, who has constant bliss, and who has almost forgotten the phenomenal universe, is accepted as a man liberated in this very life.

verses.sanskrit: लीनधीर् अपि जागर्ति जाग्रद्धर्मविवर्जितः बोधो निर्वासनो यस्य स जीवन्मुक्त इष्यते
verses.transliteration: līnadhīr api jāgarti jāgraddharmavivarjitaḥ & bodho nirvāsano yasya sa jīvanmukta iṣyate
verses.translation: He who, even having his mind merged in Brahman, is nevertheless quite alert, but free at the same time from the characteristics of the waking state, and whose realisation is free from desires, is accepted as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: शान्तसंसारकलनः कलावान् अपि निष्कलः यस्य चित्तं विनिश्चिन्तं स जीवन्मुक्त इष्यते
verses.transliteration: śāntasaṃsārakalanaḥ kalāvān api niṣkalaḥ & yasya cittaṃ viniścintaṃ sa jīvanmukta iṣyate
verses.translation: He whose cares about the phenomenal state have been appeased, who, though possessed of a body consisting of parts, is yet devoid of parts, and whose mind is free from anxiety, is accepted as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: वर्तमाने ऽपि देहे ऽस्मिञ्छायावदनुवर्तिनि अहन्ताममताभावो जीवन्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: vartamāne 'pi dehe 'smiñchāyāvadanuvartini & ahantāmamatābhāvo jīvanmuktasya lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: The absence of the ideas of "I" and "mine" even in this existing body which follows as a shadow, is a characteristic of one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: अतीताननुसन्धानं भविष्यदविचारणम् औदासीन्यम् अपि प्राप्तं जीवन्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: atītānanusandhānaṃ bhaviṣyadavicāraṇam & audāsīnyam api prāptaṃ jīvanmuktasya lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: Not dwelling on enjoyments of the past, taking no thought for the future and looking with indifference upon the present, are characteristics of one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: गुणदोषविशिष्टे ऽस्मिन्स्वभावेन विलक्षणे सर्वत्र समदर्शित्वं जीवन्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: guṇadoṣaviśiṣṭe 'sminsvabhāvena vilakṣaṇe & sarvatra samadarśitvaṃ jīvanmuktasya lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: Looking everywhere with an eye of equality in this world, full of elements possessing merits and demerits, and distinct by nature from one another, is a characteristic of one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: इष्टानिष्टार्थसम्प्राप्तौ समदर्शितयात्मनि उभयत्राविकारित्वं जीवन्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: iṣṭāniṣṭārthasamprāptau samadarśitayātmani & ubhayatrāvikāritvaṃ jīvanmuktasya lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: When things pleasant or painful present themselves, to remain unruffled in mind in both cases, through the sameness of attitude, is a characteristic of one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मानन्दरसास्वादासक्तचित्ततया यतेः अन्तर्बहिरविज्ञानं जीवन्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: brahmānandarasāsvādāsaktacittatayā yateḥ & antarbahiravijñānaṃ jīvanmuktasya lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: The absence of all ideas of interior or exterior in the case of a Sannyasin, owing to his mind being engrossed in tasting the bliss of Brahman, is a characteristic of one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियादौ कर्तव्ये ममाहंभाववर्जितः औदासीन्येन यस् तिष्ठेत्स जीवन्मुक्तलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: dehendriyādau kartavye mamāhaṃbhāvavarjitaḥ & audāsīnyena yas tiṣṭhetsa jīvanmuktalakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: He who lives unconcerned, devoid of all ideas of "I" and "mine" with regard to the body, organs, etc., as well as to his duties, is known as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञात आत्मनो यस्य ब्रह्मभावः श्रुतेर्बलात् भवबन्धविनिर्मुक्तः स जीवन्मुक्तलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: vijñāta ātmano yasya brahmabhāvaḥ śruterbalāt & bhavabandhavinirmuktaḥ sa jīvanmuktalakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: He who has realised his Brahmanhood aided by the Scriptures, and is free from the bondage of transmigration, is known as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियेष्वहंभाव इदंभावस्तदन्यके यस्य नो भवतः क्वापि स जीवन्मुक्त इष्यते
verses.transliteration: dehendriyeṣvahaṃbhāva idaṃbhāvastadanyake & yasya no bhavataḥ kvāpi sa jīvanmukta iṣyate
verses.translation: He who never has the idea of "I" with regard to the body, organs, etc., nor that of "it" in respect of things other than these, is accepted as one liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: न प्रत्यग्ब्रह्मणोर् भेदं कदापि ब्रह्मसर्गयोः प्रज्ञया यो विजानिति स जीवन्मुक्तलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: na pratyagbrahmaṇor bhedaṃ kadāpi brahmasargayoḥ & prajñayā yo vijāniti sa jīvanmuktalakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: He who through his illumination never differentiates the Jiva and Brahman, nor the universe and Brahman, is known as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: साधुभिः पूज्यमाने ऽस्मिन् पीड्यमाने ऽपि दुर्जनैः समभावो भवेद्यस्य स जीवन्मुक्तलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: sādhubhiḥ pūjyamāne 'smin pīḍyamāne 'pi durjanaiḥ & samabhāvo bhavedyasya sa jīvanmuktalakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: He who feels just the same when his body is either worshipped by the good or tormented by the wicked, is known as a man liberated-in-life.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र प्रविष्टा विषयाः परेरिता नदीप्रवाहा इव वारिराशौ लिनन्ति सन्मात्रतया न विक्रियां उत्पादयन्त्येष यतिर्विमुक्तः
verses.transliteration: yatra praviṣṭā viṣayāḥ pareritā nadīpravāhā iva vārirāśau & linanti sanmātratayā na vikriyāṃ utpādayantyeṣa yatirvimuktaḥ
verses.translation: The Sannyasin in whom the sense-objects directed by others are engulfed like flowing rivers in the sea and produce no change, owing to his identity with the Existence Absolute, is indeed liberated.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञातब्रह्मतत्त्वस्य यथापूर्वं न संसृतिः अस्ति चेन् न स विज्ञातब्रह्मभावो बहिर्मुखः
verses.transliteration: vijñātabrahmatattvasya yathāpūrvaṃ na saṃsṛtiḥ & asti cen na sa vijñātabrahmabhāvo bahirmukhaḥ
verses.translation: For one who has realised the Truth of Brahman, there is no more attachment to the sense-objects as before: If there is, that man has not realised his identity with Brahman, but is one whose senses are outgoing in their tendency.

verses.sanskrit: प्राचीनवासनावेगादसौ संसरतीति चेत् न सदेकत्वविज्ञानान्मन्दी भवति वासना
verses.transliteration: prācīnavāsanāvegādasau saṃsaratīti cet & na sadekatvavijñānānmandī bhavati vāsanā
verses.translation: If it be urged that he is still attached to the sense-objects through the momentum of his old desires, the reply is no, for desires get weakened through the realisation of ones identity with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यन्तकामुकस्यापि वृत्तिः कुण्ठति मातरि तथैव ब्रह्मणि ज्ञाते पूर्णानन्दे मनीषिणः
verses.transliteration: atyantakāmukasyāpi vṛttiḥ kuṇṭhati mātari & tathaiva brahmaṇi jñāte pūrṇānande manīṣiṇaḥ
verses.translation: The propensities of even a confirmed libertine are checked in the presence of his mother; just so, when Brahman, the Bliss Absolute, has been realised, the man of realisation has no longer any worldly tendency.

verses.sanskrit: निदिध्यासनशीलस्य बाह्यप्रत्यय ईक्ष्यते ब्रवीति श्रुतिरेतस्य प्रारब्धं फलदर्शनात्
verses.transliteration: nididhyāsanaśīlasya bāhyapratyaya īkṣyate & bravīti śrutiretasya prārabdhaṃ phaladarśanāt
verses.translation: One who is constantly practising meditation is observed to have external perceptions. The Shrutis mention Prarabdha work in the case of such a man, and we can infer this from results actually seen.

verses.sanskrit: सुखाद्यनुभवो यावत्तावत्प्रारब्धमिष्यते फलोदयः क्रियापूर्वो निष्क्रियो न हि कुत्रचित्
verses.transliteration: sukhādyanubhavo yāvattāvatprārabdhamiṣyate & phalodayaḥ kriyāpūrvo niṣkriyo na hi kutracit
verses.translation: Prarabdha work is acknowledged to persist so long as there is the perception of happiness and the like. Every result is preceded by an action, and nowhere is it seen to accrue independently of action.

verses.sanskrit: अहं ब्रह्मेति विज्ञानात् कल्पकोटिशतार्जितम् सञ्चितं विलयं याति प्रबोधात्स्वप्नकर्मवत्
verses.transliteration: ahaṃ brahmeti vijñānāt kalpakoṭiśatārjitam & sañcitaṃ vilayaṃ yāti prabodhātsvapnakarmavat
verses.translation: Through the realisation of ones identity with Brahman, all the accumulated actions of a hundred crore of cycles come to nought, like the actions of dream-state on awakening.

verses.sanskrit: यत्कृतं स्वप्नवेलायां पुण्यं वा पापम् उल्बणम् सुप्तोत्थितस्य किं तत् स्यात् स्वर्गाय नरकाय वा
verses.transliteration: yatkṛtaṃ svapnavelāyāṃ puṇyaṃ vā pāpam ulbaṇam & suptotthitasya kiṃ tat syāt svargāya narakāya vā
verses.translation: Can the good actions or dreadful sins that a man fancies himself doing in the dream-state, lead him to heaven or hell after he has awakened from sleep ?

verses.sanskrit: स्वम् असङ्गम् उदासीनं परिज्ञाय नभो यथा न श्लिष्यति च यत् किंचित् कदाचिद् भाविकर्मभिः
verses.transliteration: svam asaṅgam udāsīnaṃ parijñāya nabho yathā & na śliṣyati ca yat kiṃcit kadācid bhāvikarmabhiḥ
verses.translation: Realising the Atman, which is unattached and indifferent like the sky, the aspirant is never touched in the least by actions yet to be done.

verses.sanskrit: न नभो घटयोगेन सुरागन्धेन लिप्यते तथात्मोपाधियोगेन तद्धर्मैर् नैव लिप्यते
verses.transliteration: na nabho ghaṭayogena surāgandhena lipyate & tathātmopādhiyogena taddharmair naiva lipyate
verses.translation: The sky is not affected by the smell of liquor merely through its connection with the jar; similarly, the Atman is not, through Its connection with the limitations, affected by the properties thereof.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानोदयात्पुरारब्धं कर्मज्ञानान् न नश्यति अदत्वा स्वफलं लक्ष्यमुद्दिश्योत्सृष्टबाणवत्
verses.transliteration: jñānodayātpurārabdhaṃ karmajñānān na naśyati & adatvā svaphalaṃ lakṣyamuddiśyotsṛṣṭabāṇavat
verses.translation: The work which has fashioned this body prior to the dawning of knowledge, is not destroyed by that knowledge without yielding its fruits, like the arrow shot at an object.

verses.sanskrit: व्याघ्रबुद्ध्या विनिर्मुक्तो बाणः पश्चात्तु गोमतौ न तिष्ठति छिनत्य् एव लक्ष्यं वेगेन निर्भरम्
verses.transliteration: vyāghrabuddhyā vinirmukto bāṇaḥ paścāttu gomatau & na tiṣṭhati chinaty eva lakṣyaṃ vegena nirbharam
verses.translation: The arrow which is shot at an object with the idea that it is a tiger, does not, when that object is perceived to be a cow, check itself, but pierces the object with full force.

verses.sanskrit: प्राब्धं बलवत्तरं खलु विदां भोगेन तस्य क्षयः सम्यग्ज्ञानहुताशनेन विलयः प्राक्षंचितागामिनाम् ब्रह्मात्मैक्यमवेक्ष्य तन्मयतया ये सर्वदा संस्थिताः तेषां तत्त्रितयं नहि क्वचिद् अपि ब्रह्मैव ते निर्गुणम्
verses.transliteration: prābdhaṃ balavattaraṃ khalu vidāṃ bhogena tasya kṣayaḥ samyagjñānahutāśanena vilayaḥ prākṣaṃcitāgāminām & brahmātmaikyamavekṣya tanmayatayā ye sarvadā saṃsthitāḥ teṣāṃ tattritayaṃ nahi kvacid api brahmaiva te nirguṇam
verses.translation: Prarabdha work is certainly very strong for the man of realisation, and is spent only by the actual experience of its fruit; while the actions previously accumulated and those yet to come are destroyed by the fire of perfect knowledge. But none of the three at all affects those who, realising their identity with Brahman, are always living absorbed in that idea. They are verily the transcendent Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: उपाधितादात्म्यविहीनकेवल- ब्रह्मात्मनैवात्मनि तिष्ठतो मुनेः प्रारब्धसद्भावकथा न युक्ता स्वप्नार्थसंबन्धकथेव जाग्रतः
verses.transliteration: upādhitādātmyavihīnakevala- brahmātmanaivātmani tiṣṭhato muneḥ & prārabdhasadbhāvakathā na yuktā svapnārthasaṃbandhakatheva jāgrataḥ
verses.translation: For the sage who lives in his own Self as Brahman, the One without a second, devoid of identification with the limiting adjuncts, the question of the existence of Prarabdha work is meaningless, like the question of a man who has awakened from sleep having any connection with the objects seen in the dream-state.

verses.sanskrit: न हि प्रबुद्धः प्रतिभासदेहे देहोपयोगिन्य् अपि च प्रपञ्चे करोत्यहन्तां ममतानिदन्तां किन्तु स्वयं तिष्ठति जागरेण
verses.transliteration: na hi prabuddhaḥ pratibhāsadehe dehopayoginy api ca prapañce & karotyahantāṃ mamatānidantāṃ kintu svayaṃ tiṣṭhati jāgareṇa
verses.translation: The man who has awakened from sleep never has any idea of "I" or "mine" with regard to his dream-body and the dream-objects that ministered to that body, but lives quite awake, as his own Self.

verses.sanskrit: न तस्य मिथ्यार्थसमर्थनेच्छा न संग्रहस्तज्जगतो ऽपि दृष्टः तत्रानुवृत्तिर्यदि चेन्मृषार्थे न निद्रया मुक्त इतीष्यते ध्रुवम्
verses.transliteration: na tasya mithyārthasamarthanecchā na saṃgrahastajjagato 'pi dṛṣṭaḥ & tatrānuvṛttiryadi cenmṛṣārthe na nidrayā mukta itīṣyate dhruvam
verses.translation: He has no desire to substantiate the unreal objects, nor is he seen to maintain that dream-world. If he still clings to those unreal objects, he is emphatically declared to be not yet free from sleep.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वत्परे ब्रह्मणि वर्तमानः सदात्मना तिष्ठति नान्यदीक्षते स्मृतिर्यथा स्वप्नविलोकितार्थे तथा विदः प्राशनमोचनादौ
verses.transliteration: tadvatpare brahmaṇi vartamānaḥ sadātmanā tiṣṭhati nānyadīkṣate & smṛtiryathā svapnavilokitārthe tathā vidaḥ prāśanamocanādau
verses.translation: Similarly, he who is absorbed in Brahman lives identified with that eternal Reality and beholds nothing else. As one has a memory of the objects seen in a dream, so the man of realisation has a memory of the everyday actions such as eating.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणा निर्मितो देहः प्रारब्धं तस्य कल्प्यताम् नानादेर् आत्मनो युक्तं नैवात्मा कर्मनिर्मितः
verses.transliteration: karmaṇā nirmito dehaḥ prārabdhaṃ tasya kalpyatām & nānāder ātmano yuktaṃ naivātmā karmanirmitaḥ
verses.translation: The body has been fashioned by Karma, so one may imagine Prarabdha work with reference to it. But it is not reasonable to attribute the same to the Atman, for the Atman is never the outcome of work.

verses.sanskrit: अजो नित्यः शाश्वत इति ब्रूते श्रुतिरमोघवाक् तद् आत्मना तिष्ठतो ऽस्य कुतः प्रारब्धकल्पना
verses.transliteration: ajo nityaḥ śāśvata iti brūte śrutiramoghavāk & tad ātmanā tiṣṭhato 'sya kutaḥ prārabdhakalpanā
verses.translation: The Shrutis, whose words are infallible, declare the Atman to be "birthless, eternal and undecaying". So, the man who lives identified with That, how can Prarabdha work be attributed ?

verses.sanskrit: प्रारब्धं सिध्यति तदा यदा देहात्मना स्थितिः देहात्मभावो नैवेष्टः प्रारब्धं त्यज्यतामतः
verses.transliteration: prārabdhaṃ sidhyati tadā yadā dehātmanā sthitiḥ & dehātmabhāvo naiveṣṭaḥ prārabdhaṃ tyajyatāmataḥ
verses.translation: Prarabdha work can be maintained only so long as one lives identified with the body. But no one admits that the man of realisation ever identifies himself with the body. Hence Prarabdha work should be rejected in his case.

verses.sanskrit: शरीरस्यापि प्रारब्धकल्पना भ्रान्तिर् एव हि अध्यस्तस्य कुतः सत्त्वमसत्यस्य कुतो जनिः \ अजातस्य कुतो नाशः प्रारब्धमसतः कुतः
verses.transliteration: śarīrasyāpi prārabdhakalpanā bhrāntir eva hi & adhyastasya kutaḥ sattvamasatyasya kuto janiḥ \ ajātasya kuto nāśaḥ prārabdhamasataḥ kutaḥ
verses.translation: The attributing of Prarabdha work to the body even is certainly an error. How can something that is superimposed (on another) have any existence, and how can that which is unreal have a birth ? And how can that which has not been born at all, die ? So how can Prarabdha work exist for something that is unreal ?

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानेनाज्ञानकार्यस्य समूलस्य लयो यदि तिष्ठत्ययं कथं देह इति शङ्कावतो जडान्
