verses.transliteration: jñānenājñānakāryasya samūlasya layo yadi & tiṣṭhatyayaṃ kathaṃ deha iti śaṅkāvato jaḍān
verses.translation: "If the effects of ignorance are destroyed with their root by knowledge, then how does the body live?" it is to convince those fools who entertain a doubt like this, that the Shrutis, from a relative standpoint, hypothesise Prarabdha work, but not for proving the reality of the body etc., of the man of realisation.

verses.sanskrit: समाधातुं बाह्यदृष्ट्या प्रारब्धं वदति श्रुतिः न तु देहादिसत्यत्वबोधनाय विपश्चिताम्
verses.transliteration: samādhātuṃ bāhyadṛṣṭyā prārabdhaṃ vadati śrutiḥ & na tu dehādisatyatvabodhanāya vipaścitām
verses.translation: "If the effects of ignorance are destroyed with their root by knowledge, then how does the body live?" it is to convince those fools who entertain a doubt like this, that the Shrutis, from a relative standpoint, hypothesise Prarabdha work, but not for proving the reality of the body etc., of the man of realisation.

verses.sanskrit: परिपूर्णमनाद्यन्तमप्रमेयमविक्रियम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: paripūrṇamanādyantamaprameyamavikriyam & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, infinite, without beginning or end, transcendent and changeless; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: सद्गनं चिद्घनं नित्यमानन्दघनमक्रियम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: sadganaṃ cidghanaṃ nityamānandaghanamakriyam & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, the Essence of Existence, Knowledge and Eternal Bliss, and devoid of activity; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यगेकरसं पूर्णमनन्तं सर्वतोमुखम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: pratyagekarasaṃ pūrṇamanantaṃ sarvatomukham & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, which is within all, homogeneous, infinite, endless, and all-pervading; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: अहेयमनुपादेयमनादेयमनाश्रयम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: aheyamanupādeyamanādeyamanāśrayam & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, which is neither to be shunned nor taken up nor accepted, and which is without any support, there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: निर्गुणं निष्कलं सूक्ष्मं निर्विकल्पं निरञ्जनम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: nirguṇaṃ niṣkalaṃ sūkṣmaṃ nirvikalpaṃ nirañjanam & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, beyond attributes, without parts, subtle, absolute and taintless; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: अनिरूप्य स्वरूपं यन्मनोवाचामगोचरम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: anirūpya svarūpaṃ yanmanovācāmagocaram & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, whose real nature is incomprehensible, and which is beyond the range of mind and speech; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: सत्समृद्धं स्वतःसिद्धं शुद्धं बुद्धमनीदृशम् एकमेवाद्वयं ब्रह्म नेह नानास्ति किंचन
verses.transliteration: satsamṛddhaṃ svataḥsiddhaṃ śuddhaṃ buddhamanīdṛśam & ekamevādvayaṃ brahma neha nānāsti kiṃcana
verses.translation: There is only Brahman, the One without a second, the Reality, the One without a second, the Reality, effulgent, self-existent, pure, intelligent, and unlike anything finite; there is no duality whatsoever in It.

verses.sanskrit: निरस्तरागा विनिरस्तभोगाः शान्ताः सुदान्ता यतयो महान्तः विज्ञाय तत्त्वं परमेतदन्ते प्राप्ताः परां निर्वृतिम् आत्मयोगात्
verses.transliteration: nirastarāgā vinirastabhogāḥ śāntāḥ sudāntā yatayo mahāntaḥ & vijñāya tattvaṃ parametadante prāptāḥ parāṃ nirvṛtim ātmayogāt
verses.translation: High-souled Sannyasins who have got rid of all attachment and discarded all sense-enjoyments, and who are serene and perfectly restrained, realise this Supreme Truth and at the end attain the Supreme Bliss through their Self-realisation.

verses.sanskrit: भवानपीदं परतत्त्वम् आत्मनः स्वरूपमानन्दघनं विचार्य विधूय मोहं स्वमनःप्रकल्पितं मुक्तः कृतार्थो भवतु प्रबुद्धः
verses.transliteration: bhavānapīdaṃ paratattvam ātmanaḥ svarūpamānandaghanaṃ vicārya & vidhūya mohaṃ svamanaḥprakalpitaṃ muktaḥ kṛtārtho bhavatu prabuddhaḥ
verses.translation: Thou, too, discriminate this Supreme Truth, the real nature of the Self, which is Bliss undiluted, and shaking off thy delusion created by thy own mind, be free and illumined, and attain the consummation of thy life.

verses.sanskrit: समाधिना साधुविनिश्चलात्मना पश्यात्मतत्त्वं स्फुटबोधचक्षुषा निःसंशयं सम्यगवेक्षितश्चेच् छ्रुतः पदार्थो न पुनर्विकल्प्यते
verses.transliteration: samādhinā sādhuviniścalātmanā paśyātmatattvaṃ sphuṭabodhacakṣuṣā & niḥsaṃśayaṃ samyagavekṣitaścec chrutaḥ padārtho na punarvikalpyate
verses.translation: Through the Samadhi in which the mind has been perfectly stilled, visualise the Truth of the Self with the eye of clear realisation. If the meaning of the (Scriptural) words heard from the Guru is perfectly and indubitably discerned, then it can lead to no more doubt.

verses.sanskrit: स्वस्याविद्याबन्धसंबन्धमोक्षात् सत्यज्ञानानन्दरूपात्मलब्धौ शास्त्रं युक्तिर्देशिकोक्तिः प्रमाणं चान्तःसिद्धा स्वानुभूतिः प्रमाणम्
verses.transliteration: svasyāvidyābandhasaṃbandhamokṣāt satyajñānānandarūpātmalabdhau & śāstraṃ yuktirdeśikoktiḥ pramāṇaṃ cāntaḥsiddhā svānubhūtiḥ pramāṇam
verses.translation: In the realisation of the Atman, the Existence-Knowledge-Bliss Absolute, through the breaking of ones connection with the bondage of Avidya or ignorance, the Scriptures, reasoning and the words of the Guru are the proofs, while ones own experience earned by concentrating the mind is another proof.

verses.sanskrit: बन्धो मोक्षश्च तृप्तिश्च चिन्तारोग्यक्षुदादयः स्वेनैव वेद्या यज्ज्ञानं परेषामानुमानिकम्
verses.transliteration: bandho mokṣaśca tṛptiśca cintārogyakṣudādayaḥ & svenaiva vedyā yajjñānaṃ pareṣāmānumānikam
verses.translation: Bondage, liberation, satisfaction, anxiety, recovery from illness, hunger and other such things are known only to the man concerned, and knowledge of these to others is a mere inference.

verses.sanskrit: तटस्थिता बोधयन्ति गुरवः श्रुतयो यथा प्रज्ञयैव तरेद्विद्वानीश्वरानुगृहीतया
verses.transliteration: taṭasthitā bodhayanti guravaḥ śrutayo yathā & prajñayaiva taredvidvānīśvarānugṛhītayā
verses.translation: The Gurus as well as the Shrutis instruct the disciple, standing aloof; while the man of realisation crosses (Avidya) through Illumination alone, backed by the grace of God.

verses.sanskrit: स्वानुभूत्या स्वयं ज्ञात्वा स्वमात्मानमखण्डितम् संसिद्धः संमुखं तिष्ठेन्निर्विकल्पात्मनात्मनि
verses.transliteration: svānubhūtyā svayaṃ jñātvā svamātmānamakhaṇḍitam & saṃsiddhaḥ saṃmukhaṃ tiṣṭhennirvikalpātmanātmani
verses.translation: Himself knowing his indivisible Self through his own realisation and thus becoming perfect, a man should stand face to face with the Atman, with his mind free from dualistic ideas.

verses.sanskrit: वेदान्तसिद्धान्तनिरुक्तिरेषा ब्रह्मैव जीवः सकलं जगच्च अखण्डरूपस्थितिर् एव मोक्षो ब्रह्माद्वितीये श्रुतयः प्रमाणम्
verses.transliteration: vedāntasiddhāntaniruktireṣā brahmaiva jīvaḥ sakalaṃ jagacca & akhaṇḍarūpasthitir eva mokṣo brahmādvitīye śrutayaḥ pramāṇam
verses.translation: The verdict of all discussions on the Vedanta is that the Jiva and the whole universe are nothing but Brahman, and that liberation means abiding in Brahman, the indivisible Entity. While the Shrutis themselves are authority (for the statement) that Brahman is One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: इति गुरुवचनाच्छ्रुतिप्रमाणात् परमवगम्य सतत्त्वम् आत्मयुक्त्या प्रशमितकरणः समाहितात्मा क्वचिदचलाकृतिर् आत्मनिष्ठतो ऽभूत्
verses.transliteration: iti guruvacanācchrutipramāṇāt paramavagamya satattvam ātmayuktyā & praśamitakaraṇaḥ samāhitātmā kvacidacalākṛtir ātmaniṣṭhato 'bhūt
verses.translation: Realising, at a blessed moment, the Supreme Truth through the above instructions of the Guru, the authority of the Scriptures and his own reasoning, with his senses quieted and the mind concentrated, (the disciple) became immovable in form and perfectly established in the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: किंचित् कालं समाधाय परे ब्रह्मणि मानसम् उत्थाय परमानन्दादिदं वचनमब्रवीत्
verses.transliteration: kiṃcit kālaṃ samādhāya pare brahmaṇi mānasam & utthāya paramānandādidaṃ vacanamabravīt
verses.translation: Concentrating the mind for some time on the Supreme Brahman, he rose, and out of supreme bliss spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धिर्विनष्टा गलिता प्रवृत्तिः ब्रह्मात्मनोर् एकतयाधिगत्या इदं न जाने ऽप्य् अनिदं न जाने किं वा कियद्वा सुखमस्त्य् अपारम्
verses.transliteration: buddhirvinaṣṭā galitā pravṛttiḥ brahmātmanor ekatayādhigatyā & idaṃ na jāne 'py anidaṃ na jāne kiṃ vā kiyadvā sukhamasty apāram
verses.translation: My mind has vanished, and all its activities have melted, by realising the identity of the Self and Brahman; I do not know either this or not-this; nor what or how much the boundless Bliss (of Samadhi) is !

verses.sanskrit: वाचा वक्तुमशक्यम् एव मनसा मन्तुं न वा शक्यते स्वानन्दामृतपूरपूरितपरब्रह्माम्बुधेर्वैभवम् अम्भोराशिविशीर्णवार्षिकशिलाभावं भजन्मे मनो यस्यांशांशलवे विलीनमधुनानन्दात्मना निर्वृतम्
verses.transliteration: vācā vaktumaśakyam eva manasā mantuṃ na vā śakyate svānandāmṛtapūrapūritaparabrahmāmbudhervaibhavam & ambhorāśiviśīrṇavārṣikaśilābhāvaṃ bhajanme mano yasyāṃśāṃśalave vilīnamadhunānandātmanā nirvṛtam
verses.translation: The majesty of the ocean of Supreme Brahman, replete with the swell of the nectar-like Bliss of the Self, is verily impossible to express in speech, nor can it be conceived by the mind in an infinitesimal fraction of which my mind melted like a hailstone getting merged in the ocean, and is now satisfied with that Essence of Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: क्व गतं केन वा नीतं कुत्र लीनमिदं जगत् अधुनैव मया दृष्टं नास्ति किं महदद्भुतम्
verses.transliteration: kva gataṃ kena vā nītaṃ kutra līnamidaṃ jagat & adhunaiva mayā dṛṣṭaṃ nāsti kiṃ mahadadbhutam
verses.translation: Where is the universe gone, by whom is it removed, and where is it merged ? It was just now seen by me, and has it ceased to exist ? It is passing strange !

verses.sanskrit: किं हेयं किमुपादेयं किमन्यत्किं विलक्षणम् अखण्डानन्दपीयूषपूर्णे ब्रह्ममहार्णवे
verses.transliteration: kiṃ heyaṃ kimupādeyaṃ kimanyatkiṃ vilakṣaṇam & akhaṇḍānandapīyūṣapūrṇe brahmamahārṇave
verses.translation: In the ocean of Brahman filled with the nectar of Absolute Bliss, what is to be shunned and what accepted, what is other (than oneself) and what different ?

verses.sanskrit: न किंचिद् अत्र पश्यामि न शृणोमि न वेद्म्य् अहम् स्वात्मनैव सदानन्दरूपेणास्मि विलक्षणः
verses.transliteration: na kiṃcid atra paśyāmi na śṛṇomi na vedmy aham & svātmanaiva sadānandarūpeṇāsmi vilakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: I neither see nor hear nor know anything in this. I simply exist as the Self, the eternal Bliss, distinct from everything else.

verses.sanskrit: नमो नमस्ते गुरवे महात्मने विमुक्तसङ्गाय सदुत्तमाय नित्याद्वयानन्दरसस्वरूपिणे भूम्ने सदापारदयाम्बुधाम्ने
verses.transliteration: namo namaste gurave mahātmane vimuktasaṅgāya saduttamāya & nityādvayānandarasasvarūpiṇe bhūmne sadāpāradayāmbudhāmne
verses.translation: Repeated salutations to thee, O noble Teacher, who art devoid of attachment, the best among the good souls and the embodiment of the essence of Eternal Bliss, the One without a second who art infinite and ever the boundless ocean of mercy:

verses.sanskrit: यत्कटाक्षशशिसान्द्रचन्द्रिका- पातधूतभवतापजश्रमः प्राप्तवानहमखण्डवैभवा- नन्दम् आत्मपदमक्षयं क्षणात्
verses.transliteration: yatkaṭākṣaśaśisāndracandrikā- pātadhūtabhavatāpajaśramaḥ & prāptavānahamakhaṇḍavaibhavā- nandam ātmapadamakṣayaṃ kṣaṇāt
verses.translation: Whose glance, like the shower of concentrated moonbeams, has removed my exhaustion brought on by the afflictions of the world, and in a moment admitted me to the undecaying status of the Atman, the Bliss of infinite majesty !

verses.sanskrit: धन्यो ऽहं कृतकृत्यो ऽहं विमुक्तो ऽहं भवग्रहात् नित्यानन्दस्वरूपो ऽहं पूर्णो ऽहं त्वदनुग्रहात्
verses.transliteration: dhanyo 'haṃ kṛtakṛtyo 'haṃ vimukto 'haṃ bhavagrahāt & nityānandasvarūpo 'haṃ pūrṇo 'haṃ tvadanugrahāt
verses.translation: Blessed am I; I have attained the consummation of my life, and am free from the clutches of transmigration; I am the Essence of Eternal Bliss, I am infinite all through thy mercy !

verses.sanskrit: असङ्गो ऽहम् अनङ्गो ऽहम् अलिङ्गो ऽहम् अभङ्गुरः प्रशान्तो ऽहम् अनन्तो ऽहम् अमलो ऽहं चिरन्तनः
verses.transliteration: asaṅgo 'ham anaṅgo 'ham aliṅgo 'ham abhaṅguraḥ & praśānto 'ham ananto 'ham amalo 'haṃ cirantanaḥ
verses.translation: I am unattached, I am disembodied, I am free from the subtle body, and undecaying, I am serene, I am infinite, I am taintless and eternal.

verses.sanskrit: अकर्ताहमभोक्ताहमविकारो ऽहम् अक्रियः शुद्धबोधस्वरूपो ऽहं केवलो ऽहं सदाशिवः
verses.transliteration: akartāhamabhoktāhamavikāro 'ham akriyaḥ & śuddhabodhasvarūpo 'haṃ kevalo 'haṃ sadāśivaḥ
verses.translation: I am not the doer, I am not the experiencer, I am changeless and beyond activity; I am the essence of Pure Knowledge; I am Absolute and identified with Eternal Good.

verses.sanskrit: द्रष्टुः श्रोतुर्वक्तुः कर्तुर्भोक्तुर्विभिन्न एवाहम् नित्यनिरन्तरनिष्क्रियनिःसीमासङ्गपूर्णबोधात्मा
verses.transliteration: draṣṭuḥ śroturvaktuḥ karturbhokturvibhinna evāham & nityanirantaraniṣkriyaniḥsīmāsaṅgapūrṇabodhātmā
verses.translation: I am indeed different from the seer, listener, speaker, doer and experiencer; I am the essence of Knowledge, eternal, without any break, beyond activity, limitless, unattached and infinite.

verses.sanskrit: नाहमिदं नाहमदो ऽप्य् उभयोर् अवभासकं परं शुद्धम् बाह्याभ्यन्तरशून्यं पूर्णं ब्रह्माद्वितीयमेवाहम्
verses.transliteration: nāhamidaṃ nāhamado 'py ubhayor avabhāsakaṃ paraṃ śuddham & bāhyābhyantaraśūnyaṃ pūrṇaṃ brahmādvitīyamevāham
verses.translation: I am neither, this nor that, but the Supreme, the illuminer of both; I am indeed Brahman, the One without a second, pure, devoid of interior or exterior and infinite.

verses.sanskrit: निरुपममनादितत्त्वं त्वमहमिदमद इति कल्पनादूरम् नित्यानन्दैकरसं सत्यं ब्रह्माद्वितीयमेवाहम्
verses.transliteration: nirupamamanāditattvaṃ tvamahamidamada iti kalpanādūram & nityānandaikarasaṃ satyaṃ brahmādvitīyamevāham
verses.translation: I am indeed Brahman, the One without a second, matchless, the Reality that has no beginning, beyond such imagination as thou or I, or this or that, the Essence of Eternal Bliss, the Truth.

verses.sanskrit: नारायणो ऽहं नरकान्तको ऽहं पुरान्तको ऽहं पुरुषो ऽहम् ईशः अखण्डबोधो ऽहम् अशेषसाक्षी निरीश्वरो ऽहं निरहं च निर्ममः
verses.transliteration: nārāyaṇo 'haṃ narakāntako 'haṃ purāntako 'haṃ puruṣo 'ham īśaḥ & akhaṇḍabodho 'ham aśeṣasākṣī nirīśvaro 'haṃ nirahaṃ ca nirmamaḥ
verses.translation: I am Narayana, the slayer of Naraka; I am the destroyer of Tripura, the Supreme Being, the Ruler; I am knowledge Absolute, the Witness of everything; I have no other Ruler but myself, I am devoid of the ideas of "I and "mine".

verses.sanskrit: सर्वेषु भूतेष्व् अहम् एव संस्थितो ज्ञानात्मनान्तर्बहिराश्रयः सन् भोक्ता च भोग्यं स्वयम् एव सर्वं यद्यत्पृथग्दृष्टमिदन्तया पुरा
verses.transliteration: sarveṣu bhūteṣv aham eva saṃsthito jñānātmanāntarbahirāśrayaḥ san & bhoktā ca bhogyaṃ svayam eva sarvaṃ yadyatpṛthagdṛṣṭamidantayā purā
verses.translation: I alone reside as knowledge in all beings, being their internal and external support. I myself am the experiencer and all that is experienced whatever I looked upon as "this" or the not-Self previously.

verses.sanskrit: मय्यखण्डसुखाम्भोधौ बहुधा विश्ववीचयः उत्पद्यन्ते विलीयन्ते मायामारुतविभ्रमात्
verses.transliteration: mayyakhaṇḍasukhāmbhodhau bahudhā viśvavīcayaḥ & utpadyante vilīyante māyāmārutavibhramāt
verses.translation: In me, the ocean of Infinite Bliss, the waves of the universe are created and destroyed by the playing of the wind of Maya.

verses.sanskrit: स्थुलादिभावा मयि कल्पिता भ्रमाद् आरोपितानुस्फुरणेन लोकैः काले यथा कल्पकवत्सराय- णर्त्वादयो निष्कलनिर्विकल्पे
verses.transliteration: sthulādibhāvā mayi kalpitā bhramād āropitānusphuraṇena lokaiḥ & kāle yathā kalpakavatsarāya- ṇartvādayo niṣkalanirvikalpe
verses.translation: Such ideas as gross (or subtle) are erroneously imagined in me by people through the manifestation of things superimposed just as in the indivisible and absolute time, cycles, years, half-years, seasons, etc., are imagined.

verses.sanskrit: आरोपितं नाश्रयदूषकं भवेत् कदापि मूढैर् अतिदोषदूषितैः नार्द्रिकरोत्यूषरभूमिभागं मरीचिकावारि महाप्रवाहः
verses.transliteration: āropitaṃ nāśrayadūṣakaṃ bhavet kadāpi mūḍhair atidoṣadūṣitaiḥ & nārdrikarotyūṣarabhūmibhāgaṃ marīcikāvāri mahāpravāhaḥ
verses.translation: That which is superimposed by the grossly ignorant fools can never taint the substratum: The great rush of waters observed in a mirage never wets the desert tracts.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशवल्लेपविदूरगो ऽहं आदित्यवद्भास्यविलक्षणो ऽहम् अहार्यवन्नित्यविनिश्चलो ऽहं अम्भोधिवत्पारविवर्जितो ऽहम्
verses.transliteration: ākāśavallepavidūrago 'haṃ ādityavadbhāsyavilakṣaṇo 'ham & ahāryavannityaviniścalo 'haṃ ambhodhivatpāravivarjito 'ham
verses.translation: I am beyond contamination like the sky; I am distinct from things illumined, like the sun; I am always motionless like the mountain; I am limitless like the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: न मे देहेन संबन्धो मेघेनेव विहायसः अतः कुतो मे तद्धर्मा जाक्रत्स्वप्नसुषुप्तयः
verses.transliteration: na me dehena saṃbandho megheneva vihāyasaḥ & ataḥ kuto me taddharmā jākratsvapnasuṣuptayaḥ
verses.translation: I have no connection with the body, as the sky with clouds; so how can the states of wakefulness, dream and profound sleep, which are attributes of the body, affect me ?

verses.sanskrit: उपाधिरायाति स एव गच्छति स एव कर्माणि करोति भुङ्क्ते स एव जीर्यन् म्रियते सदाहं कुलाद्रिवन्निश्चल एव संस्थितः
verses.transliteration: upādhirāyāti sa eva gacchati sa eva karmāṇi karoti bhuṅkte & sa eva jīryan mriyate sadāhaṃ kulādrivanniścala eva saṃsthitaḥ
verses.translation: For me who am always the same and devoid of parts, there is neither engaging in work nor cessation from it. How can that which is One, concentrated, without break and infinite like the sky, ever strive ?

verses.sanskrit: न मे प्रवृत्तिर्न च मे निवृत्तिः सदैकरूपस्य निरंशकस्य एकात्मको यो निविडो निरन्तरो व्योमेव पूर्णः स कथं नु चेष्टते
verses.transliteration: na me pravṛttirna ca me nivṛttiḥ sadaikarūpasya niraṃśakasya & ekātmako yo niviḍo nirantaro vyomeva pūrṇaḥ sa kathaṃ nu ceṣṭate
verses.translation: How can there be merits and demerits for me, who am without organs, without mind, changeless, and formless who am the realisation of Bliss Absolute ? The Shruti also mentions this in the passage "Not touched", etc.

verses.sanskrit: पुण्यानि पापानि निरिन्द्रियस्य निश्चेतसो निर्विकृतेर्निराकृतेः कुतो ममाखण्डसुखानुभूतेः ब्रूते ह्यनन्वागतमित्य् अपि श्रुतिः
verses.transliteration: puṇyāni pāpāni nirindriyasya niścetaso nirvikṛternirākṛteḥ & kuto mamākhaṇḍasukhānubhūteḥ brūte hyananvāgatamity api śrutiḥ
verses.translation: If heat or cold, or good or evil, happens to touch the shadow of a mans body, it affects not in the least the man himself, who is distinct from the shadow.

verses.sanskrit: छायया स्पृष्टमुष्णं वा शीतं वा सुष्ठु दुःष्ठु वा न स्पृशत्य् एव यत् किंचित् पुरुषं तद्विलक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: chāyayā spṛṣṭamuṣṇaṃ vā śītaṃ vā suṣṭhu duḥṣṭhu vā & na spṛśaty eva yat kiṃcit puruṣaṃ tadvilakṣaṇam
verses.translation: The properties of things observed do not affect the Witness, which is distinct from the, changeless and indifferent as the properties of a room (do not affect) the lamp (that illumines it).

verses.sanskrit: न साक्षिणं साक्ष्यधर्माः संस्पृशन्ति विलक्षणम् अविकारमुदासीनं गृहधर्माः प्रदीपवत्
verses.transliteration: na sākṣiṇaṃ sākṣyadharmāḥ saṃspṛśanti vilakṣaṇam & avikāramudāsīnaṃ gṛhadharmāḥ pradīpavat
verses.translation: As the sun is a mere witness of mens actions, as fire burns everything without distinction, and as the rope is related to a thing superimposed on it, so am I, the unchangeable Self, the Intelligence Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: रवेर्यथा कर्मणि साक्षिभावो वन्हेर्यथा दाहनियामकत्वम् रज्जोर्यथारोपितवस्तुसङ्गः तथैव कूटस्थचिदात्मनो मे
verses.transliteration: raveryathā karmaṇi sākṣibhāvo vanheryathā dāhaniyāmakatvam & rajjoryathāropitavastusaṅgaḥ tathaiva kūṭasthacidātmano me
verses.translation: I neither do nor make others do any action; I neither enjoy nor make others enjoy; I neither see nor make others see; I am that Self-effulgent, Transcendent Atman.

verses.sanskrit: कर्तापि वा कारयितापि नाहं भोक्तापि वा भोजयितापि नाहम् द्रष्टापि वा दर्शयितापि नाहं सो ऽहं स्वयंज्योतिरनीदृगात्मा
verses.transliteration: kartāpi vā kārayitāpi nāhaṃ bhoktāpi vā bhojayitāpi nāham & draṣṭāpi vā darśayitāpi nāhaṃ so 'haṃ svayaṃjyotiranīdṛgātmā
verses.translation: When the supervening adjunct (Upadhi) is moving, the resulting movement of the reflection is ascribed by fools to the object reflected, such as the sun, which is free from activity (and they think) "I am the doer", "I am the experiencer", "I am killed, oh, alas!"

verses.sanskrit: चलत्युपाधौ प्रतिबिम्बलौल्यम् औपाधिकं मूढधियो नयन्ति स्वबिम्बभूतं रविवद्विनिष्क्रियं कर्तास्मि भोक्तास्मि हतो ऽस्मि हेति
verses.transliteration: calatyupādhau pratibimbalaulyam aupādhikaṃ mūḍhadhiyo nayanti & svabimbabhūtaṃ ravivadviniṣkriyaṃ kartāsmi bhoktāsmi hato 'smi heti
verses.translation: Let this inert body drop down in water or on land. I am not touched by its properties, like the sky by the properties of the jar.

verses.sanskrit: जले वापि स्थले वापि लुठत्वेष जडात्मकः नाहं विलिप्ये तद्धर्मैर् घटधर्मैर् नभो यथा
verses.transliteration: jale vāpi sthale vāpi luṭhatveṣa jaḍātmakaḥ & nāhaṃ vilipye taddharmair ghaṭadharmair nabho yathā
verses.translation: The passing states of the Buddhi, such as agency, experience, cunning, drunkenness, dullness, bondage and freedom, are never in reality in the Self, the Supreme Brahman, the Absolute, the one without a second.

verses.sanskrit: कर्तृत्वभोक्तृत्वखलत्वमत्तता- जडत्वबद्धत्वविमुक्ततादयः बुद्धेर्विकल्पा न तु सन्ति वस्तुतः स्वस्मिन् परे ब्रह्मणि केवले ऽद्वये
verses.transliteration: kartṛtvabhoktṛtvakhalatvamattatā- jaḍatvabaddhatvavimuktatādayaḥ & buddhervikalpā na tu santi vastutaḥ svasmin pare brahmaṇi kevale 'dvaye
verses.translation: Let there be changes in the Prakriti in ten, a hundred, or a thousand ways, what have I, the unattached Knowledge Absolute, got to do with them ? Never do the clouds touch the sky !

verses.sanskrit: सन्तु विकाराः प्रकृतेर्दशधा शतधा सहस्रधा वापि किं मे ऽसङ्गचितस्तैर् न घनः क्वचिदम्बरं स्पृशति
verses.transliteration: santu vikārāḥ prakṛterdaśadhā śatadhā sahasradhā vāpi & kiṃ me 'saṅgacitastair na ghanaḥ kvacidambaraṃ spṛśati
verses.translation: I am verily that Brahman, the One without a second, which is like the sky, subtle, without beginning or end, in which the whole universe from the Undifferentiated down to the gross body, appears merely as a shadow.

verses.sanskrit: अव्यक्तादिस्थूलपर्यन्तमेतत् विश्व यत्राभासमात्रं प्रतीतम् व्योमप्रख्यं सूक्ष्ममाद्यन्तहीनं ब्रह्माद्वैतं यत्तदेवाहमस्मि
verses.transliteration: avyaktādisthūlaparyantametat viśva yatrābhāsamātraṃ pratītam & vyomaprakhyaṃ sūkṣmamādyantahīnaṃ brahmādvaitaṃ yattadevāhamasmi
verses.translation: I am verily that Brahman, the One without a second, which is the support of all, which illumines all things, which has infinite forms, is omnipresent, devoid of multiplicity, eternal, pure, unmoved and absolute.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाधारं सर्ववस्तुप्रकाशं सर्वाकारं सर्वगं सर्वशून्यम् नित्यं शुद्धं निश्चलं निर्विकल्पं ब्रह्माद्वैतं यत्तदेवाहमस्मि
verses.transliteration: sarvādhāraṃ sarvavastuprakāśaṃ sarvākāraṃ sarvagaṃ sarvaśūnyam & nityaṃ śuddhaṃ niścalaṃ nirvikalpaṃ brahmādvaitaṃ yattadevāhamasmi
verses.translation: I am verily that Brahman, the One without a second, which transcends the endless differentiations of Maya, which is the inmost essence of all, is beyond the range of consciousness, and which is Truth, Knowledge, Infinity and Bliss Absolute.

verses.sanskrit: यत्प्रत्यस्ताशेषमायाविशेषं प्रत्यग्रूपं प्रत्ययागम्यमानम् सत्यज्ञानानन्तमानन्दरूपं ब्रह्माद्वैतं यत्तदेवाहमस्मि
verses.transliteration: yatpratyastāśeṣamāyāviśeṣaṃ pratyagrūpaṃ pratyayāgamyamānam & satyajñānānantamānandarūpaṃ brahmādvaitaṃ yattadevāhamasmi
verses.translation: I am without activity, changeless, without parts, formless, absolute, eternal, without any other support, the One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: निष्क्रियो ऽस्म्य् अविकारो ऽस्मि निष्कलो ऽस्मि निराकृतिः निर्विकल्पो ऽस्मि नित्यो ऽस्मि निरालम्बो ऽस्मि निर्द्वयः
verses.transliteration: niṣkriyo 'smy avikāro 'smi niṣkalo 'smi nirākṛtiḥ & nirvikalpo 'smi nityo 'smi nirālambo 'smi nirdvayaḥ
verses.translation: I am the Universal, I am the All, I am transcendent, the One without a second. I am Absolute and Infinite Knowledge, I am Bliss and indivisible.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मको ऽहं सर्वो ऽहं सर्वातीतो ऽहम् अद्वयः केवलाक्षण्डबोधो ऽहम् आनन्दो ऽहं निरन्तरः
verses.transliteration: sarvātmako 'haṃ sarvo 'haṃ sarvātīto 'ham advayaḥ & kevalākṣaṇḍabodho 'ham ānando 'haṃ nirantaraḥ
verses.translation: This splendour of the sovereignty of Self-effulgence I have received by virtue of the supreme majesty of thy grace. Salutations to thee, O glorious, noble-minded Teacher, salutations again and again !

verses.sanskrit: स्वाराज्यसाम्राज्यविभूतिरेषा भवत्कृपाश्रीमहिमप्रसादात् प्राप्ता मया श्रीगुरवे महात्मने नमो नमस्ते ऽस्तु पुनर्नमो ऽस्तु
verses.transliteration: svārājyasāmrājyavibhūtireṣā bhavatkṛpāśrīmahimaprasādāt & prāptā mayā śrīgurave mahātmane namo namaste 'stu punarnamo 'stu
verses.translation: O Master, thou hast out of sheer grace awakened me from sleep and completely saved me, who was wandering, in an interminable dream, in a forest of birth, decay and death created by illusion, being tormented day after day by countless afflictions, and sorely troubled by the tiger of egoism.

verses.sanskrit: महास्वप्ने मायाकृतजनिजरामृत्युगहने भ्रमन्तं क्लिश्यन्तं बहुलतरतापैर् अनुदिनम् अहंकारव्याघ्रव्यथितमिममत्यन्तकृपया प्रबोध्य प्रस्वापात्परमवितवान्मामसि गुरो
verses.transliteration: mahāsvapne māyākṛtajanijarāmṛtyugahane bhramantaṃ kliśyantaṃ bahulataratāpair anudinam & ahaṃkāravyāghravyathitamimamatyantakṛpayā prabodhya prasvāpātparamavitavānmāmasi guro
verses.translation: Salutations to thee, O Prince of Teachers, thou unnamable Greatness, that art ever the same and dost manifest thyself as this universe thee I salute.

verses.sanskrit: नमस्तस्मै सदैकस्मै कस्मैचिन्महसे नमः यदेतद्विश्वरूपेण राजते गुरुराज ते
verses.transliteration: namastasmai sadaikasmai kasmaicinmahase namaḥ & yadetadviśvarūpeṇa rājate gururāja te
verses.translation: Seeing the worthy disciple, who had attained the Bliss of the self, realised the Truth and was glad at heart, thus prostrating himself, that noble, ideal Teacher again addressed the following excellent words:

verses.sanskrit: इति नतमवलोक्य शिष्यवर्यं समधिगतात्मसुखं प्रबुद्धतत्त्वम् प्रमुदितहृदयं स देशिकेन्द्रः पुनरिदमाह वचः परं महात्मा
verses.transliteration: iti natamavalokya śiṣyavaryaṃ samadhigatātmasukhaṃ prabuddhatattvam & pramuditahṛdayaṃ sa deśikendraḥ punaridamāha vacaḥ paraṃ mahātmā
verses.translation: The universe is an unbroken series of perceptions of Brahman; hence it is in all respects nothing but Brahman. See this with the eye of illumination and a serene mind, under all circumstances. Is one who has eyes ever found to see all around anything else but forms? Similarly, what is there except Brahman to engage the intellect of a man of realisation ?

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मप्रत्ययसन्ततिर्जगदतो ब्रह्मैव तत्सर्वतः पश्याध्यात्मदृशा प्रशान्तमनसा सर्वास्ववस्थास्व् अपि रूपादन्यदवेक्षितं किमभितश्चक्षुष्मतां दृश्यते तद्वद्ब्रह्मविदः सतः किमपरं बुद्धेर्विहारास्पदम्
verses.transliteration: brahmapratyayasantatirjagadato brahmaiva tatsarvataḥ paśyādhyātmadṛśā praśāntamanasā sarvāsvavasthāsv api & rūpādanyadavekṣitaṃ kimabhitaścakṣuṣmatāṃ dṛśyate tadvadbrahmavidaḥ sataḥ kimaparaṃ buddhervihārāspadam
verses.translation: What wise man would discard that enjoyment of Supreme Bliss and revel in things unsubstantial ? When the exceedingly charming moon is shining, who would wish to look at a painted moon ?

verses.sanskrit: कस्तां परानन्दरसानुभूति- मृत्सृज्य शून्येषु रमेत विद्वान् चन्द्रे महाल्हादिनि दीप्यमाने चित्रेन्दुमालोकयितुं क इच्छेत्
verses.transliteration: kastāṃ parānandarasānubhūti- mṛtsṛjya śūnyeṣu rameta vidvān & candre mahālhādini dīpyamāne citrendumālokayituṃ ka icchet
verses.translation: From the perception of unreal things there is neither satisfaction nor a cessation of misery. Therefore, being satisfied with the realisation of the Bliss Absolute, the One without a second, live happily in a state of identity with that Reality.

verses.sanskrit: असत्पदार्थानुभवेन किंचिन् न ह्यस्ति तृप्तिर्न च दुःखहानिः तदद्वयानन्दरसानुभूत्या तृप्तः सुखं तिष्ठ सदात्मनिष्ठया
verses.transliteration: asatpadārthānubhavena kiṃcin na hyasti tṛptirna ca duḥkhahāniḥ & tadadvayānandarasānubhūtyā tṛptaḥ sukhaṃ tiṣṭha sadātmaniṣṭhayā
verses.translation: Beholding the Self alone in all circumstances, thinking of the Self, the One without a second, and enjoying the Bliss of the Self, pass thy time, O noble soul !

verses.sanskrit: स्वम् एव सर्वथा पश्यन्मन्यमानः स्वमद्वयम् स्वानन्दमनुभुञ्जानः कालं नय महामते
verses.transliteration: svam eva sarvathā paśyanmanyamānaḥ svamadvayam & svānandamanubhuñjānaḥ kālaṃ naya mahāmate
verses.translation: Dualistic conceptions in the Atman, the Infinite Knowledge, the Absolute, are like imagining castles in the air. Therefore, always identifying thyself with the Bliss Absolute, the One without a second, and thereby attaining Supreme Peace, remain quiet.

verses.sanskrit: अखण्डबोधात्मनि निर्विकल्पे विकल्पनं व्योम्नि पुरप्रकल्पनम् तदद्वयानन्दमयात्मना सदा शान्तिं परामेत्य भजस्व मौनम्
verses.transliteration: akhaṇḍabodhātmani nirvikalpe vikalpanaṃ vyomni puraprakalpanam & tadadvayānandamayātmanā sadā śāntiṃ parāmetya bhajasva maunam
verses.translation: To the sage who has realised Brahman, the mind, which is the cause of unreal fancies, becomes perfectly tranquil. This verily is his state of quietude, in which, identified with Brahman, he has constant enjoyment of the Bliss Absolute, the One without a second.

verses.sanskrit: तूष्णीमवस्था परमोपशान्तिः बुद्धेरसत्कल्पविकल्पहेतोः ब्रह्मात्मन ब्रह्मविदो महात्मनो यत्राद्वयानन्दसुखं निरन्तरम्
verses.transliteration: tūṣṇīmavasthā paramopaśāntiḥ buddherasatkalpavikalpahetoḥ & brahmātmana brahmavido mahātmano yatrādvayānandasukhaṃ nirantaram
verses.translation: To the man who has realised his own nature, and drinks the undiluted Bliss of the Self, there is nothing more exhilarating than the quietude that comes of a state of desirelessness.

verses.sanskrit: नास्ति निर्वासनान्मौनात्परं सुखकृदुत्तमम् विज्ञातात्मस्वरूपस्य स्वानन्दरसपायिनः
verses.transliteration: nāsti nirvāsanānmaunātparaṃ sukhakṛduttamam & vijñātātmasvarūpasya svānandarasapāyinaḥ
verses.translation: The illumined sage, whose only pleasure is in the Self, ever lives at ease, whether going or staying, sitting or lying, or in any other condition.

verses.sanskrit: गच्छंस्तिष्ठन्नुपविशञ्छयानो वान्यथापि वा यथेच्छया वेसेद्विद्वानात्नारामः सदा मुनिः
verses.transliteration: gacchaṃstiṣṭhannupaviśañchayāno vānyathāpi vā & yathecchayā vesedvidvānātnārāmaḥ sadā muniḥ
verses.translation: The noble soul who has perfectly realised the Truth, and whose mind-functions meet with no obstruction, no more depends upon conditions of place, time, posture, direction, moral disciplines, objects of meditation and so forth. What regulative conditions can there be in knowing ones own Self ?

verses.sanskrit: न देशकालासनदिग्यमादि- लक्ष्याद्यपेक्षाप्रतिबद्धवृत्तेः संसिद्धतत्त्वस्य महात्मनो ऽस्ति स्ववेदने का नियमाद्यवस्था
verses.transliteration: na deśakālāsanadigyamādi- lakṣyādyapekṣāpratibaddhavṛtteḥ & saṃsiddhatattvasya mahātmano 'sti svavedane kā niyamādyavasthā
verses.translation: To know that this is a jar, what condition, forsooth, is necessary except that the means of knowledge be free from defect, which alone ensures a cognition of the object ?

verses.sanskrit: घटो ऽयमिति विज्ञातुं नियमः को ऽन्ववेक्षते विना प्रमाणसुष्ठुत्वं यस्मिन् सति पदार्थधीः
verses.transliteration: ghaṭo 'yamiti vijñātuṃ niyamaḥ ko 'nvavekṣate & vinā pramāṇasuṣṭhutvaṃ yasmin sati padārthadhīḥ
verses.translation: So this Atman, which is an eternal verity, manifests Itself as soon as the right means of knowledge is present, and does not depend upon either place or time or (internal) purity.

verses.sanskrit: अयमात्मा नित्यसिद्धः प्रमाणे सति भासते न देशं नापि कालं न शुद्धिं वाप्यपेक्षते
verses.transliteration: ayamātmā nityasiddhaḥ pramāṇe sati bhāsate & na deśaṃ nāpi kālaṃ na śuddhiṃ vāpyapekṣate
verses.translation: The consciousness, "I am Devadatta", is independent of circumstances; similar is the case with the realisation of the knower of Brahman that he is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: देवदत्तो ऽहम् इत्य् एतद् विज्ञानं निरपेक्षकम् तद्वद् ब्रह्मविदो ऽप्य् अस्य ब्रह्माहमिति वेदनम्
verses.transliteration: devadatto 'ham ity etad vijñānaṃ nirapekṣakam & tadvad brahmavido 'py asya brahmāhamiti vedanam
verses.translation: What indeed can manifest That whose lustre, like the sun, causes the whole universe unsubstantial, unreal, insignificant to appear at all ?

verses.sanskrit: भानुनेव जगत्सर्वं भासते यस्य तेजसा अनात्मकमसत्तुच्छं किं नु तस्यावभासकम्
verses.transliteration: bhānuneva jagatsarvaṃ bhāsate yasya tejasā & anātmakamasattucchaṃ kiṃ nu tasyāvabhāsakam
verses.translation: What, indeed, can illumine that Eternal Subject by which the Vedas and Puranas and other Scriptures, as well as all beings are endowed with a meaning ?

verses.sanskrit: वेदशास्त्रपुराणानि भूतानि सकलान्य् अपि येनार्थवन्ति तं किन्नु विज्ञातारं प्रकाशयेत्
verses.transliteration: vedaśāstrapurāṇāni bhūtāni sakalāny api & yenārthavanti taṃ kinnu vijñātāraṃ prakāśayet
verses.translation: Here is the Self-effulgent Atman, of infinite power, beyond the range of conditioned knowledge, yet the common experience of all - realising which alone this incomparable knower of Brahman lives his glorious life, freed from bondage.

verses.sanskrit: एष स्वयंज्योतिरनन्तशक्तिः आत्माप्रमेयः सकलानुभूतिः यम् एव विज्ञाय विमुक्तबन्धो जयत्ययं ब्रह्मविदुत्तमोत्तमः
verses.transliteration: eṣa svayaṃjyotiranantaśaktiḥ ātmāprameyaḥ sakalānubhūtiḥ & yam eva vijñāya vimuktabandho jayatyayaṃ brahmaviduttamottamaḥ
verses.translation: Satisfied with undiluted, constant Bliss, he is neither grieved nor elated by sense-objects, is neither attached nor averse to them, but always disports with the Self and takes pleasure therein.

verses.sanskrit: न खिद्यते नो विषयैः प्रमोदते न सज्जते नापि विरज्यते च स्वस्मिन्सदा क्रीडति नन्दति स्वयं निरन्तरानन्दरसेन तृप्तः
verses.transliteration: na khidyate no viṣayaiḥ pramodate na sajjate nāpi virajyate ca & svasminsadā krīḍati nandati svayaṃ nirantarānandarasena tṛptaḥ
verses.translation: A child plays with its toys forgetting hunger and bodily pains; exactly so does the man of realisation take pleasure in the Reality, without ideas of "I" or "mine", and is happy.

verses.sanskrit: क्षुधां देहव्यथां त्यक्त्वा बालः क्रीडति वस्तुनिः तथैव विद्वान् रमते निर्ममो निरहं सुखी
verses.transliteration: kṣudhāṃ dehavyathāṃ tyaktvā bālaḥ krīḍati vastuniḥ & tathaiva vidvān ramate nirmamo nirahaṃ sukhī
verses.translation: Men of realisation have their food without anxiety or humiliation by begging, and their drink from the water of rivers; they live freely and independently, and sleep without fear in cremation grounds or forests; their clothing may be the quarters themselves, which need no washing and drying, or any bark etc., the earth is their bed; they roam in the avenue of the Vedanta; while their pastime is in the Supreme Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: चिन्ताशून्यमदैन्यभैक्षमशनं पानं सरिद्वारिषु स्वातन्त्र्येण निरङ्कुशा स्थितिरभीर्निद्रा श्मशाने वने वस्त्रं क्षालनशोषणादिरहितं दिग्वास्तु शय्या मही संचारो निगमान्तवीथिषु विदां क्रीडा परे ब्रह्मणि
verses.transliteration: cintāśūnyamadainyabhaikṣamaśanaṃ pānaṃ saridvāriṣu svātantryeṇa niraṅkuśā sthitirabhīrnidrā śmaśāne vane & vastraṃ kṣālanaśoṣaṇādirahitaṃ digvāstu śayyā mahī saṃcāro nigamāntavīthiṣu vidāṃ krīḍā pare brahmaṇi
verses.translation: The knower of the Atman, who wears no outward mark and is unattached to external things, rests on this body without identification, and experiences all sorts of sense-objects as they come, through others wish, like a child.

verses.sanskrit: विमानमालम्ब्य शरीरमेतद् भुनक्त्यशेषान्विषयानुपस्थितान् परेच्छया बालवद् आत्मवेत्ता यो ऽव्यक्तलिङ्गो ऽननुषक्तबाह्यः
verses.transliteration: vimānamālambya śarīrametad bhunaktyaśeṣānviṣayānupasthitān & parecchayā bālavad ātmavettā yo 'vyaktaliṅgo 'nanuṣaktabāhyaḥ
verses.translation: Established in the ethereal plane of Absolute Knowledge, he wanders in the world, sometimes like a madman, sometimes like a child and at other times like a ghoul, having no other clothes on his person except the quarters, or sometimes wearing clothes, or perhaps skins at other times.

verses.sanskrit: दिगम्बरो वापि च साम्बरो वा त्वगम्बरो वापि चिदम्बरस्थः उन्मत्तवद्वापि च बालवद्वा पिशाचवद्वापि चरत्यवन्याम्
verses.transliteration: digambaro vāpi ca sāmbaro vā tvagambaro vāpi cidambarasthaḥ & unmattavadvāpi ca bālavadvā piśācavadvāpi caratyavanyām
verses.translation: The sage, living alone, enjoys the sense-objects, being the very embodiment of desirelessness always satisfied with his own Self, and himself present at the All.

verses.sanskrit: कामान्निष्कामरूपी संश्चरत्येकचारो मुनिः स्वात्मनैव सदा तुष्टः स्वयं सर्वात्मना स्थितः
verses.transliteration: kāmānniṣkāmarūpī saṃścaratyekacāro muniḥ & svātmanaiva sadā tuṣṭaḥ svayaṃ sarvātmanā sthitaḥ
verses.translation: Sometimes a fool, sometimes a sage, sometimes possessed of regal splendour; sometimes wandering, sometimes behaving like a motionless python, sometimes wearing a benignant expression; sometimes honoured, sometimes insulted, sometimes unknown thus lives the man of realisation, ever happy with Supreme Bliss.

verses.sanskrit: क्वचिन्मूढो विद्वान् क्वचिद् अपि महाराजविभवः क्वचिद्भ्रान्तः सौम्यः क्वचिदजगराचारकलितः क्वचित्पात्रीभूतः क्वचिदवमतः क्वाप्यविदितः चरत्य् एवं प्राज्ञः सततपरमानन्दसुखितः
verses.transliteration: kvacinmūḍho vidvān kvacid api mahārājavibhavaḥ kvacidbhrāntaḥ saumyaḥ kvacidajagarācārakalitaḥ & kvacitpātrībhūtaḥ kvacidavamataḥ kvāpyaviditaḥ caraty evaṃ prājñaḥ satataparamānandasukhitaḥ
verses.translation: Though without riches, yet ever content; though helpless, yet very powerful, though not enjoying the sense-objects, yet eternally satisfied; though without an exemplar, yet looking upon all with an eye of equality.

verses.sanskrit: निर्धनो ऽपि सदा तुष्टो ऽप्य् असहायो महाबलः नित्यतृप्तो ऽप्य् अभुञ्जानो ऽप्य् असमः समदर्शनः
verses.transliteration: nirdhano 'pi sadā tuṣṭo 'py asahāyo mahābalaḥ & nityatṛpto 'py abhuñjāno 'py asamaḥ samadarśanaḥ
verses.translation: Though doing, yet inactive; though experiencing fruits of past actions, yet untouched by them; though possessed of a body, yet without identification with it; though limited, yet omnipresent is he.

verses.sanskrit: अपि कुर्वन्नकुर्वाणश्चाभोक्ता फलभोग्य् अपि शरीर्यप्यशरीर्येष परिच्छिन्नो ऽपि सर्वगः
verses.transliteration: api kurvannakurvāṇaścābhoktā phalabhogy api & śarīryapyaśarīryeṣa paricchinno 'pi sarvagaḥ
verses.translation: Neither pleasure nor pain, nor good nor evil, ever touches this knower of Brahman, who always lives without the body-idea.

verses.sanskrit: अशरीरं सदा सन्तमिमं ब्रह्मविदं क्वचित् प्रियाप्रिये न स्पृशतस्तथैव च शुभाशुभे
verses.transliteration: aśarīraṃ sadā santamimaṃ brahmavidaṃ kvacit & priyāpriye na spṛśatastathaiva ca śubhāśubhe
verses.translation: Pleasure or pain, or good or evil, affects only him who has connections with the gross body etc., and identifies himself with these. How can good or evil, or their effects, touch the sage who has identified himself with the Reality and thereby shattered his bondage ?

verses.sanskrit: स्थूलादिसंबन्धवतो ऽभिमानिनः सुखं च दुःखं च शुभाशुभे च विध्वस्तबन्धस्य सदात्मनो मुनेः कुतः शुभं वाप्यशुभं फलं वा
verses.transliteration: sthūlādisaṃbandhavato 'bhimāninaḥ sukhaṃ ca duḥkhaṃ ca śubhāśubhe ca & vidhvastabandhasya sadātmano muneḥ kutaḥ śubhaṃ vāpyaśubhaṃ phalaṃ vā
verses.translation: The sun which appears to be, but is not actually, swallowed by Rahu, is said to be swallowed, on account of delusion, by people, not knowing the real nature of the sun.

verses.sanskrit: तमसा ग्रस्तवद्भानादग्रस्तो ऽपि रविर्जनैः ग्रस्त इत्युच्यते भ्रान्त्यां ह्यज्ञात्वा वस्तुलक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: tamasā grastavadbhānādagrasto 'pi ravirjanaiḥ & grasta ityucyate bhrāntyāṃ hyajñātvā vastulakṣaṇam
verses.translation: Similarly, ignorant people look upon the perfect knower of Brahman, who is wholly rid of bondages of the body etc., as possessed of the body, seeing but an appearance of it.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वद्देहादिबन्धेभ्यो विमुक्तं ब्रह्मवित्तमम् पश्यन्ति देहिवन्मूढाः शरीराभासदर्शनात्
verses.transliteration: tadvaddehādibandhebhyo vimuktaṃ brahmavittamam & paśyanti dehivanmūḍhāḥ śarīrābhāsadarśanāt
verses.translation: In reality, however, he rests discarding the body, like the snake its slough; and the body is moved hither and thither by the force of the Prana, just as it listeth.

verses.sanskrit: अहिर्निर्ल्वयनीं वायं मुक्त्वा देहं तु तिष्ठति इतस्ततश्चाल्यमानो यत् किंचित् प्राणवायुना
verses.transliteration: ahirnirlvayanīṃ vāyaṃ muktvā dehaṃ tu tiṣṭhati & itastataścālyamāno yat kiṃcit prāṇavāyunā
verses.translation: As a piece of wood is borne by the current to a high or low ground, so is his body carried on by the momentum of past actions to the varied experience of their fruits, as these present themselves in due course.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रोतसा नीयते दारु यथा निम्नोन्नतस्थलम् दैवेन नीयते देहो यथाकालोपभुक्तिषु
verses.transliteration: strotasā nīyate dāru yathā nimnonnatasthalam & daivena nīyate deho yathākālopabhuktiṣu
verses.translation: The man of realisation, bereft of the body-idea, moves amid sense-enjoyments like a man subject to transmigration, through desires engendered by the Prarabdha work. He himself, however, lives unmoved in the body, like a witness, free from mental oscillations, like the pivot of the potters wheel.

verses.sanskrit: प्रारब्धकर्मपरिकल्पितवासनाभिः संसारिवच्चरति भुक्तिषु मुक्तदेहः सिद्धः स्वयं वसति साक्षिवदत्र तूष्णीं चक्रस्य मूलम् इव कल्पविकल्पशून्यः
verses.transliteration: prārabdhakarmaparikalpitavāsanābhiḥ saṃsārivaccarati bhuktiṣu muktadehaḥ & siddhaḥ svayaṃ vasati sākṣivadatra tūṣṇīṃ cakrasya mūlam iva kalpavikalpaśūnyaḥ
verses.translation: He neither directs the sense-organs to their objects nor detaches them from these, but stays like an unconcerned spectator. And he has not the least regard for the fruits of actions, his mind being thoroughly inebriated with drinking the undiluted elixir of the Bliss of the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: नैवेन्द्रियाणि विषयेषु नियुङ्क्त एष नैवापयुङ्क्त उप्दर्शनलक्षणस्थः नैव क्रियाफलमपीषदवेक्षते स स्वानन्दसान्द्ररसपानसुमत्तचित्तः
verses.transliteration: naivendriyāṇi viṣayeṣu niyuṅkta eṣa naivāpayuṅkta updarśanalakṣaṇasthaḥ & naiva kriyāphalamapīṣadavekṣate sa svānandasāndrarasapānasumattacittaḥ
verses.translation: He who, giving up all considerations of the fitness or otherwise of objects of meditation, lives as the Absolute Atman, is verily Shiva Himself, and he is the best among the knowers of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्यालक्ष्यगतिं त्यक्त्वा यस् तिष्ठेत् केवलात्मना शिव एव स्वयं साक्षादयं ब्रह्मविदुत्तमः
verses.transliteration: lakṣyālakṣyagatiṃ tyaktvā yas tiṣṭhet kevalātmanā & śiva eva svayaṃ sākṣādayaṃ brahmaviduttamaḥ
verses.translation: Through the destruction of limitations, the perfect knower of Brahman is merged in the One Brahman without a second which he had been all along becomes very free even while living, and attains the goal of his life.

verses.sanskrit: जीवन्न् एव सदा मुक्तः कृतार्थो ब्रह्मवित्तमः उपाधिनाशाद्ब्रह्मैव सन् ब्रह्माप्येति निर्द्वयम्
verses.transliteration: jīvann eva sadā muktaḥ kṛtārtho brahmavittamaḥ & upādhināśādbrahmaiva san brahmāpyeti nirdvayam
verses.translation: As an actor, when he puts on the dress of his role, or when he does not, is always a man, so the perfect knower of Brahman is always Brahman and nothing else.

verses.sanskrit: शैलूषो वेषसद्भावा- भावयोश् च यथा पुमान् तथैव ब्रह्मविच् छ्रेष्ठः सदा ब्रह्मैव नापरः
verses.transliteration: śailūṣo veṣasadbhāvā- bhāvayoś ca yathā pumān & tathaiva brahmavic chreṣṭhaḥ sadā brahmaiva nāparaḥ
verses.translation: As an actor remains a man whether wearing the costume of a role or not, the perfect knower of Brahman is always Brahman and nothing else.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र क्वापि विशीर्णं सत्पर्णम् इव तरोर् वपुः पततात् ब्रह्मीभूतस्य यतेः प्राग् एव तच्चिदग्निना दग्धम्
verses.transliteration: yatra kvāpi viśīrṇaṃ satparṇam iva taror vapuḥ patatāt & brahmībhūtasya yateḥ prāg eva taccidagninā dagdham
verses.translation: Let the body of the Sannyasin who has realised his identity with Brahman, wither and fall anywhere like the leaf of a tree, (it is of little consequence to him, for) it has already been burnt by the fire of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: सदात्मनि ब्रह्मणि तिष्ठतो मुनेः पूर्णाद्वयानन्दमयात्मना सदा न देशकालाद्युचितप्रतीक्षा त्वङ्मांसविट्पिण्डविसर्जनाय
verses.transliteration: sadātmani brahmaṇi tiṣṭhato muneḥ pūrṇādvayānandamayātmanā sadā & na deśakālādyucitapratīkṣā tvaṅmāṃsaviṭpiṇḍavisarjanāya
verses.translation: The sage who always lives in the Reality Brahman as Infinite Bliss, the One without a second, does not depend upon the customary considerations of place, time, etc., for giving up this mass of skin, flesh and filth.

verses.sanskrit: देहस्य मोक्षो नो मोक्षो न दण्डस्य कमण्डलोः अविद्याहृदयग्रन्थिमोक्षो मोक्षो यतस्ततः
verses.transliteration: dehasya mokṣo no mokṣo na daṇḍasya kamaṇḍaloḥ & avidyāhṛdayagranthimokṣo mokṣo yatastataḥ
verses.translation: For the giving up of the body is not Liberation, nor that of the staff and the water-bowl; but Liberation consists in the destruction of the hearts knot which is Nescience.

verses.sanskrit: कुल्यायामथ नद्यां वा शिवक्षेत्रे ऽपि चत्वरे पर्णं पतति चेत्तेन तरोः किं नु शुभाशुभम्
verses.transliteration: kulyāyāmatha nadyāṃ vā śivakṣetre 'pi catvare & parṇaṃ patati cettena taroḥ kiṃ nu śubhāśubham
verses.translation: If a leaf falls in a small stream, or a river, or a place consecrated by Shiva, or in a crossing of roads, of what good or evil effect is that to the tree ?

verses.sanskrit: पत्रस्य पुष्पस्य फलस्य नाशवद्- देहेन्द्रियप्राणधियां विनाशः नैवात्मनः स्वस्य सदात्मकस्या- नन्दाकृतेर् वृक्षवद् अस्ति चैषः
verses.transliteration: patrasya puṣpasya phalasya nāśavad- dehendriyaprāṇadhiyāṃ vināśaḥ & naivātmanaḥ svasya sadātmakasyā- nandākṛter vṛkṣavad asti caiṣaḥ
verses.translation: The destruction of the body, organs, Pranas and Buddhi is like that of a leaf or flower or fruit (to a tree). It does not affect the Atman, the Reality, the Embodiment of Bliss which is ones true nature. That survives, like the tree.

verses.sanskrit: प्रज्ञानघन इत्य् आत्मलक्षणं सत्यसूचकम् अनूद्यौपाधिकस्यैव कथयन्ति विनाशनम्
verses.transliteration: prajñānaghana ity ātmalakṣaṇaṃ satyasūcakam & anūdyaupādhikasyaiva kathayanti vināśanam
verses.translation: The Shrutis, by setting forth the real nature of the Atman in the words, "The Embodiment of Knowledge" etc., which indicate Its Reality, speak of the destruction of the apparent limitations merely.

verses.sanskrit: अविनाशी वा अरे ऽयमात्मेति श्रुतिर् आत्मनः प्रब्रवीत्यविनाशित्वं विनश्यत्सु विकारिषु
verses.transliteration: avināśī vā are 'yamātmeti śrutir ātmanaḥ & prabravītyavināśitvaṃ vinaśyatsu vikāriṣu
verses.translation: The Shruti passage, "Verily is this Atman immortal, my dear", mentions the immortality of the Atman in the midst of things perishable and subject to modification.

verses.sanskrit: पाषाणवृक्षतृणधान्यकडङ्कराद्या दग्धा भवन्ति हि मृद् एव यथा तथैव देहेन्द्रियासुमन आदि समस्तदृश्यं ज्ञानाग्निदग्धमुपयाति परात्मभावम्
verses.transliteration: pāṣāṇavṛkṣatṛṇadhānyakaḍaṅkarādyā dagdhā bhavanti hi mṛd eva yathā tathaiva & dehendriyāsumana ādi samastadṛśyaṃ jñānāgnidagdhamupayāti parātmabhāvam
verses.translation: Just as a stone, a tree, grass, paddy, husk, etc., when burnt, are reduced to earth (ashes) only, even so the whole objective universe comprising the body, organs, Pranas, Manas and so forth, are, when burnt by the fire of realisation, reduced to the Supreme Self.

verses.sanskrit: विलक्षणं यथा ध्वान्तं लीयते भानुतेजसि तथैव सकलं दृश्यं ब्रह्मणि प्रविलीयते
verses.transliteration: vilakṣaṇaṃ yathā dhvāntaṃ līyate bhānutejasi & tathaiva sakalaṃ dṛśyaṃ brahmaṇi pravilīyate
verses.translation: As darkness, which is distinct (from sunshine), vanishes in the suns radiance, so the whole objective universe dissolves in Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: घटे नष्टे यथा व्योम व्योमैव भवति स्फुटम् तथैवोपाधिविलये ब्रह्मैव ब्रह्मवित्स्वयम्
verses.transliteration: ghaṭe naṣṭe yathā vyoma vyomaiva bhavati sphuṭam & tathaivopādhivilaye brahmaiva brahmavitsvayam
verses.translation: As, when a jar is broken, the space enclosed by it becomes palpably the limitless space, so when the apparent limitations are destroyed, the knower of Brahman verily becomes Brahman Itself.

verses.sanskrit: क्षीरं क्षीरे यथा क्षिप्तं तैलं तैले जलं जले संयुक्तमेकतां याति तथात्मन्य् आत्मविन्मुनिः
verses.transliteration: kṣīraṃ kṣīre yathā kṣiptaṃ tailaṃ taile jalaṃ jale & saṃyuktamekatāṃ yāti tathātmany ātmavinmuniḥ
verses.translation: As milk poured into milk, oil into oil, and water into water, becomes united and one with it, so the sage who has realised the Atman becomes one in the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: एवं विदेहकैवल्यं सन्मात्रत्वमखण्डितम् ब्रह्मभावं प्रपद्यैष यतिर्नावर्तते पुनः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ videhakaivalyaṃ sanmātratvamakhaṇḍitam & brahmabhāvaṃ prapadyaiṣa yatirnāvartate punaḥ
verses.translation: Realising thus the extreme isolation that comes of disembodiedness, and becoming eternally identified with the Absolute Reality, Brahman, the sage no longer suffers transmigration.

verses.sanskrit: सदात्मैकत्वविज्ञानदग्धाविद्यादिवर्ष्मणः अमुष्य ब्रह्मभूतत्वाद् ब्रह्मणः कुत उद्भवः
verses.transliteration: sadātmaikatvavijñānadagdhāvidyādivarṣmaṇaḥ & amuṣya brahmabhūtatvād brahmaṇaḥ kuta udbhavaḥ
verses.translation: For his bodies, consisting of Nescience etc., having been burnt by the realisation of the identity of the Jiva and Brahman, he becomes Brahman Itself; and how can Brahman ever have rebirth ?

verses.sanskrit: मायाकॢप्तौ बन्धमोक्षौ न स्तः स्वात्मनि वस्तुतः यथा रज्जौ निष्क्रियायां सर्पाभासविनिर्गमौ
verses.transliteration: māyākḷptau bandhamokṣau na staḥ svātmani vastutaḥ & yathā rajjau niṣkriyāyāṃ sarpābhāsavinirgamau
verses.translation: Bondage and Liberation, which are conjured up by Maya, do not really exist in the Atman, ones Reality, as the appearance and exit of the snake do not abide in the rope, which suffers no change.

verses.sanskrit: आवृतेः सदसत्त्वाभ्यां वक्तव्ये बन्धमोक्षणे नावृतिर्ब्रह्मणः काचिदन्याभावादनावृतम् \ यद्य् अस्त्य् अद्वैतहानिः स्याद् द्वैतं नो सहते श्रुतिः
verses.transliteration: āvṛteḥ sadasattvābhyāṃ vaktavye bandhamokṣaṇe & nāvṛtirbrahmaṇaḥ kācidanyābhāvādanāvṛtam \ yady asty advaitahāniḥ syād dvaitaṃ no sahate śrutiḥ
verses.translation: Bondage and Liberation may be talked of when there is the presence or absence of a covering veil. But there can be no covering veil for Brahman, which is always uncovered for want of a second thing besides Itself. If there be, the non-duality of Brahman will be contradicted, and the Shrutis can never brook duality.

verses.sanskrit: बन्धञ्च मोक्षञ्च मृषैव मूढा बुद्धेर्गुणं वस्तुनि कल्पयन्ति दृगावृतिं मेघकृतां यथा रवौ यतो ऽद्वयासङ्गचिदेतदक्षरम्
verses.transliteration: bandhañca mokṣañca mṛṣaiva mūḍhā buddherguṇaṃ vastuni kalpayanti & dṛgāvṛtiṃ meghakṛtāṃ yathā ravau yato 'dvayāsaṅgacidetadakṣaram
verses.translation: Bondage and Liberation are attributes of the Buddhi which ignorant people falsely superimpose on the Reality, as the covering of the eyes by a cloud is transferred to the sun. For this Immutable Brahman is Knowledge Absolute, the One without a second and unattached.

verses.sanskrit: अस्तीति प्रत्ययो यश्च यश्च नास्तीति वस्तुनि बुद्धेर् एव गुणावेतौ न तु नित्यस्य वस्तुनः
verses.transliteration: astīti pratyayo yaśca yaśca nāstīti vastuni & buddher eva guṇāvetau na tu nityasya vastunaḥ
verses.translation: The idea that bondage exists, and the idea that it does not, are, with reference to the Reality, both attributes of the Buddhi merely, and never belong to the Eternal Reality, Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अतस्तौ मायया कॢप्तौ बन्धमोक्षौ न चात्मनि निष्कले निष्क्रिये शान्ते निरवद्ये निरञ्जने \ अद्वितीये परे तत्त्वे व्योमवत्कल्पना कुतः
verses.transliteration: atastau māyayā kḷptau bandhamokṣau na cātmani & niṣkale niṣkriye śānte niravadye nirañjane \ advitīye pare tattve vyomavatkalpanā kutaḥ
verses.translation: Hence this bondage and Liberation are created by Maya, and are not in the Atman. How can there be any idea of limitation with regard to the Supreme Truth, which is without parts, without activity, calm, unimpeachable, taintless, and One without a second, as there can be none with regard to the infinite sky ?

verses.sanskrit: न निरोधो न चोत्पत्तिर्न बद्धो न च साधकः न मुमुक्षुर्न वै मुक्त इत्येषा परमार्थता
verses.transliteration: na nirodho na cotpattirna baddho na ca sādhakaḥ & na mumukṣurna vai mukta ityeṣā paramārthatā
verses.translation: There is neither death nor birth, neither a bound nor a struggling soul, neither a seeker after Liberation nor a liberated one this is the ultimate truth.

verses.sanskrit: सकलनिगमचूडास्वान्तसिद्धान्तरूपं परमिदमतिगुह्यं दर्शितं ते मयाद्य अपगतकलिदोषं कामनिर्मुक्तबुद्धिं स्वसुतवदसकृत्त्वां भाव्यित्वा मुमुक्षुम्
verses.transliteration: sakalanigamacūḍāsvāntasiddhāntarūpaṃ paramidamatiguhyaṃ darśitaṃ te mayādya & apagatakalidoṣaṃ kāmanirmuktabuddhiṃ svasutavadasakṛttvāṃ bhāvyitvā mumukṣum
verses.translation: I have today repeatedly revealed to thee, as to ones own son, this excellent and profound secret, which is the inmost purport of all Vedanta, the crest of the Vedas considering thee an aspirant after Liberation, purged of the taints of this Dark Age, and of a mind free from desires.

verses.sanskrit: इति श्रुत्वा गुरोर् वाक्यं प्रश्रयेण कृतानतिः स तेन समनुज्ञातो ययौ निर्मुक्तबन्धनः
verses.transliteration: iti śrutvā guror vākyaṃ praśrayeṇa kṛtānatiḥ & sa tena samanujñāto yayau nirmuktabandhanaḥ
verses.translation: Hearing these words of the Guru, the disciple out of reverence prostrated himself before him, and with his permission went his way, freed from bondage.

verses.sanskrit: गुरुर् एव सदानन्दसिन्धौ निर्मग्नमानसः पावयन्वसुधां सर्वाण् विचचार निरन्तरः
verses.transliteration: gurur eva sadānandasindhau nirmagnamānasaḥ & pāvayanvasudhāṃ sarvāṇ vicacāra nirantaraḥ
verses.translation: And the Guru, with his mind steeped in the ocean of Existence and Bliss Absolute, roamed, verily purifying the whole world all differentiating ideas banished from his mind.

verses.sanskrit: इत्याचार्यस्य शिष्यस्य संवादेनात्मलक्षणम् निरूपितं मुमुक्षूणां सुखबोधोपपत्तये
verses.transliteration: ityācāryasya śiṣyasya saṃvādenātmalakṣaṇam & nirūpitaṃ mumukṣūṇāṃ sukhabodhopapattaye
verses.translation: Thus by way of a dialogue between the Teacher and the disciple, has the nature of the Atman been ascertained for the easy comprehension of seekers after Liberation.

verses.sanskrit: हितमिदमुपदेशमाद्रियन्तां विहितनिरस्तसमस्तचित्तदोषाः भवसुखविरताः प्रशान्तचित्ताः श्रुतिरसिका यतयो मुमुक्षवो ये
verses.transliteration: hitamidamupadeśamādriyantāṃ vihitanirastasamastacittadoṣāḥ & bhavasukhaviratāḥ praśāntacittāḥ śrutirasikā yatayo mumukṣavo ye
verses.translation: May those Sannyasins who are seekers after Liberation, who have purged themselves of all taints of the mind by the observance of the prescribed methods, who are averse to worldly pleasures, and who are of serene minds, and take a delight in the Shruti appreciate this salutary teaching !

verses.sanskrit: संसाराध्वनि तापभानुकिरणप्रोद्भूतदाहव्यथा- खिन्नानां जलकाङ्क्षया मरुभुवि भ्रान्त्या परिभ्राम्यताम् अत्यासन्नसुधाम्बुधिं सुखकरं ब्रह्माद्वयं दर्शयत्य् एषा शंकरभारती विजयते निर्वाणसंदायिनी
verses.transliteration: saṃsārādhvani tāpabhānukiraṇaprodbhūtadāhavyathā- khinnānāṃ jalakāṅkṣayā marubhuvi bhrāntyā paribhrāmyatām & atyāsannasudhāmbudhiṃ sukhakaraṃ brahmādvayaṃ darśayaty eṣā śaṃkarabhāratī vijayate nirvāṇasaṃdāyinī
verses.translation: For those who are afflicted, in the way of the world, by the burning pain due to the (scorching) sunshine of threefold misery, and who through delusion wander about in a desert in search of water for them here is the triumphant message of Shankara pointing out, within easy reach, the soothing ocean of nectar, Brahman, the One without a second to lead them on to Liberation.
